.ddx/beads.jsonl:97:{"acceptance":"1. `TestInstall_InTreeWritesProjectTreeAndLinks` (cli/cmd/install_test.go:21) asserts `ddx install \u003cname\u003e` in in-tree mode writes to `\u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/plugins/\u003cname\u003e/` and creates project-tier links under `\u003cproject\u003e/.claude/skills/\u003cname\u003e` and `\u003cproject\u003e/.agents/skills/\u003cname\u003e`.\n2. `TestInstall_ConventionModeWritesXDGProjectTree` (cli/cmd/install_test.go:73) asserts `ddx install \u003cname\u003e` in convention mode writes to `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/\u003chost\u003e/\u003cowner\u003e/\u003crepo\u003e/plugins/\u003cname\u003e/` and creates project-tier links pointing into that XDG plugins path.\n3. `TestUpdate_RespectsConventionVsInTreeMode` (cli/cmd/update_test.go:247) asserts `ddx update` (no flag) updates the project install in whichever mode is active.\n4. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./cmd/... green.\n5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"8fbb32ad36b842cefed25f048cd5511a8b64afe9","created_at":"2026-06-03T11:21:13.13129759Z","description":"PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a, slice 3 of 4)\n\nWithout `--global`, install and update must target the project's ddx root, which differs by mode: in-tree (`\u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/plugins/\u003cname\u003e`) versus convention (`${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/\u003chost\u003e/\u003cowner\u003e/\u003crepo\u003e/plugins/\u003cname\u003e`). Either way the project-tier agent skill links (`\u003cproject\u003e/.claude/skills` and `\u003cproject\u003e/.agents/skills`) must point into whichever plugins tree is active.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- `resolveInstallDirs(global=false)` (cli/cmd/install.go:184, project branch install.go:195-197) sets `installRoot = ddxroot.Path(ctx, f.WorkingDir)` and skill dirs to `\u003cworkingDir\u003e/.agents/skills` and `\u003cworkingDir\u003e/.claude/skills`. `ddxroot.Path` (cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:75) returns the in-tree `.ddx` path when `\u003cproject\u003e/.ddx` exists and the convention `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/...` path otherwise, so mode selection is implicit.\n- `installLocal` non-global branch derives `ddxRootDir := ddxroot.Path(...)` (install.go:453) for the same routing.\n- Update: the non-global path `performUpdate` (cli/cmd/update.go:204) uses `ddxroot.JoinProject(workingDir, ...)` (e.g. update.go:227) so it updates the project install in whichever mode is active; mode is not an explicit flag but follows `ddxroot`.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nEnsure `ddx install \u003cname\u003e` (no `--global`) in in-tree mode writes to `\u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/plugins/\u003cname\u003e/` and links `\u003cproject\u003e/.claude/skills/\u003cname\u003e` and `\u003cproject\u003e/.agents/skills/\u003cname\u003e`; in convention mode (no `\u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/`) writes to the XDG project plugins path and links the project-tier skill dirs into that path. Ensure `ddx update` (no flag) updates the project install in whichever mode is active.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- The `--global` install/update surface (slice 2).\n- `ddx init` behavior and doctor reporting (slice 4).\n- Removing either mode — in-tree and convention coexist.\n\nDEPS\n- Slice 1 (plugin lookup precedence) and ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — project paths route through `ddxroot.Path()`.","events_attachment":"ddx-2b9c26d7/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-2b9c26d7","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:plugin","area:cli","kind:feature","phase:3","initiative:zero-footprint","principle:skills-personas","parent:ddx-602ae31a","dep:ddx-06cbaa90"],"parent":"ddx-937de9fc","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-c7bae1d6","status":"closed","title":"project install surface: ddx install (no flag) and ddx update honor in-tree vs convention mode","updated_at":"2026-06-03T12:16:40.973821743Z","work-no-changes-count":1}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:113:{"acceptance":"1. Add Niflheim-evidence regression fixtures or table-driven cases under `cli/internal/agent/...` that encode the four observed scenario families from this bead: empty review success, land/ref/index/evidence-staging failure, decomposed-parent no-changes rationale with child beads, and repeated pre-claim system-unready warning. The fixtures must be self-contained and must not reference Niflheim paths as required inputs.\n2. `TestExecuteBeadWorker_NiflheimEvidence_EmptyReviewResultCannotClose` proves a non-skipped success path with `cycle_trace.review_result={}` or an equivalent empty `ReviewResult` cannot close a bead and records `review_error` or operator-attention with a non-empty reason.\n3. `TestExecuteBeadWorker_NiflheimEvidence_NilReviewerRequiresExplicitSkip` updates the current nil-reviewer behavior so `Reviewer: nil` only closes when an explicit durable no-review/skip reason is present; otherwise it fails review gating.\n4. `TestExecuteBeadLoop_NiflheimEvidence_LandCoordinationFailureDoesNotRerunImplementation` simulates both observed details (`cannot lock ref refs/heads/main` after a candidate/merge is already landed, and `landing worktree has staged changes after waiting 2s`) and asserts the action is reconcile/retry-land/operator-attention, not a fresh implementation run and not model escalation.\n5. `TestExecuteBeadLoop_NiflheimEvidence_DecomposedParentIsExecutionIneligible` simulates `mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale` with `orchestrator_action: decompose`, child bead IDs, and `outcome_reason=decomposed`; it asserts the parent is marked execution-ineligible/proposed or dependency-waiting according to policy and that no further implementation attempt or min-power escalation is scheduled.\n6. `TestWorkRetryEscalation_NiflheimEvidence_OnlySemanticFailuresRaiseMinPower` proves only semantic acceptance/review failures can increase requested min power/profile. Land coordination, repeated pre-claim system warnings, decomposed-parent outcomes, and dirty generated DDx evidence must not raise min power.\n7. `TestExecuteBeadResult_NiflheimEvidence_CycleTraceCarriesDecisionAudit` proves result JSON/event bodies include `failure_class`, `retry_action`, `escalation_count`, requested/actual routing facts, review status/verdict or explicit skip reason, and land/reconcile status for the Niflheim scenario families.\n8. Existing related behavior from `ddx-2965925e`, `ddx-b2846610`, and `ddx-d67f124e` remains covered; this bead either reuses their helpers or waits for those dependencies, and does not add duplicate low-level implementations.\n9. `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run 'NiflheimEvidence|EmptyReviewResult|NilReviewer|LandCoordination|DecomposedParent|RetryEscalation|CycleTraceCarriesDecisionAudit' -count=1` passes.\n10. `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -count=1` passes.\n11. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","created_at":"2026-06-06T15:55:41.332809555Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-334c0958","depends_on_id":"ddx-b2846610","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-06-06T15:55:52Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-334c0958","depends_on_id":"ddx-2965925e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-06-06T15:55:53Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-334c0958","depends_on_id":"ddx-d67f124e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-06-06T15:55:55Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM\nA live Niflheim worker audit on 2026-06-06 showed that `ddx work` is not yet a stable release-grade autonomous worker. Strong-model escalation exists, but it is not reliably tied to the class of failure. The worker spent repeated attempts on structural or system failures that stronger models cannot fix, while automatic review evidence was empty on attempts that were recorded as successful. This violates the DDx engineering principle \"Cheapest Viable, Escalate on Evidence\" (docs/dev/engineering-principles.md:39-45): review, runtime, or retry evidence should determine escalation, and only lower-power inability to meet acceptance criteria should push to stronger routes. It also threatens FEAT-010 task-execution reliability because beads can be retried, escalated, or closed without a typed, auditable reason.\n\nThis bead is a Niflheim incident/regression closure bead. It is not a duplicate of the narrower queue items listed under INTERSECTIONS; instead it captures the external evidence DDx workers will not otherwise have, and requires a DDx-side regression suite proving those observed scenarios classify correctly before rerun, escalation, operator attention, or close.\n\nBACKGROUND AND OBSERVED NIFLHEIM EVIDENCE\nThe evidence was gathered from Niflheim worker logs and result bundles, but those files are not load-bearing for this bead. The relevant facts are copied here because an isolated DDx worker cannot access the Niflheim repository or `/tmp` logs.\n\n1. Decomposed parent kept consuming attempts and escalated to a stronger model anyway.\n- Niflheim bead: `niflheim-64217cc5`, title \"Instrument cold replicator with structured per-operation S3 tracing\".\n- Observed result bundle: `.ddx/executions/20260606T152006-a4ca7254/result.json` plus worker logs from 2026-06-05/2026-06-06.\n- Routing showed escalation exists: repeated routes started at `gpt-5.4-mini` with requested min powers 5, 7, 8, and 9, then reached `gpt-5.5` at requested min power 10.\n- Metrics on the bead showed `attempt_count=24`, `total_tokens=19371353`, and average duration about `244112ms`.\n- Despite triage recommending `close_decomposed_or_mark_execution_ineligible`, retries continued until the latest event recorded:\n  `mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale`\n  `powerClass=standard`\n  `rationale: status: open`\n  `orchestrator_action: decompose`\n  `reason: parent bead exceeds the Step 0 size threshold and is already decomposed into focused child beads for tracing, counters, tests, and runbook work.`\n  `child_beads: niflheim-f03c61a2, niflheim-df407aa8, niflheim-7a421ede, niflheim-0bbed6c9`\n  `outcome_reason=decomposed`\n- Expected DDx behavior: once the worker has reliable evidence that the bead is a decomposed parent or execution-ineligible parent, no further implementation attempt or model escalation should occur. It should mark execution-ineligible/proposed/operator-attention according to policy and move on.\n\n2. Land/evidence coordination failures were treated like implementation failure and retried expensively.\n- Niflheim bead: `niflheim-c75aba30`, title \"Materializer per-merge memory footprint OOMs even at concurrent_limit=8\".\n- Two expensive attempts both used `gpt-5.4-mini`; no useful stronger-model escalation was observed.\n- Metrics showed `attempt_count=2`, `total_tokens=65513510`, and average duration about `3306461.5ms`.\n- First attempt result: `.ddx/executions/20260606T113550-7b29ee42/result.json`.\n  - `status=execution_failed`, `outcome=error`.\n  - Implementation result commit was `7aaeb0712...`; a merge commit `2c6f4d6e6` was later observed as an ancestor of HEAD, but the result still recorded a land coordination failure.\n  - Error detail:\n    `land coordination failed: land advancer: restoring refs/heads/main to ba2d3059f14e834dcb147f2a6db3b294d83b0fac after evidence commit failed: git update-ref refs/heads/main: fatal: update_ref failed for ref 'refs/heads/main': cannot lock ref 'refs/heads/main': is at 5a176b332b182f2a959614f63769d835c47ffd97 but expected 2c6f4d6e6961b8a93742cde66bceec056934655a`\n  - `cycle_trace[0].final_decision=\"success\"` and `cycle_trace[0].review_result={}`.\n- Second attempt result: `.ddx/executions/20260606T142223-94f8f0f8/result.json`.\n  - `status=execution_failed`, `outcome=error`.\n  - Error detail:\n    `land coordination failed: land advancer: landing worktree has staged changes after waiting 2s: A .ddx/executions/20260606T150832-af40450b/result.json`\n  - `cycle_trace[0].final_decision=\"success\"` and `cycle_trace[0].review_result={}`.\n- At observation time the worker had claimed it again (`20260606T152024-a21d8c54`) and edited six files in a fresh isolated worktree:\n  `crates/niflheim-core/src/config/mod.rs`, `crates/niflheim-core/src/config/tests.rs`, `crates/niflheim-core/src/config/types.rs`, `crates/niflheim-delta-writer/src/materializer/config.rs`, `crates/niflheim-delta-writer/src/materializer/executor.rs`, and `crates/niflheim-delta-writer/src/materializer/tests.rs`.\n- Expected DDx behavior: land/ref/index/evidence-staging failures are system or coordination failures, not model-quality failures. The retry policy should reconcile a successfully landed candidate when possible, retry only the land/evidence step when safe, or park/operator-attention the bead when the landing worktree is dirty. It should not blindly rerun implementation at the same or stronger route.\n\n3. Pre-claim intake/system dirt caused repeated warning churn instead of a clear operator lane.\n- Niflheim bead: `niflheim-a62d31ce`, title \"Publish competitive benchmark artifacts and provenance metadata\".\n- A live worker repeatedly ran readiness/intake. The log showed readiness timeout after five minutes, then:\n  `preserved pre-dispatch implementation changes in /home/erik/Projects/niflheim under refs/ddx/pre-dispatch/20260606T153517-f7a32618; released niflheim-a62d31ce and continuing watch. dirty paths: .ddx/harness-sessions/-2929681.json. recover with git stash apply refs/ddx/pre-dispatch/20260606T153517-f7a32618`\n- Bead events included `pre_claim_intake.warn` with timeout/system-unready reason and generic suggested action.\n- Expected DDx behavior: repeated same/systemic pre-claim warnings and dirty generated DDx artifacts should become an operator-visible/system-unready state after the configured threshold, not an indefinite poll/release loop.\n\n4. Automatic reviews were not forcing meaningful quality improvement.\n- Recent Niflheim \"success\" attempts had empty review results:\n  - `niflheim-9dea95da`, result `.ddx/executions/20260606T043655-845c77ee/result.json`: closed success, `cycle_trace.review_result={}`, `final_decision=\"success\"`, model `gpt-5.4-mini`, result commit `78e51a168...`.\n  - `niflheim-da4e1311`, result `.ddx/executions/20260606T085358-d80ada3e/result.json`: closed success, `cycle_trace.review_result={}`, `final_decision=\"success\"`, model `gpt-5.4-mini`, result commit `d43be3a3...`.\n  - `niflheim-c75aba30` attempts listed above: `cycle_trace.final_decision=\"success\"` with `review_result={}` despite overall land failures.\n- Manual Claude review earlier improved bead wording and completeness, but the automatic worker path did not show enforceable per-AC review evidence.\n- Expected DDx behavior: unless review is explicitly skipped with a durable reason (`runtime.NoReview` or a valid `review:skip` + reason policy), an empty review result must be a review error or operator-attention signal. It must not be treated as approval, success, or evidence of quality improvement.\n\nROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE\n- `cli/internal/agent/failclass/failclass.go:103-142` contains the normative routing table: only `OutcomeGenuineFailure` can escalate; dirty worktree, transport, quota, auth, setup, timeout, and merge-conflict do not escalate. The live Niflheim behavior shows execute-bead/work outcomes are not consistently reduced to this typed table before retry/escalation decisions.\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:305-309` wraps `LandingAdvancer` errors as generic `land advancer` errors. The caller can record an overall `execution_failed` without distinguishing landed-candidate reconciliation, ref/index contention, staged evidence dirt, or dirty landing worktree from implementation failure.\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1967-2072` emits pre-claim timeout/system-unready outcomes as warn-only by default and continues implementation when strict intake blocking is off. This permits repeated same-fingerprint/system warnings to look like a live worker instead of a system-unready/operator lane.\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2175-2315` handles `PreClaimIntakeTooLargeDecomposed`, but missing/empty decomposition paths can warn and continue. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3826-3831` can recognize execution-ineligible/decomposed state only after the bead has the right metadata. The Niflheim parent evidence shows structural/decomposed-parent outcomes can still reach repeated implementation attempts and power escalation.\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3042-3138` treats success disposition/status as closeable once candidate-cycle review/landing has allowed success, and the old post-merge review is optional. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_test.go:799-823` explicitly proves a worker with `Reviewer: nil` can skip review and close the bead. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:168-193` defines `ReviewResult` with all fields optional except the zero-value enum field; no invariant rejects `{}` as a success-path review artifact.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n- Add compact Niflheim-style regression fixtures under `cli/internal/agent/testdata/niflheim_worker_evidence/` or equivalent. The fixtures should encode the four scenario families above: empty review success, land coordination/ref-lock/staged-evidence failure, decomposed-parent no-changes rationale with child beads, and repeated pre-claim system-unready warning. Do not depend on Niflheim checkout paths.\n- Introduce or tighten a single execute-bead retry classification boundary that maps attempt result, land result, review result, no-changes rationale, and pre-claim intake outcome into exactly one action: close, retry same tier, escalate min power/profile, reconcile land/evidence, execution-ineligible/proposed, or operator-attention. Wire this boundary into `ddx work`/`ddx try` so `failclass.Classify` is not merely a standalone helper.\n- Enforce review evidence on success. For non-skipped success paths, `cycle_trace.review_result` or the equivalent result field must contain a non-empty verdict, per-AC evidence or documented reason, reviewer routing/session metadata when available, and a classification. `{}` is invalid and must prevent close or produce a review-error/operator-attention event. Explicit review skips remain allowed only when they are durable, reasoned, and visible in result JSON.\n- Classify land/ref/index/evidence-staging failures separately from implementation/model failures. If the candidate commit or merge is already landed or is an ancestor of the target, reconcile and close/continue without rerunning implementation. If the landing worktree has staged generated evidence or DDx metadata, retry/preserve the land/evidence step or move to operator attention according to the existing local reliability policy; do not escalate the model for this class.\n- Classify decomposed-parent/no-changes rationale as execution-ineligible or operator work before another implementation attempt. Stronger models cannot fix parent beads that are already split into executable children.\n- Make worker result JSON and event bodies carry enough audit fields to explain the decision: `failure_class`, `retry_action`, `escalation_count`, requested/actual power/profile/model, review status/verdict, and land/reconcile status.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Do not change Fizeau route selection or hard-code concrete provider/model names. DDx should continue to pass power/profile intent and let Fizeau choose concrete routes.\n- Do not edit Niflheim beads or Niflheim implementation code.\n- Do not duplicate the low-level index-contention fix tracked by `ddx-2965925e`; reuse or align with it.\n- Do not duplicate the post-commit review/land timeout work tracked by `ddx-b2846610`; this bead requires typed classification and review evidence, not another timeout implementation.\n- Do not duplicate the repeated pre-claim warn operator-attention bead `ddx-d67f124e`; this bead uses the Niflheim observation as cross-scenario regression coverage.\n- Do not implement a full operator-lane product workflow beyond the state transitions/events needed to keep the worker from blind retry or false close.\n\nINTERSECTIONS\n- Parent: `ddx-3106674f` (multi-worker hardening and local reliability gates). This bead is the external Niflheim regression/incident closure layer under that reliability epic.\n- Dependency: `ddx-2965925e` covers tracker commit index contention. This bead must not reimplement that fix; it must prove land/ref/index/evidence failures classify separately from model failure once the low-level path is hardened.\n- Dependency: `ddx-b2846610` covers bounded post-commit review/land. This bead must still require typed review/land outcomes after the timeout wedge is fixed.\n- Dependency: `ddx-d67f124e` covers same-fingerprint pre-claim warning escalation. This bead adds the Niflheim `niflheim-a62d31ce` family as regression evidence.\n- Related but not a dependency: `ddx-e1a576a7` is an execution-ineligible decomposed parent and a local precedent for the `niflheim-64217cc5` class.\n- Related quality framework: `ddx-a9d130d0` and `ddx-ac10e7af` own broader bead lifecycle/operator-lane policy; this bead is concrete worker behavior and evidence gating.","id":"ddx-334c0958","issue_type":"bug","labels":["phase:iterate","area:agent","area:execute-bead","area:workers","area:review","kind:bug","kind:reliability","severity:critical","spec:FEAT-010","spec:TP-021","adr:024","incident:niflheim-2026-06-06","prevention"],"parent":"ddx-3106674f","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"spec-id":"FEAT-010","status":"open","title":"execute-bead worker: gate success on review evidence and classify Niflheim retry failures","updated_at":"2026-06-06T15:55:55.96445459Z"}

The orchestrator already detects the no-progress state but its only remediation is 'retry parent', which re-enters the same loop.\n\nThere is also a 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning emitted by the readiness check during this state; that's a downstream symptom of the same problem, not the cause.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nImplement only this child acceptance slice:\n- Programmatic decomposition path (the action that fans a too-large bead into children) MUST NOT add the parent as a dep. TestInitProject_DoesNotInstallPlugins asserts `ddx init` (no flag, in-project) creates \u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/ and its manifest but does not write under plugins/ — the plugin lazy-resolves from the global tier.\n3. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./cmd/... green.\n4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"4664c3d9dbff6177e63eb5dde486415159e2413f","created_at":"2026-06-03T05:42:23.055248099Z","description":"PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)\n\nThere is no first-time machine setup command, and `ddx init` bundles plugin install with manifest creation, forcing a plugin write into every newly initialized project instead of lazy-resolving from the global tier.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- cli/cmd/init.go bundles plugin install with manifest creation; there is no `--global` umbrella path that installs the default `ddx` plugin globally and writes global config.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n1. `ddx init --global` is the umbrella first-time-setup command: installs the default `ddx` plugin globally to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx (via GlobalPluginDir), creates agent-tier links (~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx pointing into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/), and writes ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml with convention defaults.\n2. `ddx init` (no flag) creates \u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/ and its manifest but does NOT install plugins; plugins are lazy-resolved on first use, preferring the project tier over global per the registry precedence bead.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Install command `--global` flag (sibling install bead).\n- Update command and doctor reporting (sibling update bead).\n- The runtime precedence implementation (registry bead).\n- Removing the existing per-project install (both modes coexist).\n- Migrating legacy ~/.config/ddx paths.\n- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.\n\nDEPS\n- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution).\n- Registry resolution sibling bead (GlobalPluginDir) — init --global writes through it.\n\nGOVERNING ARTIFACTS\n- Epic ddx-602ae31a, Principle 2.","events_attachment":"ddx-3ecfd11d/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-3ecfd11d","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:config","area:cli","initiative:zero-footprint","principle:skills-personas","kind:feature","phase:3"],"parent":"ddx-937de9fc","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-b321fc9b","status":"closed","title":"init: add `ddx init --global` umbrella setup and make plain `ddx init` skip plugin install","updated_at":"2026-06-03T06:52:49.876373361Z"}

AC1 requires these methods to take `ctx context.Context` as a typed first parameter (matching the BeadEventReader / BeadQueries shape already in cli/internal/bead/backend.go:91-114). Any concrete signature satisfying AC1 — `Init(ctx context.Context) error`, `Init(ctx context.Context, args ...any) error`, etc. — rejects the current zero-arg `s.Init()` callsites that exist in external packages. 3. The bead's PROPOSED FIX and NON-SCOPE explicitly forbid touching external callers (\"Do not touch the external caller sweep in this bead\"; \"NON-SCOPE: Caller migration in cli/cmd, cli/internal/agent, cli/internal/server, cli/internal/escalation, and metrics packages.\") and AC4 requires \"no caller-package changes in this bead.\" 4. AC3 requires `lefthook run pre-commit` to pass, which runs `go build -buildvcs=false -v ./...` from cli/ (lefthook.yml:122-129). That build covers every package in the module, including all the external caller packages NON-SCOPE forbids touching. 5. Concrete external breakage if AC1 is implemented today (`grep -rE '\\b(store|s)\\.(Init|Create|Update|Get|Close|ReadAll|GenID|GetWithArchive|ReadAllFiltered)\\(' cli/ --include='*.go'`): - 174 Go files reference these Store methods. - 73 distinct files outside cli/internal/bead/ use the zero-arg `store.Init()` form (e.g. cli/cmd/ac_test.go:37, cli/cmd/bead_human_test.go:20, cli/internal/agent/..., cli/internal/server/...). - Production-code stragglers without ctx exist too: cli/cmd/bead.go:414  s.GetWithArchive(args[0]) cli/cmd/bead.go:1281 s.Close(id) cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4485 store.Get(id) cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4811 store.Update(beadID, ...) `go build ./...` and the lefthook go-build hook will fail at every one of those callsites the instant Init/Get/Update/Close/ReadAll/GenID/ GetWithArchive/ReadAllFiltered become non-variadic ctx-first. There is no signature shape that satisfies AC1 while preserving all current call patterns (Go does not allow same-name method overloads on a single receiver type). 6. Step 0 size envelope is also exceeded for the only resolution that preserves AC1+AC3: the necessary mechanical caller sweep crosses ~170 files in five packages (cmd, internal/agent, internal/server, internal/attemptmetrics, internal/agentmetrics) — well past the \"~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages\" guardrail. That is exactly the sweep that NON-SCOPE excludes, and the parent split (TD-027 / ddx-464feba2) was rejected for the same reason. Operator action requested: Re-scope this bead to one of: (a) Add the caller-package migration to scope (delete the NON-SCOPE block and AC4's \"no caller-package changes\"). This becomes the original TD-027 sweep that previously failed as too large; if pursued, expect a multi-file diff across cli/cmd, cli/internal/agent, cli/internal/server, cli/internal/attemptmetrics, cli/internal/agentmetrics. (b) Replace AC3 with a narrower verification (e.g. \"go test ./internal/bead/... and go vet ./internal/bead/... pass\"), and explicitly accept that `go build ./...` and lefthook pre-commit will be red until follow-up caller-migration beads land. The follow-up beads would then re-establish AC3 once the sweep is complete. (c) Replace AC1 with an additive shape that does not rename or retype the existing variadic methods — e.g. \"add new ctx-first Store methods with distinct names (InitCtx / GetCtx / …) so the legacy variadic surface stays in place during the migration\", then a follow-up bead deletes the variadic methods after the caller sweep finishes. This satisfies the spirit of TD-027 (introducing the typed ctx-first surface in the bead package first) without breaking the build outside the package. No code changes were made on this attempt. AC1 requires these methods to take `ctx context.Context` as a typed first parameter (matching the BeadEventReader / BeadQueries shape already in cli/internal/bead/backend.go:91-114). Any concrete signature satisfying AC1 — `Init(ctx context.Context) error`, `Init(ctx context.Context, args ...any) error`, etc. — rejects the current zero-arg `s.Init()` callsites that exist in external packages. 3. The bead's PROPOSED FIX and NON-SCOPE explicitly forbid touching external callers (\"Do not touch the external caller sweep in this bead\"; \"NON-SCOPE: Caller migration in cli/cmd, cli/internal/agent, cli/internal/server, cli/internal/escalation, and metrics packages.\") and AC4 requires \"no caller-package changes in this bead.\" 4. Concrete external breakage if AC1 is implemented today (`grep -rE '\\b(store|s)\\.(Init|Create|Update|Get|Close|ReadAll|GenID|GetWithArchive|ReadAllFiltered)\\(' cli/ --include='*.go'`): - 174 Go files reference these Store methods. - 73 distinct files outside cli/internal/bead/ use the zero-arg `store.Init()` form (e.g. cli/cmd/ac_test.go:37, cli/cmd/bead_human_test.go:20, cli/internal/agent/..., cli/internal/server/...). - Production-code stragglers without ctx exist too: cli/cmd/bead.go:414  s.GetWithArchive(args[0]) cli/cmd/bead.go:1281 s.Close(id) cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4485 store.Get(id) cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4811 store.Update(beadID, ...) `go build ./...` and the lefthook go-build hook will fail at every one of those callsites the instant Init/Get/Update/Close/ReadAll/GenID/ GetWithArchive/ReadAllFiltered become non-variadic ctx-first. There is no signature shape that satisfies AC1 while preserving all current call patterns (Go does not allow same-name method overloads on a single receiver type). 6. Step 0 size envelope is also exceeded for the only resolution that preserves AC1+AC3: the necessary mechanical caller sweep crosses ~170 files in five packages (cmd, internal/agent, internal/server, internal/attemptmetrics, internal/agentmetrics) — well past the \"~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages\" guardrail. That is exactly the sweep that NON-SCOPE excludes, and the parent split (TD-027 / ddx-464feba2) was rejected for the same reason. Operator action requested: Re-scope this bead to one of: (a) Add the caller-package migration to scope (delete the NON-SCOPE block and AC4's \"no caller-package changes\"). This becomes the original TD-027 sweep that previously failed as too large; if pursued, expect a multi-file diff across cli/cmd, cli/internal/agent, cli/internal/server, cli/internal/attemptmetrics, cli/internal/agentmetrics. (b) Replace AC3 with a narrower verification (e.g. \"go test ./internal/bead/... and go vet ./internal/bead/... pass\"), and explicitly accept that `go build ./...` and lefthook pre-commit will be red until follow-up caller-migration beads land. The follow-up beads would then re-establish AC3 once the sweep is complete. (c) Replace AC1 with an additive shape that does not rename or retype the existing variadic methods — e.g. \"add new ctx-first Store methods with distinct names (InitCtx / GetCtx / …) so the legacy variadic surface stays in place during the migration\", then a follow-up bead deletes the variadic methods after the caller sweep finishes. .ddx/beads.jsonl:147:{"acceptance":"1. `TestInitProject_DoesNotInstallPlugins` verifies that `ddx init` with no flag creates `\u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/` and the manifest but does not write `.ddx/plugins/ddx`, `.agents/skills/ddx`, `.claude/skills/ddx`, or any project-local `plugins/` tree.\n2. `TestInitProject_LeavesPluginsForLazyResolution` verifies the initialized project can lazy-resolve the default `ddx` plugin from the global layer through `registry.ResolvePlugin` when the sibling resolver behavior is present.\n3. `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./cmd/... -run 'TestInitProject_DoesNotInstallPlugins|TestInitProject_LeavesPluginsForLazyResolution'` passes.\n4. `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./cmd/...` passes.\n5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","closing_commit_sha":"3b82c8ae41c24296760ec4c7b1bd41c7bb964c8b","created_at":"2026-06-02T03:19:04.472608585Z","description":"PROBLEM\nPlain `ddx init` eagerly installs the default `ddx` plugin into every project. .ddx/beads.jsonl:163:{"acceptance":"1. TestInitGlobal_CreatesAgentTierLinks asserts ~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx point into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/.\n2. TestInitProject_DoesNotInstallPlugins asserts `ddx init` (in-project) does not write to plugins/ — plugins lazy-resolve from global.\n3. A Test* in cli/cmd/doctor_test.go asserts doctor renders both the \"Global Install (...)\" and \"Project Install (...)\" lines with the correct ok|missing|lazy-resolves-to-global statuses (doctor_test.go:273,276).\n4. A test asserts the ddx-validate pre-commit hook runs skill validation only when skill files are staged, independent of install location.\n5. The SKILL.md \"How this skill works\" install-locations note is updated in this bead's commit.\n6. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./cmd/... green.\n7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"bd11f3c9adebedc32b3639a97362f4129071bab6","created_at":"2026-06-03T01:02:41.360272672Z","description":"PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)\n\nFirst-time machine setup needs an umbrella `ddx init --global` that installs the default ddx plugin globally and wires agent-tier links, while plain `ddx init` must create only the project workspace and lazy-resolve plugins (preferring project over global) rather than eagerly installing. `ddx doctor` must surface both install layers so operators can see where the skill resolves from. The ddx-validate pre-commit hook (ddx-777f4a09, already landed) must keep scoping skill checks to staged skill files regardless of install location.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- cli/cmd/init.go:75 dispatches to initGlobal (init.go:318) when --global is set; initGlobal installs the bundled default ddx plugin into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx (init.go:320), creates symlinks ~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx into that dir (init.go:336), and writes ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml with convention defaults (init.go:351, writeGlobalConfig init.go:362).\n- Plain `ddx init` (no flag) must create \u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/ and NOT write plugins/ — plugins lazy-resolve via the registry resolver, preferring the project copy over global.\n- cli/cmd/doctor.go:421-448 computes globalPath/projectPath and the projectStatus \"lazy-resolves-to-global\" (doctor.go:436-444) and prints the \"Global Install (...) — \u003cstatus\u003e\" and \"Project Install (...) — \u003cstatus\u003e\" lines (doctor.go:447-448).\n- Before this, `ddx init` bundled plugin install with manifest creation, there was no global setup command, and doctor reported only a single install layer.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n1. `ddx init --global` installs the default ddx plugin globally, creates ~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx links into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx, and writes the global config with convention defaults.\n2. `ddx init` (no flag) creates \u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/ and does NOT install plugins; first use lazy-resolves from the project layer, falling back to global.\n3. `ddx doctor` renders two lines: \"Global Install (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx) — ok|missing\" and \"Project Install (\u003cprojectRoot\u003e/plugins/ddx) — ok|missing|lazy-resolves-to-global\".\n4. The ddx-validate pre-commit hook continues to run skill-related validation only when skill files are staged, regardless of whether the install is global or project-scoped.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Does NOT remove the per-project install path (both layers coexist).\n- Does NOT migrate legacy ~/.config/ddx paths.\n- Does NOT add agent-harness skill paths beyond Claude Code and Codex.\n- Plugin content writing for non-init installs is owned by the install child beads.\n\nDEPS\nddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — global setup routes through ddxroot.GlobalDir(), project init through ddxroot.Path(). Governing epic: ddx-602ae31a (Principle 2). Related: ddx-777f4a09 (ddx-validate hook, landed).","events_attachment":"ddx-4c58ea3e/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-4c58ea3e","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:cli","area:config","area:plugin","kind:feature","phase:3","initiative:zero-footprint","principle:skills-personas"],"parent":"ddx-937de9fc","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-04beccf3","status":"closed","title":"init/doctor: --global umbrella setup, lazy project init, and two-layer doctor reporting","updated_at":"2026-06-03T02:46:46.787576605Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:180:{"acceptance":"1. `ddx bead doctor --json` reports `parent_ancestor_in_deps` findings with stable fields for bead id, offending dependency id, offending ancestor id, and parent chain. Test: TestBeadDoctorDetectsParentAncestorBackEdge.\n\n2. `ddx bead doctor --fix` removes only dependency edges that point to the bead's parent or ancestor, leaves sibling and external deps intact, writes stable output, and is idempotent on a second run. Test: TestBeadDoctorRepairsParentAncestorBackEdge.\n\n3. A fixture representing an existing valid tracker graph passes `ddx bead doctor` without `--fix` and without regression findings. Test: TestBeadDoctorValidGraphSnapshotClean.\n\n4. Verification gate passes: `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./cmd/... ./internal/bead/... -run 'TestBeadDoctorDetectsParentAncestorBackEdge|TestBeadDoctorRepairsParentAncestorBackEdge|TestBeadDoctorValidGraphSnapshotClean' -count=1`.\n\n5. Repository gate passes: `lefthook run pre-commit`.","closing_commit_sha":"fbf13a409d8c9d3ae68e9210f832ff898990340d","created_at":"2026-06-06T03:26:10.230579648Z","description":"PROBLEM: Existing trackers can already contain `child -\u003e parent` or `child -\u003e ancestor` dependency edges. Without a diagnostic and repair path, those trackers remain wedged even after future writers stop producing bad edges.\n\nROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE: `ddx bead doctor` is wired in `cli/cmd/bead_doctor.go:31` and delegates normal scan/fix to `bead.BeadDoctor` / `bead.BeadDoctorFix` at `cli/cmd/bead_doctor.go:110`, but `cli/internal/bead/doctor.go:34` only scans oversized fields. It never builds a parent graph or reports dependency edges that intersect the parent chain.\n\nPROPOSED FIX: Extend bead doctor with a `parent_ancestor_in_deps` check that scans all bead rows, walks each bead's parent chain, and reports any dependency target that equals the direct parent or any ancestor. JSON output must be stable and scriptable. `--fix` must remove only offending dependency edges, preserve unrelated deps and events, and remain idempotent. Keep existing oversized-field and dangling-success behavior intact.\n\nNON-SCOPE: Do not modify decomposition prompts. Do not change `ddx work` retry behavior. Do not manually edit tracker storage outside the doctor implementation. Do not remove valid `parent -\u003e child` dependencies.\n\nGOVERNING ARTIFACTS: `docs/triage/decomposition.md:75` makes AC mapping load-bearing; this diagnostic preserves existing child work without lossy tracker rewrites.\n\nINHERITED DEPENDENCIES: none specified by the parent bead.","events_attachment":"ddx-5255a25c/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-5255a25c","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:tracker","area:lifecycle","kind:safety","kind:correctness","ac-quality:needs-refinement"],"parent":"ddx-e19185aa","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-5cd7eadf","status":"closed","title":"Detect and repair parent ancestor deps in bead doctor","updated_at":"2026-06-07T04:12:26.084120204Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:182:{"acceptance":"1. TestPluginLookup_PrefersProjectOverGlobal (cli/internal/registry/resolve_test.go): with the plugin dir present under BOTH `\u003cprojectRoot\u003e/.ddx/plugins/\u003cname\u003e` and `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/\u003cname\u003e`, `ResolvePlugin` returns layer==\"project\" and the project path.\n2. TestPluginLookup_FallsBackToGlobal (cli/internal/registry/resolve_test.go): with the project copy absent and only the global copy present, `ResolvePlugin` returns layer==\"global\" and the path returned by `GlobalPluginDir(name)`.\n3. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/registry/... green.\n4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"ecb015c2fb2effc5bff40afd42cf058b2606c65e","created_at":"2026-06-03T07:17:39.015088022Z","description":"PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)\nRuntime plugin lookup must follow a single authoritative precedence — install-in-project (project tier) overrides install-to-agent (global tier), with the embedded default ddx plugin as the last-resort baked-in fallback. Without one resolver, install/update/doctor each reimplement path logic and drift, so a project-local plugin can be silently shadowed by a global copy (or vice-versa).\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- cli/internal/registry/resolve.go:29 `ResolvePlugin(ctx, projectRoot, name)` is the intended single entry point. It checks the project path `ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot)/plugins/\u003cname\u003e` (resolve.go:30), then the global path `ddxroot.GlobalDir()/plugins/\u003cname\u003e` (resolve.go:35), then returns layer `\"baked-in\"` with an empty path only for name==\"ddx\" (resolve.go:41), else errors (resolve.go:44).\n- cli/internal/registry/resolve.go:15 `GlobalPluginDir(name)` defines the global-tier path `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/\u003cname\u003e`.\n- The precedence contract is currently under-tested, so regressions in ordering would go undetected.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n- Guarantee `ResolvePlugin` returns layer `\"project\"` and the project path when the plugin dir exists in BOTH the project and global tiers (project wins).\n- Guarantee it returns layer `\"global\"` and the global path when the project copy is absent but the global copy exists.\n- Keep baked-in (empty path, caller uses `defaultplugin.FS()`) valid only for name==\"ddx\"; return an error for an unknown plugin present in no tier.\n- Add table-driven coverage in cli/internal/registry/resolve_test.go using `t.Setenv(\"XDG_DATA_HOME\", ...)` and a temp projectRoot with an in-tree `.ddx/` so both tiers are exercised deterministically.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Changing how install/update/doctor WRITE plugins (covered by sibling beads).\n- Removing the existing per-project install (both modes coexist).\n- Migrating any legacy ~/.config/ddx paths.\n- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.\n\nDEPS\n- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution): `ResolvePlugin` routes through `ddxroot.Path()` and `ddxroot.GlobalDir()`; both must be agreed-on. No other deps — this bead is the foundation the install/update/doctor siblings build on.","events_attachment":"ddx-536c3dd2/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-536c3dd2","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:plugin","area:config","kind:feature","phase:3","initiative:zero-footprint","principle:skills-personas"],"parent":"ddx-937de9fc","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-b67715d8","status":"closed","title":"registry: ResolvePlugin honors project→global→baked-in precedence with table tests","updated_at":"2026-06-03T07:21:01.247260548Z","work-no-changes-count":1}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:205:{"acceptance":"1. 'ddx bead dep add \u003cbead\u003e \u003cdep\u003e' HARD-REJECTS the edge when \u003cdep\u003e is \u003cbead\u003e's parent OR any ancestor in the parent chain. Error message names the offending ancestor and prints the relevant parent-chain. Test: ddx_bead_dep_add_rejects_parent_ancestor.\n2. 'ddx bead create --depends-on X --parent Y' with X == Y (or X an ancestor of Y) HARD-REJECTS at creation time with the same error shape as AC #1. No silent rewrite.\n3. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./... passes.\n4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","created_at":"2026-06-06T03:22:30.475161508Z","description":"PROBLEM\nParent bead ddx-e19185aa is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 3 of that parent scope.\n\nROOT CAUSE\nThe decomposition action (currently invoked when a bead is too large for one execution and the agent splits it into child beads) is writing the parent's ID as a dep edge on every child. This produces an unbreakable deadlock:\n\n  e29892a4 (parent, open) -- parent --\u003e child_A\n  e29892a4 -- parent --\u003e child_B\n  child_A.deps = [e29892a4]           \u003c- BAD: parent is also a dep\n  child_B.deps = [child_A, e29892a4]  \u003c- BAD\n\nThe parent stays open until its children close. Every child is blocked on the parent. Nothing can be claimed. ddx work loops on the parent, the orchestrator emits 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale', the worker retries, and burns claim budget without progress.\n\nObserved scenario (niflheim, 2026-06-04 04:30-05:13 UTC):\n- Parent: niflheim-e29892a4 ('feat(delta): async write-behind ...')\n- 10 children created during planning loops (23223661, a44a8ceb, 054c89b7, 0470ba0d, f10e9e35, f076b08c, 6c041162, 94e1df73, 9da62c2d, 5e4f9fb2)\n- Every child carried deps=[..siblings.., niflheim-e29892a4]\n- ~50 minutes of agent time + 4 strong-policy claude/opus attempts with zero feature commits; only tracker chores\n- Unwedged manually by closing the parent via 'ddx bead close', after which siblings drained correctly in foundational order\n\nRoot cause is structural, not model-capability — auto-upgrading to a stronger model does NOT fix this. The orchestrator already detects the no-progress state but its only remediation is 'retry parent', which re-enters the same loop.\n\nThere is also a 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning emitted by the readiness check during this state; that's a downstream symptom of the same problem, not the cause.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nImplement only this child acceptance slice:\n- 'ddx bead dep add \u003cbead\u003e \u003cdep\u003e' HARD-REJECTS the edge when \u003cdep\u003e is \u003cbead\u003e's parent OR any ancestor in the parent chain. Error message names the offending ancestor and prints the relevant parent-chain. Test: ddx_bead_dep_add_rejects_parent_ancestor.\n- 'ddx bead create --depends-on X --parent Y' with X == Y (or X an ancestor of Y) HARD-REJECTS at creation time with the same error shape as AC #1. No silent rewrite.\n\nNON-SCOPE\nDo not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{\"resolved_provider\":\"codex\",\"resolved_model\":\"gpt-5.4-mini\",\"route_reason\":\"explicit_pin_discovery_unavailable\",\"fallback_chain\":[]}","created_at":"2026-06-07T02:14:59.440423267Z","kind":"routing","source":"legacy .ddx/beads.jsonl:208:{"acceptance":"1. `ddx init --global` creates `~/.claude/skills/ddx` and `~/.agents/skills/ddx` pointing into `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/` and writes `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml` — TestInitGlobal_CreatesAgentTierLinks.\n2. `ddx init` (no flag, in-project) does NOT write to any `plugins/` tree; the plugin lazy-resolves from the global layer — TestInitProject_DoesNotInstallPlugins.\n3. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./cmd/... green.\n4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"3946d29110f96ee9d380f6ad28f88ab1fa4fb526","created_at":"2026-06-02T01:50:49.178056222Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-64ecd687","depends_on_id":"ddx-07af62e6","type":"depends_on","created_at":"2026-06-02T03:19:04Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-64ecd687","depends_on_id":"ddx-42c2c630","type":"depends_on","created_at":"2026-06-02T03:19:04Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM\nFirst-time per-machine setup has no entry point, and `ddx init` eagerly installs the default ddx plugin into every project. The intended model is: `ddx init --global` does one-time machine setup (global default plugin + agent-tier links + global config), while plain `ddx init` only creates `\u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/` and lazily resolves plugins from the global layer on first use.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- cli/cmd/init.go:73 `runInit` always installs the default ddx plugin (init.go:271-284 via `registry.InstallPackageFromFS(pkg, defaultplugin.FS(), workingDir)`), producing `.ddx/plugins/ddx`, `.agents/skills/ddx`, `.claude/skills/ddx` unconditionally.\n- There is no `--global` flag and no `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml` writer.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n1. Add `--global` bool flag to `ddx init` (cli/cmd/init.go).\n2. init --global: install the default `ddx` plugin to `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx` (via the install-global path from the sibling install bead), create agent-tier links `~/.claude/skills/ddx` and `~/.agents/skills/ddx` pointing into the global plugin dir, and write `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml` with convention defaults.\n3. init (no flag): create `\u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/` and the manifest, but do NOT install any plugin — plugins lazy-resolve via `registry.ResolvePlugin` (sibling resolver bead), preferring project over global, on first use. Remove the eager install at init.go:271-284 from the no-flag path.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- The resolver precedence itself (sibling bead: registry two-layer resolution).\n- install/update `--global` wiring (sibling bead).\n- `ddx doctor` reporting and spec/doc updates (sibling bead: doctor + specs).\n- Migrating any `~/.config/ddx/` legacy paths.\n\nDEPS\nddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution); sibling registry-resolver bead; sibling install/update --global bead (reuse its agent-tier link helper).","events_attachment":"ddx-64ecd687/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-64ecd687","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:plugin","area:cli","kind:feature","phase:3","initiative:zero-footprint","principle:skills-personas"],"parent":"ddx-937de9fc","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-a08a425b","status":"closed","title":"init: add `ddx init --global` umbrella setup and make plain `ddx init` lazy-resolve plugins","updated_at":"2026-06-02T05:38:52.288275281Z","work-no-changes-count":1}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:210:{"acceptance":"See bead body — 3 verifiable AC items: unit tests covering the four classification scenarios; lefthook green; manual smoke confirming worker emits 'server unreachable' instead of flagging beads when server is down.","created_at":"2026-06-01T01:13:59.24088712Z","description":"**Problem**\n\nWhen `ddx work --watch` runs against a `ddx server` that is partially\nunavailable (e.g. TLS handshake failure, agent-service connection\nrefusal, persistent route-resolution failures with no candidates), the\nworker treats every individual bead as the failure unit. Each attempt\ngets flagged with `operator_attention` via the progress-watchdog or\n`execution_failed` via the routing preflight, then the next bead is\ntried, then the next.\n\nResult: a single server-side problem (one bad TLS cert, an expired\nprovider credential, a stale model catalog) silently turns the entire\nready queue into \"needs human\" beads, even though the *beads* are fine\n— the *server* is broken. The worker keeps idling, the operator sees\n\"0 closures in 24h\" with no clear signal that this is environmental.\n\nObserved concretely on niflheim on 2026-05-31:\n\n- `ddx server` was serving a TLS cert from\n  `$DDX_PROJECT_ROOT/.ddx/server/tls/cert.pem` (Apr 17 fingerprint)\n  while the client `ddx work` pinned the cert in `~/.ddx/server/tls/`\n  (May 18 fingerprint). The server logged\n  `http: TLS handshake error from 127.0.0.1:XXXXX: remote error: tls:\n  bad certificate` every ~5 min for ~36 hours.\n- Every `niflheim-71365a9f` attempt during that window failed with\n  `ResolveRoute: no viable routing candidate: 16 candidates rejected,\n  outcome_reason=no_viable_provider`.\n- After 15+ attempts the worker flagged the bead for operator\n  attention, then quietly idled with `execution-ready=0 blocked=47\n  operator-attention=17 needs-human/investigation=10`. No clear\n  signal to the operator that the *server* was the cause.\n\n**Desired behavior**\n\nThe worker should detect server-level failures separately from\nbead-level failures. Two-tier classification:\n\n1. **Bead-level failure** (current behavior): the bead's tests/lint\n   fail, the agent commits something invalid, etc. → mark the bead\n   `execution_failed`, retry per policy, eventually\n   `operator-attention`.\n2. **Server-level failure** (new): TLS handshake error, agent-service\n   connection refused, persistent `no_viable_provider` across ≥ K\n   distinct beads in the last M minutes (default K=3, M=10) → mark\n   the *server* unavailable, NOT the individual beads. Loop into a\n   probe-and-resume mode: every 30 s ping\n   `https://server/api/health`; the moment the server returns 200\n   and a small `ddx run --text` smoke test succeeds, re-enter the\n   queue. None of the beads attempted during the server-failure\n   window get penalized.\n\nThe server-failure backoff prevents the per-bead operator-attention\nflagging storm we observed. It also makes the actual root cause\nvisible — `ddx work` should emit a single distinctive log line\n(\"server unreachable: holding queue until /api/health returns\") and\nexpose that state via `ddx work status` so the operator notices it.\n\n**In-scope files (likely; verify before edit)**\n\n- `cli/internal/agent/service_run.go` — wraps `ResolveRoute` →\n  classification site for routing failures.\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead*.go` — the place where\n  `no_viable_provider` is turned into `outcome_reason` for the bead.\n- The watcher loop in `cli/internal/work/` (or wherever `ddx work\n  --watch` lives) — needs the new server-unavailable state machine.\n- `cli/internal/agent/` — health-probe helper.\n- New unit tests covering: (a) K consecutive `no_viable_provider`\n  across distinct bead IDs trips server-failure mode; (b)\n  server-failure backoff doesn't penalize the beads it was about to\n  try; (c) successful health probe transitions back to normal queue\n  drain; (d) TLS handshake error from the server is classified as\n  server-level regardless of bead.\n\n**Acceptance**\n\n1. `go test ./cli/internal/agent/... ./cli/internal/work/...` passes\n   with the new tests above.\n2. `lefthook run pre-commit` (or equivalent gates) green.\n3. Manual smoke: stop `ddx server`, run `ddx work --watch`; observe\n   the worker emits \"server unreachable\" within 30 s and does NOT\n   flag any beads for operator attention while the server is down.\n   Restart `ddx server`; observe queue drain resumes.\n\n**Out of scope**\n\n- Auto-repairing server-side issues (TLS cert refresh, credential\n  rotation). Operator still has to fix the server. The worker just\n  has to detect and not amplify the failure.\n- Multi-server failover.\n- Changing the existing per-bead `operator-attention` semantics —\n  those stay for true bead-level failures.\n\n**Severity**: P1. Prevents quiet queue-stall pattern that wastes\noperator hours on triage when the actual cause is environmental.","id":"ddx-658cb394","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:work","area:agent","kind:reliability","kind:operator-experience"],"priority":1,"schema_version":1,"status":"open","title":"ddx work: detect server-level failures separately from bead-level (don't flag beads when server is unavailable)","updated_at":"2026-06-06T09:49:52.633763488Z","work-last-detail":"tracker lock timeout (max elapsed, lock: /Users/erik/Projects/ddx/.ddx/.git-tracker.lock, owner pid: 1993005)","work-last-status":"preclaim-hook-failed","work-retry-after":"2026-06-06T09:50:22Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:213:{"acceptance":"1. Agent and Claude execute-bead prompt snapshots no longer instruct `ddx bead dep add \u003cchild-id\u003e \u003cparent-id\u003e`; they instruct parent metadata plus `parent -\u003e child` dependency semantics. Test: TestExecuteBeadPromptSnapshotsUseParentToChildDecompositionEdges.","created_at":"2026-06-07T02:24:01.272784138Z","description":"PROBLEM\nParent bead ddx-f3c3b38a is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 3 of that parent scope.\n\nROOT CAUSE\nPROBLEM: Decomposition can still produce child beads whose deps include the parent when agent-facing instructions or structured decomposition payloads request `child -\u003e parent` edges. That recreates the deadlock even if the direct `dep add` path is fixed only later or if another writer bypasses the CLI.\n\nROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE: The execute-bead prompt snapshots instruct agents to run `ddx bead dep add \u003cchild-id\u003e \u003cparent-id\u003e` at `cli/internal/agent/testdata/execute_bead_prompt_snapshots/agent.txt:18` and `cli/internal/agent/testdata/execute_bead_prompt_snapshots/claude.txt:16`. The preclaim readiness payload model includes suggested child `Parent` and `Deps` fields at `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:259`, but the decomposition application path at `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4678` only creates children and wires parent deps, with no golden assertion that children exclude the parent from deps.\n\nPROPOSED FIX: Update live prompt construction and checked-in prompt snapshots so decomposition instructions create children with `parent=\u003cparent-id\u003e` and wire only `parent -\u003e child` dependencies. Add validation in the programmatic decomposition path that rejects or strips any suggested child dep equal to the parent or an ancestor before tracker mutation. Add a golden e2e test that decomposes a bead into N children and asserts every child has `Parent == parent.ID` and no dependency target equal to the parent.\n\nNON-SCOPE: Do not implement the global `DepAdd` invariant here except where needed by decomposition writer tests. Do not add doctor repair. Do not change post-attempt remediation or operator-attention behavior. Do not relax AC-map completeness rules.\n\nGOVERNING ARTIFACTS: `docs/triage/decomposition.md:65` requires child beads to link back via `parent`; `docs/triage/decomposition.md:72` requires dependency edges in the parent-to-child direction; `docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:193` requires executable child beads with dependency edges.\n\nINHERITED DEPENDENCIES: none specified by the parent bead.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nImplement only this child acceptance slice:\n- Agent and Claude execute-bead prompt snapshots no longer instruct `ddx bead dep add \u003cchild-id\u003e \u003cparent-id\u003e`; they instruct parent metadata plus `parent -\u003e child` dependency semantics. Test: TestExecuteBeadPromptSnapshotsUseParentToChildDecompositionEdges.\n\nNON-SCOPE\nDo not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.","id":"ddx-66d090f7","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:lifecycle","area:decomposition","kind:safety","kind:correctness","ac-quality:needs-refinement","decomposed"],"parent":"ddx-f3c3b38a","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"status":"open","title":"Stop decomposition writers from creating parent back-edges: part 1","updated_at":"2026-06-07T02:24:01.272784138Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:216:{"acceptance":"1. TestInitGlobal_CreatesAgentTierLinks (cli/cmd/init_test.go or init_functional_test.go) asserts `ddx init --global` makes ~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx resolve into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/ and writes ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml.\n2. TestInitProject_DoesNotInstallPlugins (cli/cmd/init_test.go) asserts plain `ddx init` creates `\u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/` and writes nothing under plugins/.\n3. TestDoctor_ReportsInstallTopology (cli/cmd/doctor_plugins_test.go) asserts `ddx doctor` output contains a \"Global Install (...) — ok|missing\" line and a \"Project Install (...) — ok|missing|lazy-resolves-to-global\" line, and that the project line reads `lazy-resolves-to-global` when only the global install is present.\n4. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./cmd/... ./internal/registry/... green.\n5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"5843edc5ebcb5f64b14cae384b8f22dbb6bdbea5","created_at":"2026-06-03T12:37:10.50344695Z","description":"PROBLEM (epic ddx-602ae31a, Principle 2)\n\nFirst-time machine setup needs a single umbrella command (`ddx init --global`) that installs the default ddx plugin globally, creates the agent-tier links, and writes the global config. A plain `ddx init` must create the project workspace WITHOUT installing plugins (they lazy-resolve from the global/baked-in layers on first use). `ddx doctor` must surface both install layers so operators can see whether a project install exists or is lazy-resolving to global.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- `ddx init --global` dispatches to `initGlobal()` (cli/cmd/init.go:75,318), which: creates ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx (init.go:319-323), installs the bundled default plugin there (init.go:328), creates agent-tier links so every harness discovers the skill (init.go:314-316), and writes ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml with convention defaults via `writeGlobalConfig` (init.go:351-366).\n- Plain `ddx init` (no flag) must create `\u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/` and must NOT write to plugins/ — plugins lazy-resolve via ResolvePlugin (project→global→baked-in).\n- `ddx doctor` topology reporting lives at cli/cmd/doctor.go:415 `reportSkillInstallTopology`, which prints `Global Install (\u003cglobalPath\u003e) — ok|missing` (doctor.go:447) and `Project Install (\u003cprojectPath\u003e) — ok|missing|lazy-resolves-to-global` (doctor.go:448), using `registry.ResolvePlugin` to detect lazy resolution (doctor.go:431-444).\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n1. Verify `ddx init --global` creates ~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx pointing into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/ and writes the global config.\n2. Verify plain `ddx init` creates the project workspace and writes nothing under plugins/.\n3. Verify `ddx doctor` renders both the \"Global Install\" and \"Project Install\" lines, including the `lazy-resolves-to-global` project status when no project install exists but the global one does.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Runtime lookup precedence implementation (sibling child — ResolvePlugin).\n- The `--global` install/update surface and project install modes (sibling children).\n- Removing the existing per-project install entirely (both modes coexist).\n- Migrating any ~/.config/ddx/ legacy paths.\n- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.\n\nDEPS\n- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — initGlobal and doctor topology both route through `ddxroot.GlobalDir()`.","events_attachment":"ddx-67b477d3/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-67b477d3","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:cli","area:config","kind:feature","phase:3","initiative:zero-footprint","principle:skills-personas"],"parent":"ddx-937de9fc","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-524be1ab","status":"closed","title":"init/doctor: `ddx init --global` umbrella setup, `ddx init` lazy (no plugin install), doctor reports both layers","updated_at":"2026-06-03T16:48:57.361307585Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:218:{"acceptance":"`ddx install --force --local \u003cpath\u003e` against a repo containing broken symlinks ONLY under gitignored paths completes successfully. The validation logic either consults .gitignore or skips well-known scratch/dotfile paths.","closing_commit_sha":"08558b9bffef71ec63fdf2485ee4f4d054d8c166","created_at":"2026-05-18T02:12:47.205231666Z","description":"## Summary\n\nObserved against ddx v0.6.2-alpha103-12-ge6379ebc5 on 2026-05-18.\n\n`ddx install \u003cplugin\u003e --force --local \u003crepo\u003e` validates ALL symlinks in the source tree, including those under gitignored paths. Result: any scratch/tmp file with a stale symlink (e.g. files written by a previous `ddx doctor` invocation under doctor/home/.codex/tmp/) breaks the install despite being gitignored and never intended to ship.\n\n## Reproduction\n\n1. In a repo with `doctor/home/.codex/tmp/` gitignored (matches `.codex`)\n2. Run any operation that creates broken symlinks under that dir (e.g. an old codex CLI install no longer present)\n3. Run `ddx install helix --force --local /path/to/helix`\n4. Install fails: `validating package structure: ... broken symlink target ...`\n\nRepro on HELIX:\n```\nddx install helix --force --local /home/erik/Projects/helix\n# Error: validating package structure:\n#   doctor/home/.codex/tmp/arg0/codex-arg0zt0PZe/apply_patch: broken symlink target ...\n```\n\nWorkaround: rm -rf the offending tmp paths and retry.\n\n## Expected behavior\n\n`ddx install --local` should skip files that are gitignored in the source repository (run via `git check-ignore -v \u003cpath\u003e` or read `.gitignore` directly). It never diagnoses or auto-remediates. FEAT-010 §Idle-Path Diagnosis and Auto-Remediation requires the loop to diagnose every non-ready bead, fire matching remediators, and re-scan without sleeping when at least one remediation succeeded.\n\nROOT CAUSE\nNo diagnose+dispatch logic exists at the insertion point.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nInside execute_bead_loop.go, after ReadyExecutionBreakdown populates breakdown (~line 1265) and before applyStop/sleepOrWake (~line 1271), insert the idle-path auto-remediation block.\n\nPseudo-flow (translate to Go):\n1. Build childIndex, openChildIndex, depIndex from the store (these may already be computed inside ReadyExecutionBreakdown; expose them or rebuild as needed).\n2. Walk breakdown.Epics ∪ breakdown.DepWaiting ∪ breakdown.OperatorAttention. For each bead, call bead.Diagnose(...) (from cli/internal/bead/diagnose.go).\n3. Switch on the BlockReasonCode:\n   - superseded_pending_close: queue a no-op (the cascade happens automatically inside any subsequent Close(); to actively trigger it on a known X, call store.Close on the SUPERSEDER Y if it's somehow still open in this branch — typically not; if X is the bead and Y is already closed but the cascade never ran, call a new store.RunSupersededCascade(X, Y) helper that runs the same scope-checked cascade out of band).\n   - closure_candidate_misclassified: this should disappear once the RC-1 classifier fix lands; if it still appears, log and surface (defensive).\n   - dead_intermediate_all_children_closed: trigger the existing closure-evaluation path against the bead.\n   - dead_intermediate_open_children_pending OR epic_of_epic: skip (recursion via children handles it).\n   - genuinely_needs_decomposition: queue an AutoDecomposeEpic call. At most ONE auto-decompose dispatch per loop iteration (track a counter). Skip if --no-auto-epic-decompose set.\n   - dep_blocked_by, parent_child_state_conflict, claimed_in_progress, provider_route_unavailable, gated_by_budget_or_cooldown, malformed_parent_or_dep_ref, dependency_cycle, closed_or_missing_parent, auto_remediation_exhausted, no_diagnosis: no idle-path action; will surface via ddx work focus.\n   - stale_graph_index: trigger an index rebuild and re-diagnose next loop.\n4. Execute queued remediations sequentially. Track count of successes.\n5. If any remediation succeeded, emit loop.auto_remediated event with {remediation_counts: {...}, total_attempted, total_succeeded}, clearTransientCandidateSkips, and `continue` (re-scan without entering sleepOrWake).\n6. If none succeeded, fall through to the existing emit(\"loop.idle\", ...) + sleepOrWake path.\n\nAdd operator override flags to cli/cmd/work.go: --no-auto-supersede-close, --no-auto-epic-decompose, --no-auto-closure-reclassify. Plumb them through to the work loop via runtime config (mirror how existing --max-recovery-cost flows in).\n\nAdd config key work.autoRemediations: { autoSupersedeClose: bool, autoEpicDecompose: bool, autoClosureReclassify: bool } with defaults of true. Flags override config; per-project config overrides defaults.\n\nReuse existing cost budget threading: AutoDecomposeEpic counts against --max-recovery-cost just like the existing post-TooLarge decomposer does. Honor SmartRetryCooldown / SetExecutionCooldown precedent for per-bead cooldown after failed auto-decompose (already implemented in the auto_decompose_epic bead).\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Implementing the diagnoser, RC-1 fix, RC-2 cascade, RC-3 walk-up, RC-6 handler — those are separate beads this one depends on.\n- Operator surface changes (ddx work focus, ddx work plan) — separate beads.\n\nPARENT AND DEPS\nParent: ddx-2a1ece2b. TestInstallGlobal_WritesToGlobalTreeAndAgentLinks (cli/cmd/install_global_test.go) asserts `ddx install \u003cname\u003e --global --silent` installs to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/\u003cname\u003e/ and creates a symlink-or-copy under ~/.claude/skills/\u003cname\u003e and ~/.agents/skills/\u003cname\u003e, and records the entry in the global state file only.\n2. TestUpdateGlobal_UpdatesGlobalNotProject (cli/cmd/update_test.go) asserts `ddx update --global` updates only the global plugin tree and does not modify the project install or project state.\n3. A test asserts `ddx plugin list --global` and `ddx plugin show \u003cname\u003e --global` enumerate/report from the global tier.\n4. library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md documents the global install location and agent-tier link paths.\n5. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./cmd/... ./internal/registry/... green.\n6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"4ac5ec29273c18d7542398a829434fd1683b8ee6","created_at":"2026-06-03T12:37:10.38876229Z","description":"PROBLEM (epic ddx-602ae31a, Principle 2)\n\nOperators who run one Claude Code/Codex install per machine need a `--global` surface so skills/personas install once into the machine-wide DDx tree and are discoverable from every project without per-project setup. Today the global install and update paths must write to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/\u003cname\u003e/ and link into the home-tier agent skill dirs, using a separate global state file so they never touch a project's install.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- `ddx install` carries the `--global` flag at cli/cmd/install.go:49 and routes through `resolveInstallDirs(global)` (install.go:184) and `selectInstallState(global)` (install.go:202). For global=true, resolveInstallDirs returns installRoot=`ddxroot.GlobalDir()`, agentSkillsDir=`~/.agents/skills`, claudeSkillsDir=`~/.claude/skills` (install.go:185-193); selectInstallState returns `registry.LoadGlobalState`/`SaveGlobalState` (install.go:206-208). Targets are remapped by `adjustInstallTargets`/`remapSkillTargets` (install.go:215,235). Global installs skip the project auto-commit (install.go:174).\n- `ddx update --global` routes through `performGlobalUpdate` (cli/cmd/update.go:300), which operates exclusively on `registry.LoadGlobalState()`/`ddxroot.GlobalDir()` (update.go:301,337,345) and never touches the project tree; the flag is parsed at update.go:395 and dispatched at update.go:66.\n- The `--global` flag must also be present on `ddx plugin list`/`ddx plugin show` so query surfaces can target the global tier.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n1. Verify `ddx install \u003cname\u003e --global --silent` writes the plugin under ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/\u003cname\u003e/ and creates a symlink-or-copy under ~/.claude/skills/\u003cname\u003e and ~/.agents/skills/\u003cname\u003e, recording the result in the global state file (not the project state).\n2. Verify `ddx update --global` updates only the global plugin tree and leaves any project install untouched.\n3. Add/confirm `--global` on `ddx plugin list` and `ddx plugin show`.\n4. Document the global install location in library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md \"How this skill works\".\n5. The ddx-validate pre-commit hook (ddx-777f4a09) must continue to scope skill validation to staged skill files regardless of install location.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Runtime lookup precedence (sibling child — ResolvePlugin).\n- Project (non-global) install behavior and init/doctor wiring (sibling children).\n- Removing the existing per-project install entirely (both modes coexist).\n- Migrating any ~/.config/ddx/ legacy paths.\n- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.\n\nDEPS\n- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — global install routes through `ddxroot.GlobalDir()`.","events_attachment":"ddx-6931d485/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-6931d485","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:cli","area:plugin","kind:feature","phase:3","initiative:zero-footprint","principle:skills-personas","triage:no-changes-unjustified"],"parent":"ddx-937de9fc","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-93a1e107","status":"closed","title":"install/update: --global surface installs to XDG global tree + agent-tier skill links","updated_at":"2026-06-03T17:16:46.067151221Z","work-no-changes-count":1}

The hook fires\non every acquire+release cycle at line 232. **No production code wires the\nsink to anything operator-visible** — the sample is computed and dropped on\nthe floor. Similarly, `lockmetrics.Instrument(\"tracker.lock\",\n\"tracker.commit\", fn)` at line 231 always passes the literal label\n\"tracker.commit\" regardless of which of the five call sites\n(`durable_audit.go:50`, `execute_bead.go:694`, `execute_bead.go:1493`,\n`execute_bead_land.go:839`, `execute_bead_land.go:857`,\n`execute_bead_land.go:1210`) is holding the lock — so the metric cannot\ndistinguish a slow durable-audit commit from a slow Land or a slow\npre-dispatch worktree creation.\n\nOBSERVED 2026-05-28: a niflheim drain with two concurrent `ddx work`\nprocesses saw worker B (pid 681801) time out on the tracker lock with\n`tracker lock timeout (max elapsed, lock: ..., owner pid: 424293)` after\nthe 30s `MaxElapsed` budget defined in `DefaultLockRetryPolicy`. We have\nno log of how long A held the lock or which call site was holding —\ndiagnosis required reading the source to enumerate the call sites and\ninfer plausible candidates (pre-dispatch worktree creation, Land merge).\nThe metric to answer \"was that a 31s hold or a 2.9s hold blocked by a\n30s queue\" is computed but never logged.\n\nROOT CAUSE: Two coupled gaps —\n  1. `withTrackerLock` / `withMainGitLock` (line 145 and 128) take no\n     section/caller identifier, so the sink receives only `LockDir`\n     plus timings. There is no way to attribute a long hold to a\n     specific call site from the metric alone.\n  2. No worker startup code installs a `TrackerLockMetricsSink`. The\n     hook is a dead extension point in production.\n\nPROPOSED FIX:\n\nA) Plumb a section identifier through the lock API:\n   1. Extend `withMainGitLock` and `withTrackerLock` to accept a\n      `section string` parameter (e.g. \"durable_audit\", \"pre_dispatch\",\n      \"land\", \"preserve_dirt\", \"ancestry_local\").\n   2. Extend `TrackerLockSample` with `Section string`.\n   3. Pass `section` to `lockmetrics.Instrument` (replacing the hardcoded\n      \"tracker.commit\") so cap- and metrics-level views also distinguish\n      sites.\n   4. Update all six call sites listed above to pass a stable section\n      string.\n\nB) Wire the sink to the worker log in `ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime`:\n   1. In `execute_bead_loop.go` near where the runtime / log is\n      constructed (search for `runtime.Log` usage near `emit(...)`\n      callsites), install a sink that, on every release, emits a\n      structured line. Use the existing `emit` event stream when\n      available (`emit(\"loop.tracker_lock\", map[string]any{...})`) so\n      the data shows up in the same channel as `loop.idle`,\n      `loop.operator_attention`, etc. Always also write a plain-text\n      log line for terminal monitors:\n        `tracker_lock section=\u003cx\u003e wait=\u003cdur\u003e hold=\u003cdur\u003e retries=\u003cn\u003e`\n   2. Make the install per-worker and idempotent (the sink is a package\n      global today; if multiple workers run in the same process the\n      sink must serialize or use sync/atomic). For per-process worker\n      isolation as it stands, idempotent install at worker start is\n      sufficient.\n\nC) Surface lock samples in `ddx work status`:\n   - Out of scope for this bead. Track separately if useful — the log\n     wiring is the immediate need.\n\nNON-SCOPE:\n- Narrowing the lock-held region itself — tracked separately in the\n  sibling bead about pre-dispatch and Land lock-surface narrowing.\n- Changing the retry policy (30s MaxElapsed, 200 MaxRetries, 1s ceiling).\n- Adding a new metric backend or storage; emit through existing\n  `loop.*` event stream and `runtime.Log`.\n- Adding telemetry to non-execute-bead lock sites (e.g.\n  `cmd/bead.go:167`).\n\nIN-SCOPE FILES:\n- cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go (add Section field, plumb param)\n- cli/internal/agent/durable_audit.go (pass \"durable_audit\" section)\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go (pass \"preserve_dirt\" at 694,\n  \"pre_dispatch\" at 1493)\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go (pass \"ancestry_fetch\" at 839,\n  \"ancestry_local\" at 857, \"land\" at 1210)\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (install sink at worker start)\n- cli/cmd/bead.go (pass a section identifier at line 167 —\n  \"cmd_bead_write\" or similar)\n- cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock_test.go (extend tests for Section\n  field and sink behavior)\n\nOUT-OF-SCOPE FILES:\n- gitlock package\n- cli/internal/lockmetrics — already accepts the label parameter; only\n  change is in callers\n- ddx work status command (separate bead if surfaced there later)","events_attachment":"ddx-69a9ecd3/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-69a9ecd3","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:agent","area:execution","area:observability","kind:feature","kind:operability","phase:iterate","spec:FEAT-010"],"priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-33072644","spec-id":"FEAT-010","status":"closed","title":"execute-bead: wire TrackerLockMetricsSink to worker log with section identifier (current sink is unread, lock hold/wait times invisible)","updated_at":"2026-06-06T01:30:58.684619682Z"}

The skill-install-topology ADR (ADR-025 or equivalent) and the CLAUDE.md \"Persona System\" precedence text are updated, and library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md documents the install locations, all landed in this bead's commit.\n5. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./cmd/... ./internal/registry/... green.\n6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"299098ab440486a2d72db530d3eae213925c1c19","created_at":"2026-06-03T05:42:23.121155693Z","description":"PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)\n\n`ddx update` targets the project path unconditionally with no `--global` surface, `ddx doctor` does not report the global vs project install layers, and the install-topology decision is undocumented.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- cli/cmd/update.go targets ddxroot.Path() unconditionally and has no `--global` flag and no in-tree-vs-convention branch.\n- ddx doctor has no lines reporting the global tier (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx) or the project tier (ddxroot.Path()/plugins/ddx) install status.\n- No ADR or CLAUDE.md text records the install-to-agent vs install-in-project topology and precedence.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n1. Add `--global` to `ddx update`. When set, update only the global plugin tree under ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/\u003cname\u003e; do not touch the project tree.\n2. Without `--global`, `ddx update` updates the project install in whichever mode is active (in-tree \u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/plugins or convention-mode XDG project plugins).\n3. `ddx doctor` reports both layers: a \"Global Install (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx) — ok | missing\" line and a \"Project Install (ddxroot.Path()/plugins/ddx) — ok | missing | lazy-resolves-to-global\" line, using the tier-resolution info exposed by the registry bead.\n4.
