The concrete types (`*Store`, `RawBackend`, `JSONLBackend`, `ExternalBackend`, `AxonStore`, `*WatcherHub`) live at `cli/internal/bead/` — publicly importable from any other package.\n\nThe goal of TD-027 §21 is **physical impossibility** of bypass: not lint-prevented, not review-prevented, but a Go compiler error if any code outside `cli/internal/bead/` tries to import the concrete storage implementations. This bead enforces that via Go's `internal/` rule.\n\n# ROOT CAUSE\n- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` public.\n- `cli/internal/bead/backend_jsonl.go` — `JSONLBackend` public.\n- `cli/internal/bead/backend_external.go` — `ExternalBackend` public.\n- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go` (or `AxonStore` post `ddx-9c5bca8f`) — public.\n- `cli/internal/bead/watcher.go` — `*WatcherHub` public.\n- All supporting concrete implementation files (`claim_liveness.go`, `attachments.go`, `archive.go`, `migrate.go`, etc.) — public.\n\nPlus the final assembly step (formerly \"K\" in earlier breakdowns): `Backend` composite interface declared, `ReadOnlyBackend` convenience composite, compile-time assertion `var _ Backend = (*Store)(nil)`. These were deferred from Foundation because the composite couldn't be asserted until all per-concern beads removed their methods.\n\n# PROPOSED FIX\n\nThis bead does three load-bearing things in one atomic change:\n\n## 1. Narrow caller field types from `*bead.Store` to `bead.Backend` (or narrower)\n\nEvery caller file in `cli/cmd/`, `cli/internal/agent/`, `cli/internal/server/`, `cli/internal/escalation/`, `cli/internal/agentmetrics/`, `cli/internal/attemptmetrics/`, `cli/internal/processmetrics/`, `cli/internal/exec/`, `cli/internal/agent/try/` that holds `*bead.Store` as a struct field or local variable is updated:\n\n- Most callers: change `*bead.Store` to `bead.Backend` (full interface). Mechanical rename.\n- Narrow candidates (identified during per-concern beads): use narrower sub-interface where appropriate (e.g., `cli/cmd/work_focus.go` → `bead.BeadQueries`; `cli/internal/agentmetrics/loader.go` → `bead.BeadReader`).\n- Documented exceptions remain on concrete `*Store` access OR on direct file I/O (allowlisted):\n  - `cli/cmd/bead_doctor.go` — file-level repair; reads `.ddx/beads.jsonl` directly.\n  - `cli/cmd/bead.go` (git auto-commit + conflict-resolution code paths only) — operates on the file as a file.\n  - `cli/cmd/sync.go` — `ddx sync` operates on the file.\n\n## 2. Add `Backend` composite + compile-time assertion\n\n- Declare `Backend` as the union of all 11 sub-interfaces:\n  `BeadInitializer + BeadReader + BeadLifecycle + BeadEventReader + BeadEventWriter + BeadQueries + BeadDependencyReader + BeadDependencyWriter + BeadArchive + BeadInterchangeReader + BeadInterchangeWriter`.\n- Declare `ReadOnlyBackend` convenience composite (5 reader sub-interfaces) per TD-027 §6.1.\n- Add compile-time assertion: `var _ Backend = (*Store)(nil)`.\n\n## 3. Move concretes to internal/\n\n- Create `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/` and `cli/internal/bead/internal/lifecycle/`.\n- Move (with package rename):\n  - `store.go` → `internal/storage/store.go` (package `storage`)\n  - `backend_jsonl.go` → `internal/storage/backend_jsonl.go`\n  - `backend_external.go` → `internal/storage/backend_external.go`\n  - `axon_backend.go` / `axon_store.go` → `internal/storage/`\n  - `claim_liveness.go` → `internal/storage/`\n  - `attachments.go`, `archive.go`, `migrate.go`, etc. → `internal/storage/`\n  - `watcher.go` → `internal/lifecycle/watcher_hub.go` (package `lifecycle`)\n  - `RawBackend` and `OperationApplier` interfaces also move into `internal/storage/`\n- Create `cli/internal/bead/factory.go` as the SOLE public construction path:\n  ```go\n  package bead\n  import \"cli/internal/bead/internal/storage\"\n  func NewStore(opts StoreOptions) (Backend, error) { return storage.New(opts) }\n  ```\n- The public `cli/internal/bead/` package surface is now: `Bead` struct, `Backend` + sub-interfaces, `Operation` + named ops, errors, `IDGenerator` + `ValidateID`, ctx accessors, `LifecycleSubscriber` interface, `Migrator` factory (from MIG bead), and the `ops/` helper sub-packages.\n\n## 4. Lint cleanup\n\nAfter lockdown, two analyzers in the lint suite (`ddx-e91a45c0`) are redundant because Go's `internal/` rule enforces the same property at the compiler level:\n- `concrete-store-methods` — removed.\n- `no-internal-store-construction` — removed.\n\nRetained analyzers (defense-in-depth inside `cli/internal/bead/`):\n- `direct-bead-jsonl-io` (callers still shouldn't bypass the abstraction)\n- `no-new-rawbackend-impls` (new backends use Backend directly)\n- `typed-context-accessors-only` (ctx.Value discipline)\n\n# NON-SCOPE\n- Per-concern bead work — must complete first.\n- AxonStore implementation — `ddx-9c5bca8f`.\n- IMP (JSONL→Postgres importer) — separate downstream bead.\n- WIRE (real-wire integration tests) — separate downstream bead.\n\n# Acceptance criteria\n\n1. Every caller file in scope (per PROPOSED FIX §1) has its `*bead.Store` field/variable types narrowed to `bead.Backend` or a sub-interface, with the allowlist exceptions (`bead_doctor.go`, file-level paths in `bead.go`, `sync.go`).\n2. `cli/internal/bead/backend.go` declares `Backend` as the union of 11 sub-interfaces. `ReadOnlyBackend` declared per TD-027 §6.1.\n3. `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/` and `cli/internal/bead/internal/lifecycle/` exist; all concrete implementation files relocated with appropriate package renames.\n4. `cli/internal/bead/factory.go` is the sole public construction path. Exports `NewStore`, `NewLifecycleSubscriber`, `NewMigrator` (from MIG). No other public function constructs a concrete storage type.\n5. **Build must succeed**: `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go build ./...`. **Failure indicates incomplete per-concern bead migration** — diagnose the failing caller and complete its migration before retrying. Do NOT bypass via build tags or temporary public re-exports.\n6. Boundary verification test: `TestModuleBoundary_NoInternalImportsOutsideBead` — static analysis scans every Go file under `cli/` outside `cli/internal/bead/` and asserts none of them import `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage` or `cli/internal/bead/internal/lifecycle`. AST-based.\n7. Compile-time `var _ Backend = (*storage.Store)(nil)` exists inside `internal/storage/` (verifying Backend satisfaction now that the concrete type is private to that package).\n8. Lint cleanup: `concrete-store-methods` and `no-internal-store-construction` analyzers removed from the bead-lints binary. Documented in `cli/tools/lints/README.md`. The retained 3 analyzers stay.\n9. Existing tests pass without modification. `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./...` is green.\n10. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.\n\n# Labels\nphase:iterate, area:bead, area:storage, kind:refactor, spec:TD-027, reliability\n\n# Parent + Deps\nParent: ddx-8d747049\nDeps: all per-concern beads must complete before this bead can execute:\n- ddx-bca628fa (L)\n- ddx-e1c743d3 (C)\n- ddx-f39b41b3 (E)\n- ddx-aed7c7ab (X)\n- ddx-bc07270f (Q)\n- ddx-3af1c1a6 (D)\n- ddx-71d3a2de (A)\n- ddx-0ab16765 (MIG)\n\nPlus required infrastructure:\n- ddx-9c5bca8f (AxonStore — its concrete type is also relocated)\n- ddx-29f02cf4 (config factory — factory.go takes over construction)\n- ddx-900a8d38 (WatcherHub factory — must be migrated to BeadReader factory before lockdown)\n\n# What this earns\n\n**Physically impossible bypass enforced by the Go compiler.** Any future caller in `cli/cmd/`, `cli/internal/agent/`, `cli/internal/server/`, etc. that tries to import `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage` fails to build. The contract becomes: callers see only the public `Backend` interface (and sub-interfaces, Operations, sentinels, IDGenerator, factory); they cannot reach the concrete type.","id":"ddx-74452926","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:bead","area:storage","kind:refactor","phase:iterate","reliability","spec:TD-027"],"notes":"DESIGN HOLD 2026-05-11: parked alongside ddx-c6317784 until TD-027/TD-031 consolidation lands and operator clears via 'ddx bead cooldown clear \u003cid\u003e'.","parent":"ddx-8d747049","priority":1,"schema_version":1,"status":"open","title":"BL (Boundary Lockdown): caller narrowing + relocate concretes to internal/storage + Backend composite + compile-time enforcement (TD-027 §21)","updated_at":"2026-05-11T20:29:34.455757285Z"}

In both modes the project-scoped agent skill dirs (`\u003cproject\u003e/.claude/skills/` and `\u003cproject\u003e/.agents/skills/`) must link into whichever plugins/ path the mode resolves to.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- For global=false, `resolveInstallDirs` (cli/cmd/install.go:184) returns installRoot=`ddxroot.Path(context.Background(), f.WorkingDir)` and skill dirs under `f.WorkingDir/.agents/skills` and `f.WorkingDir/.claude/skills` (install.go:195-197). `ddxroot.Path()` is what selects the in-tree vs convention plugins root, so the install root automatically follows the mode.\n- `ddx update` (no flag) must update the project install in whichever mode is active; the non-global path in update.go (dispatched when `opts.Global` is false, update.go:66) operates on the project working dir's resolved root.\n- Project installs auto-commit trackable project changes (install.go:174-176) — global installs do not.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n1. A top-level operator-visible signal is needed when the worker's recent claim success rate drops to zero or below a configured threshold over a rolling window.\n\nROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE\nThe work loop owns claim-attempt telemetry and operator-visible events. Runtime configuration for claim throughput belongs with `ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime` in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:86-92`, and operator-visible loop events are emitted from the loop. Without a rolling claim-success-rate guard, repeated non-successes only appear as low-level intake warnings or idle events instead of a clear stuck-worker signal.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nTrack claim-attempt outcomes in a rolling window, default window=10 and threshold=0.0, and emit a single `loop.operator_attention` event plus stderr/log text when the full window is at or below threshold. Expose CLI knobs on `ddx work --watch` for window and threshold.\n\nNON-SCOPE\nDo not change verdict decoding, same-fingerprint escalation, shared schema files, README documentation beyond CLI help if needed, Fizeau route/harness configuration, lock handling from ddx-57c40485, or route-resolution wedge handling from ddx-8f2e0ebf. Do not make low claim rate close, park, or mutate individual beads by itself. Parent lifecycle, child dependency wiring, and tracker mutation are orchestrator-owned.\n\nGOVERNING ARTIFACT / CROSS-REF\nAR-2026-05-17 follow-up context; ADR-023 readiness warn-only policy; adjacent reliability bugs ddx-57c40485 and ddx-8f2e0ebf are cross-references only.\n\nDEPENDENCIES\nNone.","events_attachment":"ddx-7e0f78a5/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-7e0f78a5","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:execute-bead","area:pre-claim","area:workers","kind:bug","kind:reliability","observability","priority:reliability","severity:high","ac-quality:needs-refinement"],"parent":"ddx-f9ddaa68","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-bb692ef1","status":"closed","title":"Surface low claim-success-rate warnings from ddx work watch","updated_at":"2026-05-27T07:21:17.46046998Z","work-no-changes-count":1}

For projects with native build toolchains (Rust + cargo,\nJVM + gradle, Go + module cache, etc.), the fresh worktree starts with an\nempty toolchain build cache, so the agent's first build inside is a cold\nbuild from scratch. FEAT-010 §Layer 2 and TD-010 §Storage Model are silent\non this — they specify worktree creation and evidence capture but make no\nstatement about toolchain build-cache sharing or per-worktree I/O budgets.\n\nOBSERVED COST (2026-05-28, niflheim project):\n- A single in-flight `ddx try` attempt accumulated `target/` = 14 GB in the\n  execute-bead worktree.\n- Two concurrent workers + the project's own niflheim recovery run\n  collectively wrote ~23 GB to the same SSD over ~30 minutes.\n- The project's WAL recovery (read-bound, ~2 MB written) was visibly\n  starved by worker cargo build I/O.\n- Each fresh attempt repeats this cost — the worktree is destroyed at\n  attempt end (or preserved with `--no-merge`), so the cache is lost.\n\nROOT CAUSE (spec-level): FEAT-010 treats worktree resource cost as a\nnon-concern. The feature explicitly lists \"workspace creation, bead\nattempt, merge or preserve, evidence bundle\" but lists no resource-budget\nor cache-reuse hook surface. There is no ADR governing per-worktree I/O,\nno documented hook for projects to opt into shared toolchain caches, and\nno operator-visible counter that would surface this contention in\n`ddx work status` or `ddx doctor`.\n\nPROPOSED EVOLUTION (the executing agent should choose how to slot these\ninto the artifact stack; below is the recommended shape):\n\n1. **Amend FEAT-010 §Layer 2** (`docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md`):\n   - Add a \"Worktree resource expectations\" subsection acknowledging that\n     execute-bead worktrees do not, by default, share native toolchain\n     build caches with the parent checkout or with sibling attempts.\n   - State the operator's responsibility (set per-project env like\n     `CARGO_TARGET_DIR`, `GRADLE_USER_HOME`, `GOPATH/pkg/mod`, etc., or\n     project-level config) versus DDx's responsibility (pass through\n     environment unchanged, do not invent toolchain-specific knobs).\n   - Document that `ddx work` makes no concurrency guarantee for native\n     build steps inside attempts; concurrent workers may contend.\n\n2. **Amend TD-010** (`docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-010-executions.md`):\n   - Add a §Worktree environment passthrough subsection naming\n     `CARGO_TARGET_DIR`, `GRADLE_USER_HOME`, `MAVEN_OPTS`, `GOPATH`,\n     `GOMODCACHE`, `npm_config_cache`, `PIP_CACHE_DIR`, etc. as\n     environment variables that flow unmodified from the `ddx try`\n     caller into the agent process inside the worktree.\n   - Specify the invariant: DDx never sets, overrides, or unsets any\n     toolchain cache env var. Operator/project owns the policy.\n\n3. **Optionally add an ADR** (e.g., `ADR-027-worktree-resource-isolation.md`)\n   recording the decision to keep build-cache policy outside DDx, with the\n   rationale (cross-language, single-responsibility, prevents lock-in to a\n   specific toolchain). If the executing agent decides one ADR is overkill,\n   adding the same paragraph as a §Rationale block at the end of TD-010 is\n   acceptable.\n\n4. **Update CLAUDE.md \"Execution Evidence Convention\" section** if needed\n   to cross-reference the new TD-010 environment-passthrough subsection\n   so agents working in execute-bead worktrees know they may set\n   `CARGO_TARGET_DIR` (or equivalent) per project policy.\n\nNON-SCOPE:\n- Implementing build-cache mounting, sccache integration, or any\n  toolchain-specific knob inside ddx itself. This bead is documentation\n  only — it locks down the boundary so future implementation work has\n  a clear policy to refer to.\n- Changing `ddx try` worktree creation semantics or evidence capture.\n- Changing the worktree path under `~/.cache/ddx/exec-wt/`.\n- Adding `ddx doctor` or `ddx work status` instrumentation for disk\n  contention (potential follow-up bead, not this one).\n- Project-side fixes (niflheim has its own bead niflheim-e5cd7c69 for\n  the cargo-target-dir workflow documentation).\n\nIN-SCOPE FILES:\n- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md\n- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-010-executions.md\n- Optionally: docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-027-worktree-resource-isolation.md\n  (new) — only if the agent chooses the ADR path over TD-010 §Rationale.\n- Optionally: CLAUDE.md (cross-reference paragraph only, no behavior change).\n\nOUT-OF-SCOPE FILES:\n- Any cli/ code — this is a docs-only evolution.\n- Other FEAT/TD/ADR documents — touch only the listed artifacts unless\n  the agent discovers a direct conflicting claim in another doc, in\n  which case file a follow-up bead rather than expanding scope here.","events_attachment":"ddx-828a1b8f/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-828a1b8f","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:execution","area:specs","kind:doc","phase:iterate","spec:FEAT-010","spec:TD-010"],"priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-8bfa661c","spec-id":"FEAT-010","status":"closed","title":"specs: worktree resource-isolation expectations (build-cache reuse, I/O budgets) for ddx try/work","updated_at":"2026-05-30T06:16:21.596613826Z"}

Worker 1 ran ddx-69a9ecd3 to completion (closed, 1h11m) but its durable-audit commit was SIGKILLed and the worker exited \"operator attention required\", leaving .ddx/beads.jsonl, .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl, .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl, .ddx/attachments dirty. Memory was healthy (31Gi free) — NOT OOM. Error: `commit durable audit outputs: committing durable audit outputs: : signal: killed`.\n\nROOT CAUSE: cli/internal/agent/durable_audit.go:88 wraps the WHOLE commit body (status/add/diff/commit) in context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 30*time.Second); that ctx flows to exec.CommandContext (cli/internal/git/exec.go:38). While the git commit was blocked behind the concurrent worker's hold on the shared main-git lock (.ddx/.git-tracker.lock via withTrackerLock, tracker_lock.go:133-152) / .git/index.lock, the 30s deadline fired and Go sent SIGKILL to git -\u003e CombinedOutput() returns empty output + *exec.ExitError(\"signal: killed\") (durable_audit.go:125-127). The classifier isTransientGitContention (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1026-1041) matches index.lock / \"unable to write new index file\" / \"cannot lock ref\" / \"unable to update the ref\" but NOT \"signal: killed\"/context-deadline, so finalizeDurableAuditOrStop (execute_bead_loop.go:1311-1336) sets OperatorAttention and exits. The commit is ALREADY idempotent on re-entry (durable_audit.go:98-122), so a retry would succeed — only the misclassification-driven EXIT blocks the idempotent re-run.\n\nPROPOSED FIX: Make isTransientGitContention (execute_bead_loop.go:1026) a SUPERSET that returns true for: (a) errors.Is(err, context.DeadlineExceeded) and substrings \"signal: killed\" and \"context deadline exceeded\" (the SIGKILL/deadline class — defense-in-depth since the exec.ExitError from a ctx kill may not retrievably wrap context.DeadlineExceeded), AND (b) the \"tracker lock timeout (...)\" string from tracker_lock.go:283 (the lock-acquire-wait-exhausted class). This SUPERSEDES ddx-2189a07f (which adds only the tracker-lock-timeout string and is currently wedging on a routing issue) — folding both transient forms into one coherent classifier change avoids two beads racing to edit the same function. Keep all existing matches.\n\nNON-SCOPE: changing the 30s deadline value or decoupling it from the lock wait (that is the sibling bead B2); worktree-leak cleanup (ddx-274943fc); classifier call sites other than the durable-audit branch.\nIN-SCOPE FILES: cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (classifier), cli/internal/agent/durable_audit_test.go (extend the transient table ~lines 175-185 via runAuditWithFinalizeErr).","events_attachment":"ddx-83361480/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-83361480","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:execution","kind:bug","kind:reliability","phase:iterate","spec:FEAT-010","spec:TP-021","prevention:ddx-23ac2796","supersedes:ddx-2189a07f"],"parent":"ddx-3106674f","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-2bebdf9d","status":"closed","title":"execute-bead: classify SIGKILL/context-deadline durable-audit commit (signal: killed) + tracker-lock-timeout as transient, not operator-attention","updated_at":"2026-06-06T07:42:44.170267001Z"}

On the Fizeau project (sibling repo, /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau) on 2026-05-14:\n- /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/ contained 9,735 inodes across ~9700+ directories. Many were .execute-bead-wt-* worktrees dating back to 2026-05-07 (a week stale). No automatic GC.\n- .ddx/attachments/ holds 355 per-bead artifact directories. They flood `git status` as untracked since nothing gitignores them.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- `ddx try` creates a worktree under `/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-\u003cbead\u003e-\u003ctimestamp\u003e-\u003chash\u003e/` and only sometimes cleans up. Failure paths (worktree_lost, execution_failed, process crash) consistently leave the worktree behind. There's no startup reaper for stale worktrees.\n- `ddx work` and `ddx try` write a worker status.json under .ddx/workers/agent-loop-\u003cnano-id\u003e/ and the writer is responsible for cleanup. Process crashes leave the directory behind; no startup reaper claims them.\n- .ddx/executions/\u003ctimestamp\u003e/ is meant to be retained as evidence (per CONTRACT-003 / FEAT-010 evidence pile). But there's no retention policy: directories accumulate forever. Some land in unmerged git state after partial commits.\n- .ddx/attachments/ and .ddx/run-state*.json are written by every ddx invocation, but the project's .gitignore policy for them is inconsistent (.ddx/executions is ignored; .ddx/attachments and .ddx/run-state.json appear in `git status` constantly).\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n1. Add a startup reaper to `ddx work` and `ddx try`: on launch, scan /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/, identify worktrees whose parent ddx-try/ddx-work process is dead (no PID match), `git worktree remove` + `rm -rf` them. Bound the scan to N=200 per launch so a cold start doesn't take forever. Configurable via DDX_WORKTREE_REAP_MAX_AGE (default 72h).\n2. Add a startup reaper for `.ddx/workers/agent-loop-*/`: read status.json's `pid` and `last_activity_at`; if pid is gone OR activity \u003e 30 min stale, remove the directory.\n3. Add a configurable execution-evidence retention policy:\n...[truncated]\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nImplement only this child acceptance slice:\n- TestWorkStartup_ReapsStaleWorkerDirs asserts that .ddx/workers/agent-loop-*/ directories whose status.json shows pid=dead or last_activity_at \u003e 30 min stale are removed at startup.\n- TestExecutionsRetention_ArchivesOldEvidence asserts that .ddx/executions/\u003ctimestamp\u003e/ entries older than DDX_EXECUTION_RETAIN_DAYS are moved under .ddx/executions-archive/\u003cyear\u003e/\u003cmonth\u003e/ (or deleted if DDX_EXECUTION_RETAIN_DAYS=0).\n- `ddx doctor` reports the count of stale worktrees, worker dirs, and execution dirs without making changes. `ddx doctor --apply` runs the reaper paths.\n\nNON-SCOPE\nDo not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. .ddx/beads.jsonl:296:{"acceptance":"1. `TestDefaultVerificationCommandRunnerTimeoutKillsProcessGroup` proves a timed-out nested shell command (`sh -lc 'sleep ...'` or equivalent fixture) leaves no child/grandchild process alive after the runner returns.\n2. `TestDefaultVerificationCommandRunnerAllowsConfiguredLongGate` or equivalent proves the verification timeout is configurable/worker-derived and can run a command longer than 60s without forcing `no_changes_unverified`.\n3. `TestExecuteBeadWorkerNoChangesVerifiedLongCommandCloses` proves a `verification_command` that runs longer than the old 60s limit but exits 0 closes the bead as `already_satisfied` and emits `no_changes_verified`.\n4. `TestExecuteBeadWorkerNoChangesVerificationTimeoutKeepsOpenAndReaps` proves timeout keeps the bead open with `triage:no-changes-unverified`, records timeout evidence, and leaves no verification process tree behind.\n5. `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'Test(DefaultVerificationCommandRunner|ExecuteBeadWorkerNoChanges.*Verification)' -count=1` passes.\n6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","closing_commit_sha":"f609f22ef84a0d7b05ba89dd3e9fd5b92fbfa446","created_at":"2026-05-15T13:09:07.942514913Z","description":"PROBLEM\nA `no_changes_rationale.txt` with `verification_command:` can time out, leave its child process tree running, and let `ddx work --watch` move on to another bead while the old verification is still consuming the project. Live reproduction on 2026-05-15 in `/Users/erik/Projects/fizeau`: `fizeau-bc9586d7` returned `verification_command: sh -lc \"go test ... \u0026\u0026 lefthook run pre-commit\"`; the watcher logged `• fizeau-bc9586d7 → no_changes` and started `fizeau-7af3f548` at 13:07:21Z, while `ps` still showed orphaned PID 3510889 (`sh -lc go test ...`) and child PID 3512539 (`go test ./... -count=1`) under PPID 1.\n\nROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE\n`cli/internal/agent/try/no_changes.go:117-128` hard-codes `DefaultVerificationCommandTimeout = 60 * time.Second` and runs the command with `exec.CommandContext(cctx, \"/bin/sh\", \"-c\", command)`. `exec.CommandContext` kills only the direct shell on timeout; because workers commonly emit nested shell commands such as `sh -lc \"go test ...\"`, the actual test process survives as an orphan. The 60s timeout is also shorter than normal bead acceptance gates like `go test ./...` plus `lefthook run pre-commit`, so legitimate already-satisfied checks are classified as unverified/no-progress instead of closing the bead.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nRun no_changes verification commands with the same process-tree discipline used by agent executors: start the shell in its own process group on Unix, terminate the group on timeout/cancel, and wait/reap before returning. Replace the hard-coded 60s ceiling with a worker/config-derived timeout that is long enough for normal acceptance gates, or make it configurable with a conservative default. Preserve the contract: exit 0 closes as `already_satisfied`; non-zero/timeout keeps the bead open with clear `no_changes_unverified` evidence.\n\nNON-SCOPE\nDo not weaken no_changes verification semantics. Do not let verification run asynchronously. Do not close a bead without a passing command. Do not change readiness classification or route selection.","events_attachment":"ddx-8a0aea8e/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-8a0aea8e","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:work","kind:bug","operator-ux","downstream:fizeau","dirty-tree"],"priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-af6f8fc7","status":"closed","title":"work: reap and correctly budget no_changes verification commands","updated_at":"2026-05-15T15:59:18.191703305Z"}

This child owns part 2 of 3 of that parent scope.\n\nROOT CAUSE\nThe decomposition action (currently invoked when a bead is too large for one execution and the agent splits it into child beads) is writing the parent's ID as a dep edge on every child. This produces an unbreakable deadlock:\n\n  e29892a4 (parent, open) -- parent --\u003e child_A\n  e29892a4 -- parent --\u003e child_B\n  child_A.deps = [e29892a4]           \u003c- BAD: parent is also a dep\n  child_B.deps = [child_A, e29892a4]  \u003c- BAD\n\nThe parent stays open until its children close. Every child is blocked on the parent. Nothing can be claimed. ddx work loops on the parent, the orchestrator emits 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale', the worker retries, and burns claim budget without progress.\n\nObserved scenario (niflheim, 2026-06-04 04:30-05:13 UTC):\n- Parent: niflheim-e29892a4 ('feat(delta): async write-behind ...')\n- 10 children created during planning loops (23223661, a44a8ceb, 054c89b7, 0470ba0d, f10e9e35, f076b08c, 6c041162, 94e1df73, 9da62c2d, 5e4f9fb2)\n- Every child carried deps=[..siblings.., niflheim-e29892a4]\n- ~50 minutes of agent time + 4 strong-policy claude/opus attempts with zero feature commits; only tracker chores\n- Unwedged manually by closing the parent via 'ddx bead close', after which siblings drained correctly in foundational order\n\nRoot cause is structural, not model-capability — auto-upgrading to a stronger model does NOT fix this. .ddx/beads.jsonl:302:{"acceptance":"1. ddx classifies each outcome per the addendum table (table-driven test). 2. Exactly one middle-\u003emax escalation, only on typed genuine failure; no_changes/dirty/transport/quota/auth do NOT escalate. 3. Per-pool in-flight max-attempt cap enforced (test simulates concurrent genuine failures, asserts cap). 4. quota-exhausted -\u003e global pool cooldown, not N per-bead mutations (test). 5. cd /Users/erik/Projects/ddx/cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... green. 6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"65f0da163dbdd3845b28118fbc84fc0a965bfa2d","created_at":"2026-05-31T15:22:49.608697929Z","description":"Implement the ddx side of the FEAT-004 failure-classification table: ddx work maps each attempt outcome to exactly the action in the addendum table (success/already_satisfied=close; no_changes=close/unclaim NO tier-escalation; genuine impl/capability failure=escalate middle-\u003emax ONCE; dirty=clean+same-tier retry; transport=same-tier reroute; quota-exhausted=global pool cooldown; auth/setup=operator-attention; timeout=clean+retry then operator-attention; merge-conflict=unclaim+retry; max genuine failure=operator-attention). ddx owns the ONE middle-\u003emax tier escalation (per the escalation-owner decision bead fizeau-994604ec); a per-pool in-flight max-attempt cap prevents multi-worker stampede. Depends on the escalation-owner contract + the shared classifier (fizeau-44e98499).","events_attachment":"ddx-8d6af771/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-8d6af771","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:execute-bead","area:workers","kind:feature","spec:FEAT-004","phase:1","triage:no-changes-unjustified"],"notes":"SELF-CONTAINED SPEC (the ddx-side FEAT-004 here is Beads; the ROUTING policy lives in the fizeau repo, NOT accessible to a cold ddx agent — so the normative failure-classification table is inlined below; implement against THIS, do not look for a ddx FEAT-004 routing addendum):\n\nMap each ddx-work attempt outcome to exactly one action:\n- success / already_satisfied -\u003e close bead\n- no_changes (agent produced nothing + rationale) -\u003e close or unclaim per rationale; do NOT escalate tier\n- genuine implementation/capability failure (tests fail after real work; AC unmet) -\u003e escalate middle-\u003emax ONCE\n- dirty worktree / partial write -\u003e clean/reset worktree, same-tier retry (not escalation)\n- provider transport failure (i/o timeout, conn refused, 5xx) -\u003e same-tier reroute to another dispatchable candidate; no tier change\n- quota exhausted (retry_after) -\u003e GLOBAL pool cooldown/sleep until reset; NOT per-bead mutation, not escalation\n- auth/config missing, toolchain/setup failure -\u003e operator-attention (typed bead state); do not escalate\n- timeout / no-progress watchdog -\u003e clean + same-tier retry once, then operator-attention\n- merge/land conflict -\u003e unclaim + retry later; not escalation\n- max-tier genuine failure -\u003e operator-attention (stop escalating)\n\noperator-attention = explicit bead state transition (not a notification/indefinite wait). The single middle-\u003emax escalation owner is ddx work; a per-pool in-flight max-attempt cap prevents multi-worker stampede. Scope tests to avoid hangs (the prior attempt hit a 3m test timeout — bound any test that spawns/loops).","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-f5db2b63","spec-id":"FEAT-004","status":"closed","title":"routing P1: ddx applies shared failure-classification table; single-owner one-hop middle-\u003emax escalation","updated_at":"2026-05-31T20:49:57.479846861Z","work-no-changes-count":1}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:317:{"acceptance":"1. cli/internal/agent/work/stop.go defines StopCondition enum: Drained | Once | Signal | Budget | NoProgress | Blocked, and drain.stop(state) is the only production budget/terminal stop classifier.\n2. Cost-cap checks are removed from executor closure call sites and reviewer group cost is charged to the same budget accumulator before close eligibility is evaluated.\n3. No-progress retry budget is incremented only for valid implementation attempts; TestStopCondition_NoProgress_IgnoresIntakeRoutingReviewAndOperatorStates covers intake block, decomposition, claim race, routing preflight, quota/transport/auth/tool setup, review-error, and needs_human cases.\n4. Review REQUEST_CHANGES/BLOCK no longer reopens a closed bead. A review_fixable_gap prevents close, records review-fixable-gap, and schedules one bounded repair cycle with repair_context_from_review_group. TestPreCloseReviewFixableGap_PreventsCloseAndSchedulesRepair covers this.\n5. review_spec_gap, review_missing_acceptance, review_too_large at depth cap, review_unsafe_or_out_of_scope, and exhausted review_error transition to blocked with needs_human and do not consume no-progress budget. TestReviewTerminalClassifications_BlockWithoutNoProgress covers this.\n6. MinPower retry/escalation behavior from ddx-cfedee8e is wired in the attempt/retry path without mutating explicit harness/model/profile pins. TestRetryPolicy_ReviewFixableGapRaisesMinPowerAndPreservesPins covers the path.\n7. Execution records include append-only cycle trace data for initial and repair cycles: cycle_index, attempt_id, result_rev, implementer route facts, review_group_id, reviewer_index values, review verdicts, and final decision. TestExecutionTrace_RecordsInitialAndRepairCycles covers both cycles.\n8. Downstream-consumer audit for cost-cap and stop-condition event names is committed to .ddx/executions/\u003crun-id\u003e/cost-cap-consumers.md.\n9. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... green.\n10. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","created_at":"2026-05-02T20:02:28.321106862Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-9228a484","depends_on_id":"ddx-848069a3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-02T20:02:28Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-9228a484","depends_on_id":"ddx-a8718bec","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-02T20:02:28Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-9228a484","depends_on_id":"ddx-dc157075","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-02T20:02:28Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-9228a484","depends_on_id":"ddx-09d2990c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-02T20:15:17Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-9228a484","depends_on_id":"ddx-99a3be32","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-06T15:17:58Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-9228a484","depends_on_id":"ddx-7e1fed08","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-07T16:13:58Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM\nThe execution loop now needs a single durable stop/retry policy for the reliable factory workflow. Current code still exposes older post-merge review/reopen semantics and scattered stop logic: ExecuteBeadReport.ReviewVerdict is documented as post-merge in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:226, Reopen is documented for REQUEST_CHANGES/BLOCK at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:300, no-progress suppression only excludes a narrow transient set at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1576, and C9 previously depended on ddx-cfedee8e even though C9 absorbs that scope.\n\nROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE\n- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1722 and cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1818 contain historical cost-cap stop sites outside a first-class StopCondition gate.\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:226 and cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:300 still describe post-merge reviewer close/reopen behavior, which conflicts with ADR-024 default adversarial pre-close review.\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1576 counts no-progress by base/result equality but only excludes transport/quota/routing/timeout/merge_conflict outcome reasons; intake blocks, decomposition, review errors, and operator-required states must not consume no-progress retry budget.\n- ddx-9228a484 previously listed ddx-cfedee8e as a dependency while also absorbing it, creating a queue deadlock.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n- Implement cli/internal/agent/work/stop.go with StopCondition values Drained, Once, Signal, Budget, NoProgress, Blocked.\n- Move all budget/cost-cap decisions, including implementer plus adversarial reviewer group cost, into drain.stop(state).\n- Relocate MinPower retry behavior from ddx-cfedee8e into the attempt/retry path without mutating requested harness/model/profile pins. A review_fixable_gap may raise MinPower for the repair attempt; spec gaps, missing acceptance, too-large, unsafe/out-of-scope, review errors beyond cap, intake ambiguity, and decomposition overflow block for the operator instead.\n- Make no-progress budget count only valid implementation attempts where checkout/context existed and the implementer had an opportunity to modify or verify code. Exclude intake rewrites/blocks/decomposition, claim races, routing preflight, quota/transport/auth/tool setup, review errors, and operator-action states.\n- Keep append-only cycle trace fields for every implementation cycle and repair cycle: cycle_index, attempt_id, result_rev, implementer route facts, review_group_id, reviewer slots, review result, final decision.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Implementing the adversarial review gate itself; that is tracked separately.\n- Frontend display of stop reasons.\n- Poll-interval defaults and idle workers; already handled by ddx-dc157075 / C10.","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{\"resolved_provider\":\"codex\",\"resolved_model\":\"gpt-5.4-mini\",\"fallback_chain\":[],\"actual_power\":8}","created_at":"2026-05-06T09:57:33.739548542Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=codex model=gpt-5.4-mini"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{\"attempt_id\":\"20260506T095557-abbe996b\",\"harness\":\"codex\",\"model\":\"gpt-5.4-mini\",\"input_tokens\":1639001,\"output_tokens\":9876,\"total_tokens\":1648877,\"cost_usd\":0,\"duration_ms\":93966,\"exit_code\":0}","created_at":"2026-05-06T09:57:33.982549382Z","kind":"cost","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"tokens=1648877 model=gpt-5.4-mini"},{"actor":"erik","body":"This bead still spans multiple distinct implementation slices that are not safely landable as one execute-pass at the current decomposition depth: stop-condition classification and cost-cap routing, no-progress accounting, pre-close review repair-cycle orchestration, execution trace schema, and the associated regression tests. .ddx/beads.jsonl:320:{"acceptance":"1. `ddx install \u003cname\u003e --global --silent` installs the plugin to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/\u003cname\u003e/ and creates a symlink or copy under ~/.claude/skills/\u003cname\u003e and ~/.agents/skills/\u003cname\u003e.\n2. `ddx install \u003cname\u003e` (no --global, in-tree mode) installs to \u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/plugins/\u003cname\u003e/ and creates project-tier links under \u003cproject\u003e/.claude/skills/\u003cname\u003e and \u003cproject\u003e/.agents/skills/\u003cname\u003e.\n3. `ddx install \u003cname\u003e` (no --global, convention mode) installs to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/\u003chost\u003e/\u003cowner\u003e/\u003crepo\u003e/plugins/\u003cname\u003e/ and creates project-tier links pointing into that XDG plugins/ path.\n4. TestPluginLookup_PrefersProjectOverGlobal asserts that when both paths have the plugin, the project copy wins.\n5. TestPluginLookup_FallsBackToGlobal asserts that when the project copy is missing, the global copy is used.\n6. TestInitGlobal_CreatesAgentTierLinks asserts ~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx point into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/.\n7. TestInitProject_DoesNotInstallPlugins asserts `ddx init` (in-project) does not write to plugins/ — plugins lazy-resolve from global.\n8. TestUpdateGlobal_UpdatesGlobalNotProject asserts `ddx update --global` updates only the global plugin tree.\n9. fc","depends_on_id":"ddx-6a23cbd3","type":"depends_on","created_at":"2026-06-03T21:49:46Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-937de9fc","depends_on_id":"ddx-fab52595","type":"depends_on","created_at":"2026-06-03T23:07:18Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-937de9fc","depends_on_id":"ddx-b418c28c","type":"depends_on","created_at":"2026-06-03T23:07:18Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-937de9fc","depends_on_id":"ddx-c0292631","type":"depends_on","created_at":"2026-06-03T23:07:18Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-937de9fc","depends_on_id":"ddx-7e585c0a","type":"depends_on","created_at":"2026-06-03T23:07:18Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)\n\nSkills, personas, and plugin content need to install in two distinct locations depending on operator intent:\n\n- **install-to-agent (global)**: written under the user's agent-tier paths (e.g. ~/.claude/skills/\u003cname\u003e, ~/.codex/skills/\u003cname\u003e, or the equivalent each harness reads). Skills and personas are then available across every project on the machine without per-project setup. Lives under ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/\u003cname\u003e/ with symlinks/copies into the agent-tier paths.\n- **install-in-project**: written under ddxroot.Path()/plugins/\u003cname\u003e/. In in-tree mode that is \u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/plugins/\u003cname\u003e/; in convention mode that is ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/\u003chost\u003e/\u003cowner\u003e/\u003crepo\u003e/plugins/\u003cname\u003e/. Either way the project-scoped agent paths point into it (e.g. \u003cproject\u003e/.claude/skills/ link target).\n\nToday `ddx install`, `ddx update`, `ddx plugin list`, and `ddx init` all assume per-project installs only. There is no `--global` surface. Operators who want a single Claude Code install per machine (the common case) must repeat the install for every project.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- cli/cmd/install.go and cli/cmd/update.go target ddxroot.Path() unconditionally.\n- cli/cmd/init.go bundles plugin install with manifest creation.\n- The agent-tier paths (~/.claude/skills/...) are written only when the operator manually links them, or via the legacy install paths that were retired (per the user's `feedback_no_local_replace` and the home-directory-install retirement noted in CLAUDE.md).\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n\n1. Add `--global` flag to `ddx install`, `ddx update`, `ddx plugin list`, `ddx plugin show`. When set:\n   - Resolve target: ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/\u003cname\u003e/.\n   - Write the plugin content there.\n   - Create symlinks (or copies, OS-dependent) into the agent-tier skill paths the user's agents read: ~/.claude/skills/\u003cname\u003e, ~/.codex/skills/\u003cname\u003e, and any others the agent registry knows about.\n   - Skill discovery from a fresh shell finds the skill via the agent-tier path.\n\n2. Without `--global`, `ddx install` writes to ddxroot.Path()/plugins/\u003cname\u003e/ and links into \u003cproject\u003e/.claude/skills/\u003cname\u003e AND \u003cproject\u003e/.agents/skills/\u003cname\u003e. In convention mode (no \u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/) the project's agent-tier paths still point into the XDG project dir's plugins. In in-tree mode they point into \u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/plugins/.\n\n3. `ddx init --global` is the umbrella command for first-time setup: installs the default ddx plugin globally, creates agent-tier links, and writes ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml with convention defaults.\n\n4. `ddx init` (no flag) creates \u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/ and does NOT install plugins — plugins are lazy-resolved on first use, preferring the project install over global.\n\n5. Plugin lookup precedence at runtime:\n   - \u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/plugins/\u003cname\u003e/ (or ddxroot.Path()/plugins/\u003cname\u003e/ in convention mode) if present.\n   - ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/\u003cname\u003e/ otherwise.\n   - Hardcoded baked-in fallback (the default ddx plugin only).\n\n6. The \"ddx-validate\" pre-commit hook (per ddx-777f4a09 already landed) must continue to scope its checks correctly: skill-related validation runs only when skill files are staged, regardless of install location.\n\n7. `ddx doctor` reports both install layers: \"Global Install (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx) — ok | missing\" and \"Project Install (ddxroot.Path()/plugins/ddx) — ok | missing | lazy-resolves-to-global\".\n\nSPEC IMPACT (in addition to ddx-06cbaa90's spec updates):\n- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-025-or-equivalent (skill install topology) — file or update.\n- library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md \"How this skill works\" section — document install locations.\n- library/skills/ddx/reference/agents.md (if exists) — document agent-tier paths.\n- CLAUDE.md \"Persona System\" section — document install precedence.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Removing the existing per-project install entirely (both modes coexist).\n- Migrating ~/.config/ddx/ legacy paths if any exist (likely none today).\n- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex (add others as those harnesses define their skill paths).\n\nDEPS\nddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — install paths route through ddxroot.Path() AND the new ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/ path. Both must be agreed-on before this lands.","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{\"resolved_provider\":\"claude\",\"resolved_model\":\"opus\",\"fallback_chain\":[]}","created_at":"2026-06-02T01:48:17.508736688Z","kind":"routing","source":"legacy .ddx/beads.jsonl:336:{"acceptance":"1. TestPluginLookup_PrefersProjectOverGlobal (cli/internal/registry/resolve_test.go) asserts that when both the project plugins/\u003cname\u003e dir and ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/\u003cname\u003e exist, ResolvePlugin returns the project path with layer==\"project\".\n2. TestPluginLookup_FallsBackToGlobal (cli/internal/registry/resolve_test.go) asserts that when the project copy is absent but the global copy exists, ResolvePlugin returns the global path with layer==\"global\".\n3. A test asserts ResolvePlugin returns layer==\"baked-in\" with empty path for name==\"ddx\" when neither project nor global copy exists, and a non-nil error for any other unresolved name.\n4. The skill-install-topology ADR file exists and documents the project→global→baked-in precedence; the \"Persona System\" precedence text in CLAUDE.md and cli/cmd/CLAUDE.md matches it.\n5. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./cmd/... ./internal/registry/... green.\n6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"9a1fd9da9ca03fdf83540ce34b5890966824d755","created_at":"2026-06-03T12:37:10.32456447Z","description":"PROBLEM (epic ddx-602ae31a, Principle 2 — \"Platform Services in CLI, Opinions in Workflows\")\n\nSkills/personas/plugins can live in two install layers (project-local and machine-global). Runtime resolution must deterministically prefer the project copy, fall back to the global copy, and only then use the baked-in default ddx plugin. This resolution function is the foundation every install/update/doctor surface depends on, so it is split out first.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- The resolution helper lives at cli/internal/registry/resolve.go:29 `ResolvePlugin(ctx, projectRoot, name)`. It must return path + layer (\"project\"|\"global\"|\"baked-in\") with the documented precedence and a non-nil error for any other unresolved name. The project path is computed via `ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot)` (resolve.go:30) which already handles in-tree vs convention mode; the global path via `ddxroot.GlobalDir()` (resolve.go:35). Baked-in is valid only for name==\"ddx\" (resolve.go:40).\n- The governing decision (skill install topology) and the precedence documentation in CLAUDE.md must match this behavior.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n1. Ensure cli/internal/registry/resolve.go:29 `ResolvePlugin` implements precedence project → global → baked-in exactly, returning empty path for the baked-in layer (callers use defaultplugin.FS()).\n2. Land/refresh the skill-install-topology ADR (docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-027-skill-install-topology.md or the equivalent ADR referenced by CLAUDE.md) describing the three-layer precedence.\n3. Confirm the \"Persona System\" sections of CLAUDE.md and cli/cmd/CLAUDE.md document the precedence (project-local → ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx → baked-in default).\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- The `--global` install/update CLI surface (sibling child).\n- init/doctor wiring (sibling child).\n- Removing the existing per-project install entirely (both modes coexist).\n- Migrating any ~/.config/ddx/ legacy paths.\n- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.\n\nDEPS\n- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — `ResolvePlugin` routes through `ddxroot.Path()` and `ddxroot.GlobalDir()`; both must be agreed-on before this lands.","events_attachment":"ddx-9ae1192f/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-9ae1192f","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:plugin","area:config","kind:feature","phase:3","initiative:zero-footprint","principle:skills-personas"],"parent":"ddx-937de9fc","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-c6148486","status":"closed","title":"registry: plugin lookup precedence project→global→baked-in + skill-install-topology spec","updated_at":"2026-06-03T12:54:00.440122017Z"}

.ddx/attachments/ddx-6ff597ca/events.jsonl:14:{"actor":"ddx","body":"post-merge review: REQUEST_CHANGES\n**AC 7 — command path**: The command is registered as `ddx doc audit` (`cli/cmd/doc.go:59`), but the acceptance criteria specifies `ddx docs audit` (or `ddx doctor docs`). If `doc` is the existing noun, this is arguably fine, but the AC should be reconciled. The `Aliases: []string{\"integrity\"}` alias doesn't cover the spec'd names.\n**AC 7 — `--json` exit code**: `cli/cmd/doc.go:79-83` — when `--json` is set, the command encodes issues and returns `nil` unconditionally. The AC requires exit 1 when issues are present regardless of output format, so CI pipelines using `ddx doc audit --json` would incorrectly report success on a broken graph. The test `TestDocAuditCommand_JSONOutput` even documents this as intentional (\"--json always succeeds (exit 0) so piping into jq works\"), which contradicts the AC. Either the AC should be amended or the `--json` path should also return `ExitError` when issues exist (with a `--exit-zero` escape hatch for pipe-friendly use).\nresult_rev=61a8029103abff2e64442ff7b81ab92d04d84f69\nbase_rev=48f9750c2e18e96af665670c653a8f4f72e27ebf","created_at":"2026-04-23T02:38:22.039899928Z","kind":"execute-bead","source":"ddx agent work","summary":"review_request_changes"}

Commit to copy-at-build approach.\n\nSteps:\n1. Add Makefile target copy-skills in cli/Makefile that rsyncs skills/ddx/ into cli/internal/skills/ddx/ before every build. Example:\n     copy-skills:\n         rsync -a --delete ../skills/ddx/ ./internal/skills/ddx/\n2. Wire copy-skills as a prerequisite of make build, make test, make dev (and any other targets that invoke go build or go test).\n3. Update cli/internal/skills/embed.go to replace the current multi-dir embed line with: //go:embed all:ddx\n4. Delete cli/internal/skills/ddx-{bead,agent,doctor,install,status,review,run}/ — the new embed picks up the copied ddx/ tree instead. (Deletion of top-level /skills/ddx-* is Phase 2; this bead only touches cli/internal/skills/.)\n5. Commit cli/internal/skills/ddx/ as a tracked copy so contributors who run go build directly (without make) still get a valid binary. Add a pre-commit hook (lefthook.yml) that re-runs make copy-skills if skills/ddx/ is dirty and cli/internal/skills/ddx/ is stale; this keeps the copy in sync without manual intervention.\n\nIn-scope files:\n- cli/Makefile\n- cli/internal/skills/embed.go\n- cli/internal/skills/ddx/ (committed copy — gets populated by copy-skills)\n- lefthook.yml (add pre-commit hook)\n\nOut-of-scope:\n- skills/ddx/SKILL.md and reference files — separate beads\n- ddx init / ddx update changes — separate beads\n- Old /skills/ddx-* (top-level) deletion — Phase 2\n\nPrerequisite: skills/ddx/SKILL.md + reference/*.md exist before make copy-skills runs, else the rsync copies an empty dir.","id":"ddx-086fc990","issue_type":"task","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:implementation","area:cli","area:build"],"owner":"erik","parent":"ddx-f586b4fd","priority":1,"schema_version":1,"spec-id":"FEAT-011","status":"closed","title":"Wire Makefile copy-skills target and update embed.go for single ddx skill","updated_at":"2026-04-17T22:25:06.598409573Z","work-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-17T22:23:14.204652504Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:121:{"acceptance":"1. `TestExecuteBeadRejectsCommitPlusNoChangesRationale` or equivalent proves an attempt with a synthesized implementation commit and `.ddx/executions/\u003cattempt\u003e/no_changes_rationale.txt` does not return `status=success`, `outcome=merged`, or close the bead.\n2. `TestExecuteBeadMixedCommitAndBlockedNoChangesRationalePreservesEvidence` or equivalent proves a mixed result containing `status: blocked` preserves the rationale text in result/evidence and exposes a clear `mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale`-style detail.\n3. Existing legitimate zero-diff no_changes coverage still passes, including `TestNoChangesRationaleWithVerificationCommandClosesAlreadySatisfied` or the local equivalent for verification_command behavior.\n4. Existing normal commit success coverage still passes when no `no_changes_rationale.txt` is present.\n5. `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... -run \"TestExecuteBead.*NoChanges|TestNoChanges.*|Test.*Mixed.*NoChanges\" -count=1` passes.\n6. `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/...` passes or any pre-existing unrelated failure is recorded in the bead evidence without being converted to success.\n7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","archived_at":"2026-05-15T00:08:56Z","closing_commit_sha":"694426cc7e302ed91531fbf6a082c6b7c5a0d21c","created_at":"2026-05-12T05:23:44.206444045Z","description":"PROBLEM\nA DDx execute-bead attempt can produce both a substantive implementation commit and `.ddx/executions/\u003cattempt\u003e/no_changes_rationale.txt`, and DDx still records the attempt as `success`/`merged`. Live evidence: attempt `20260512T050728-bb18a61f` for `ddx-ef9e8aec` landed commit `15772fc85`, merged evidence commit `9e1ab8506`, and closed the bead, while `.ddx/executions/20260512T050728-bb18a61f/no_changes_rationale.txt` says `status: blocked` because the required `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/...` gate timed out/failed. That turns a self-reported blocked verification into a successful close.\n\nROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1413-1428` classifies `task_succeeded` solely from `resultRev != baseRev`; it only reads `no_changes_rationale.txt` when `resultRev == baseRev`.\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:615-616` deliberately excludes `.ddx/executions/*/no_changes_rationale.txt` from synthesized code commits, so the file can coexist with a synthesized implementation commit without affecting `resultRev`.\n- `cli/internal/agent/try/no_changes.go:56-90` already parses structured `status: blocked|open|proposed` no_changes rationales, but that parser is bypassed when any implementation commit exists.\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1360-1407` treats the returned report as authoritative; by the time the loop sees the result, the contradictory no_changes signal has been lost behind a success/merged outcome.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n- Make execute-bead treat an implementation diff plus `no_changes_rationale.txt` as a non-successful mixed-signal result before close/merge success is reported. A worker must choose one lane: commit green implementation, or no_changes/status rationale.\n- Preserve the rationale text in evidence and surface a clear failure/detail such as `mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale` so the worker can retry or a follow-up bead can handle the stated blocker.\n- Ensure `status: blocked` in the rationale cannot close a bead as success when a commit also exists.\n- Keep legitimate `no_changes` behavior unchanged for zero-diff attempts and keep ordinary green implementation commits unchanged when no rationale file exists.\n\nIN-SCOPE FILES\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go`\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_*test.go` or `cli/internal/agent/try/*test.go` as needed for focused coverage\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Do not rewrite the already-landed `ddx-ef9e8aec` history.\n- Do not change Fizeau routing/model/provider selection.\n- Do not weaken legitimate already-satisfied no_changes verification.\n\nPARENT/DEPS\nParent: ddx-cc0641a7. No deps.","events_attachment":"ddx-0e46a2f5/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-0e46a2f5","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:bead-lifecycle","kind:bug","phase:2","prevention","spec:FEAT-010","td:031"],"parent":"ddx-cc0641a7","priority":1,"schema_version":1,"spec-id":"FEAT-010","status":"closed","title":"agent: reject commit plus no_changes rationale mixed results","updated_at":"2026-05-12T13:19:57.035027062Z"}

TestIntake_ActionableAtomic_ClaimsNormally verifies actionable_atomic proceeds to Claim and implementation.\n3. TestIntake_InfrastructureErrorFailsOpen verifies intake_error from hook infrastructure records a warning event and does not count as implementation no-progress.\n4. TestIntake_HookRunsBeforeClaim verifies the hook fires before Store.Claim and after route preflight.\n5. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... -run \"TestIntake_.*(ActionableAtomic|InfrastructureError|HookRunsBeforeClaim)\" -count=1 passes.\n6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","archived_at":"2026-05-15T00:08:56Z","closing_commit_sha":"fb2578c6f0451e7190ba4923c5fddfdcf28e043b","created_at":"2026-05-06T03:29:31.72689253Z","description":"PROBLEM\nThe intake parent bead ddx-f3bbcfce was too broad and bounced because it combined hook contracts, safe rewrites, decomposition, AC maps, depth overflow, and no-progress policy. The first executable slice is the typed pre-claim intake hook contract and loop insertion point.\n\nROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:680 invokes PreDispatchLintHook before claim, but there is no typed PreClaimIntakeHook with the finalized intake outcomes.\n- cli/internal/agent/lint_hook.go:86 constructs lint-only behavior, not actionability/decomposition decisions.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n- Add PreClaimIntakeHook (or equivalent guard) to ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime.\n- Define typed outcomes actionable_atomic, actionable_but_rewritten, too_large_decomposed, ambiguous_needs_human, and intake_error.\n- Invoke the hook after ready selection/routing preflight and before Claim.\n- Implement actionable_atomic pass-through and intake_error fail-open for infrastructure failures only.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Safe rewrite mutation.\n- Child bead creation/decomposition.\n- No-progress accounting changes beyond not counting intake_error as implementation no-progress.","events_attachment":"ddx-12638823/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-12638823","issue_type":"task","labels":["adr:023","adr:024","area:agent","from:ddx-f3bbcfce","kind:feature","phase:2","reconciled:no-changes-state","reliability","story:10"],"parent":"ddx-a9d130d0","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-b939bd40","spec_id":"ADR-023","status":"closed","title":"intake: add typed pre-claim actionability hook","updated_at":"2026-05-07T05:58:24.33987307Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:184:{"acceptance":"1. `TestPostAttemptTriageHook_MissingSkillErrorIncludesProjectRootPathsAndRemediation` verifies the missing-skill error includes the resolved project root, both checked nested skill paths, `ddx update --force`, and `ddx doctor`.\n2. `TestRunPostAttemptTriage_MissingSkillStillFailOpen` verifies a missing lifecycle skill logs the improved diagnostic but returns the original `ExecuteBeadReport` unchanged.\n3. `TestHasBeadLifecycleSkillDiagnostics_FindsAgentsOrClaudePath` verifies the diagnostic helper reports success when either `.agents/.../bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md` or `.claude/.../bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md` exists.\n4. `TestPreDispatchLintHook_MissingSkillUsesSameDiagnostic` verifies lint/readiness missing-skill errors use the same checked-path/remediation wording or shared error type.\n5. `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'Test(PostAttemptTriageHook_MissingSkill|RunPostAttemptTriage_MissingSkill|HasBeadLifecycleSkillDiagnostics|PreDispatchLintHook_MissingSkill)'` green.\n6. `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/...` green.\n7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","archived_at":"2026-05-15T00:08:56Z","closing_commit_sha":"8a181b4c942f8d4a3b82144be191599c467ed1e6","created_at":"2026-05-06T22:45:03.194529263Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-1c3a78f2","depends_on_id":"ddx-11f0cbf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-07T22:55:43Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM\nWhen the post-attempt triage hook cannot find the nested `bead-lifecycle` skill, the operator sees `post-attempt triage error (\u003cbead\u003e execution_failed): triage hook: skill missing: bead-lifecycle (continuing)`. The message is technically true but does not say which project root was inspected, which paths were checked, or what command remediates the install.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n`cli/internal/agent/triage_hook.go:90-92` returns `triage hook: skill missing: bead-lifecycle` when `hasBeadLifecycleSkill(projectRoot)` is false. `cli/internal/agent/lint_hook.go:218-227` hard-codes the checked paths but returns only a boolean, so callers cannot include the checked path list in diagnostics. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:175-179` logs the hook error verbatim and appends `(continuing)`, which confirms fail-open behavior but hides the actionable remediation.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n- Replace the boolean-only missing-skill path with a diagnostic helper that returns whether the lifecycle skill exists, the resolved project root, and the exact checked paths.\n- Update post-attempt triage errors to say the hook was skipped/degraded because `bead-lifecycle` was missing under the project root.\n- Include the checked paths `.agents/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md` and `.claude/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md` in the error text.\n- Include remediation in the hook-time error: run `ddx update --force`, then `ddx doctor`.\n- Preserve fail-open behavior: missing lifecycle skill must not change the underlying attempt status or close/park behavior by itself.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Do not run full `ddx doctor` from inside the hook.\n- Do not dispatch the lifecycle hook through a fallback global skill path.\n- Do not change lint/readiness/triage JSON output contracts.\n- Do not change retry or cooldown policy in this bead.\n\nPARENT + DEPS\nParent: ddx-a9d130d0. No deps.","events_attachment":"ddx-1c3a78f2/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-1c3a78f2","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:agent","area:cli","area:skills","bead-quality","kind:bug","operator-ux","phase:2","reliability","spec:FEAT-010","spec:FEAT-015"],"lifecycle-migrated-at":"2026-05-09T13:09:06Z","lifecycle-migrated-from-status":"closed","lifecycle-migrated-to-status":"closed","notes":"Unblocked 2026-05-08: stale needs_human marker came from full-suite blockers outside this bead. The result.json for the cheap-tier attempt records status != \"execution_failed\"\n   when the live provider accepts the request (status may be success, no_changes,\n   or a non-dispatch failure downstream, but must NOT be \"did not match any\n   provider, catalog ref, or model-route\" and must NOT be \"harness lmstudio\n   not available: not found in PATH\").\n3. New unit / integration test in the execute-bead routing package asserts:\n   given a catalog entry cheap.embedded-openai: qwen3.5-27b and a discovered\n   provider advertising qwen3.5-27b, the resolver returns that provider.\n4. New unit / integration test asserts: the lmstudio harness is treated as\n   HTTP-probeable, not PATH-probeable, so it survives harness availability\n   checks when the live-discovery path sees an LM Studio provider.\n5. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./... \u0026\u0026 go build .\n6. ddx agent doctor and ddx agent check exit 0 on a host with live LM Studio\n   providers.","archived_at":"2026-05-07T04:29:14Z","created_at":"2026-04-17T02:49:32.207204061Z","description":"Observed on 2026-04-17T02:39 during an work --once cycle against bead\nhelix-ede8085a (from /Users/erik/Projects/helix). Evidence preserved at\n/Users/erik/Projects/helix/.ddx/executions/20260417T023917-dc4a49d3/result.json\nand /Users/erik/Projects/helix/.ddx/executions/20260417T023921-e66e9375/result.json.\n\nddx agent check --verbose on this host reports live LM Studio providers:\nbragi (10 models), grendel (3), vidar (11), vidar-coder (11), openrouter (345).\nSo model discovery IS working — the gap is in execute-bead's catalog→provider\nresolution.\n\nSymptom 1 — cheap tier / embedded-openai surface\n  Routing decision: tier=cheap harness=agent model=qwen3.5-27b\n  Failure: \"model \\\"qwen3.5-27b\\\" did not match any provider, catalog ref, or\n  model-route; specify --provider or --model-ref explicitly\"\n  The user's ~/.ddx/model-catalog.yaml maps cheap.embedded-openai → qwen3.5-27b,\n  but the embedded ddx-agent harness has no provider registration that serves\n  qwen3.5-27b. Meanwhile bragi / grendel / vidar all expose lmstudio-backed\n  models and could plausibly serve it — but execute-bead never consults live\n  provider discovery when a catalog model-ref fails to resolve.\n\nSymptom 2 — lmstudio harness fallback\n  Routing decision: harness=lmstudio model=minimax/minimax-m2.7\n  Failure: \"agent: harness lmstudio not available:  not found in PATH\"\n  lmstudio is an HTTP harness (ddx agent doctor reports it as \"(http)\" and OK),\n  yet the execute-bead harness probe path-checks it like a binary CLI and\n  rejects it before any HTTP probe runs.\n\nNet effect: the auto-escalation ladder skips cheap and standard tiers entirely\non this machine and lands on smart-tier codex/gpt-5.4, defeating the\ncost-tiered routing the parent epic (ddx-4b59b816) is designed to enable.\nThe operator should not have to pass --provider vidar or --harness codex to\nroute around this — that re-introduces the hyper-specification this epic is\ntrying to eliminate.\n\nRoot-cause candidates (pick one, not both):\n  a) Extend execute-bead catalog resolution so that when a catalog surface\n     maps to a model-ref the local harness cannot serve, it consults live\n     provider discovery (the same discovery ddx agent check uses) to locate\n     a reachable provider that advertises the model, and routes there.\n  b) Teach ddx agent execute-bead that the lmstudio harness is HTTP-backed\n     and probe it via the live-discovery path instead of PATH lookup, so the\n     cheap-tier fallback from (qwen3.5-27b) → (minimax/minimax-m2.7 via\n     lmstudio) actually reaches the bragi/grendel/vidar endpoints.\n\nBoth symptoms share a root: execute-bead's harness/model resolution has no\nlive-discovery hook. A fix should close that gap.","id":"ddx-1cfce460","issue_type":"bug","labels":["phase:build","area:agent","kind:routing","goal:throughput-per-dollar"],"parent":"ddx-4b59b816","priority":1,"schema_version":1,"status":"closed","title":"execute-bead cheap tier cannot resolve catalog models to live LM Studio providers","updated_at":"2026-04-17T02:58:29.941739396Z"}
