TestStopCondition_NoProgress_IgnoresIntakeRoutingReviewAndOperatorStates covers intake block, decomposition, claim race, routing preflight, quota, transport, auth/tool setup, review-error, and needs_human/operator-required cases.\n3. TestStopCondition_NoProgress_CountsRealImplementationNoCommit covers a valid implementation attempt where base_rev == result_rev and no system/operator classifier applies.\n4. Existing TestSuppressNoProgress_HonorsTransientReasons, TestNoChangesWithoutRationaleDoesNotUseNoProgressCooldown, and TestLoop_GenuineNoProgress_NoDefaultCooldown still pass.\n5. No-progress decisions surface StopCondition NoProgress in the work-layer test path without changing existing durable work-retry-after metadata keys.\n6. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... -run \"TestStopCondition_NoProgress|TestSuppressNoProgress|TestNoChangesWithoutRationaleDoesNotUseNoProgressCooldown|TestLoop_GenuineNoProgress\" -count=1 passes.\n7. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... passes.\n8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","archived_at":"2026-05-15T00:08:56Z","closing_commit_sha":"40fb7bcd7005f50d7ba42ce0b3c9fa72daf3f649","created_at":"2026-05-07T16:11:14.118505625Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-224ffcd2","depends_on_id":"ddx-89ab3fda","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-07T16:12:35Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM\nThe drain loop still treats no-progress mostly as base_rev == result_rev after a failed implementation attempt. That is too broad now that ddx work has pre-claim intake, pre-dispatch lint, routing preflight, review malfunctions, operator parks, and other non-implementation outcomes. Those states should not consume no-progress retry budget or trigger long cooldowns.\n\nROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1964-1978 implements shouldSuppressNoProgress with only three checks: disrupted, transient outcome reason, and base/result equality.\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1980-1987 only treats transport, quota, routing, timeout, merge_conflict, lock contention, and no viable provider as transient; it omits intake blocks, decomposition, claim races, routing preflight, auth/tool setup, review errors, and needs_human/operator states.\n- cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go:230-249 increments no_changes metadata before the work layer has a reusable valid-implementation-attempt classifier.\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1199-1215 and cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1262-1275 apply no-progress cooldowns from separate branches instead of a shared eligibility decision.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n- Add a reusable no-progress eligibility helper, either in cli/internal/agent/try or cli/internal/agent/work, that answers whether the implementer had a real opportunity to inspect/modify/verify code.\n- Count no-progress only for valid implementation attempts with checkout/context established and an implementer result available.\n- Exclude intake rewrites/blocks/decomposition, claim races, routing preflight, quota, transport, auth/tool setup, review errors, needs_human/operator-required states, and other system failures.\n- Route eligible no-progress stops through the StopCondition NoProgress vocabulary from ddx-89ab3fda.\n- Preserve existing behavior for genuine no-commit implementation attempts.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Do not change review verdict taxonomy beyond marking review/system outcomes no-progress-ineligible.\n- Do not schedule repair cycles.\n- Do not alter max-cost handling.\n\nDEPS\nDepends on ddx-89ab3fda for the NoProgress StopCondition vocabulary.","events_attachment":"ddx-224ffcd2/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-224ffcd2","issue_type":"task","labels":["adr:024","area:agent","area:try","area:work","kind:fix","phase:2","reliability","spec:FEAT-010","split-from:ddx-9228a484","story:10"],"parent":"ddx-5cb6e6cd","priority":1,"schema_version":1,"spec-id":"FEAT-010","split-from":"ddx-9228a484","status":"closed","title":"try: count no-progress only for valid implementation attempts","updated_at":"2026-05-07T18:31:57.184074831Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:214:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As an operator, I want a single view that shows every place my agents can run — endpoint providers and subprocess harnesses alike — with status, recent token usage, and quota utilization. When I click into one, I see a 7-day trend so I can tell if I'm about to run out.\n\n**Acceptance criteria:**\n\n1. **Deliverable 1 — Unified view.**\n   - New `harnessStatuses` GraphQL resolver returns subprocess harnesses (claude, codex, gemini, agent) with: name, kind=`harness`, reachable, detail, lastCheckedAt, defaultForProfile.\n   - Existing `providerStatuses` shape gains `kind=\\\"endpoint\\\"` and `lastCheckedAt`.\n   - UI page loads within 500ms on a warm cache — it renders from last-known status **first**, then patches rows live as async probes return. Playwright asserts the table is interactive within 500ms of navigation and that row status patches within 5s of the probe completing.\n   - Subprocess harnesses claude and codex **appear in the table** on a machine where their binaries are installed, with correct reachable status. Integration test.\n\n2. **Deliverable 2 — Usage + quota fields (stubbable).**\n   - Per-row fields `usage` and `quota` as described. All fields nullable.\n   - For endpoint providers, `usage.tokensUsedLast24h` and `usage.tokensUsedLastHour` are computed from the sessions index (fixture: ≥1k session rows covering the last 24h). Unit test asserts correctness on a seeded window.\n   - For subprocess harnesses that expose rate-limit headers (claude, codex), parsed headers populate `quota.ceilingTokens`, `quota.ceilingWindowSeconds`, `quota.remaining`, `quota.resetAt`. Parser tested with captured header fixtures from each harness.\n   - For harnesses/providers that expose nothing, fields remain null and the UI renders `—` / \"not reported\"; no fabricated data.\n   - Utilization bar renders when both `usage` and `quota.ceilingTokens` are present. Sparkline renders when ≥6 hourly buckets of usage are available.\n\n3. **Deliverable 3 — Trend detail.**\n   - Detail route `/nodes/.../providers/[name]` shows 7-day and 30-day tokens/hour + requests/hour time series, with quota ceiling overlay when known.\n   - Buckets computed server-side from the sessions index; the client receives ≤ 200 data points per series.\n   - \"Projected to hit quota in ~X\" callout uses last-24h slope only. Renders only when `quota.ceilingTokens` is known and the last-24h slope is non-zero positive; otherwise absent.\n   - Playwright asserts the detail route renders with seeded usage and the projection text appears for a fixture that has a ceiling + ascending usage.\n\n4. **Perf posture.**\n   - Fixture for perf test: ≥1,000 session index rows covering the last 30 days with usage distributed across ≥5 endpoints/harnesses. Per standing directive in `ddx-9ce6842a`.\n   - Targets: unified list p95 ≤ 200ms HTTP (excluding async probes); trend detail p95 ≤ 400ms HTTP on the fixture.\n   - If in-place aggregation cannot hit the detail-view p95 on a 10,000-row stretch fixture, pause and raise the DB-substrate decision with the user per the standing directive — do not silently switch.\n\n5. **Design note.**\n   - One-pager in `docs/helix/02-design/` before implementation recording the page-rename decision, the row model (common fields, kind-specific fields), async-probe strategy, and the quota source per harness/provider. PR links to it.\n\n6. **No regressions.**\n   - `ddx agent providers`, `ddx agent doctor`, `ddx agent list`, `ddx agent check` CLI commands continue to work unchanged.\n   - `defaultRouteStatus` query remains.\n\n7. **Cross-references.**\n   - Depends on `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions index) for usage aggregation. If that bead is not yet landed when this work starts, ship Deliverable 1 standalone with usage/quota stubbed as \"not available,\" and re-open the remaining deliverables when the index is ready.","archived_at":"2026-05-07T04:29:14Z","closing_commit_sha":"7ef7a4b1c517483485821f99aec728292bbe83a1","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:48:37.871527387Z","description":"## Observed\n\nThree related issues on `/nodes/.../providers`:\n\n1. **Loads slowly.** The page fires two GraphQL queries on mount — `providerStatuses` (which probes every configured provider for live connectivity) and `defaultRouteStatus` (which resolves the default route). Both are synchronous and serialized only by `Promise.all`. Each provider probe is a real network call.\n2. **Missing harnesses.** The pane shows API-endpoint providers (qwen, minimax, etc. — anything wired through the service with an endpoint). It does not show subprocess-invoked harnesses like `claude` (Claude Code) or `codex`. That is accurate to the current `providerStatuses` resolver (`cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_providers.go:15-50`, which calls `svc.ListProviders`) but not what an operator wants: the operator thinks of \"where their agent work can run,\" which includes both endpoint providers AND subprocess harnesses.\n3. **No quota visibility.** Operator need: see current token utilization rate + quota utilization per harness/provider, trend it, and use the trend to decide whether to throttle before hitting a cap.\n\n## Root cause by area\n\n- **Slow load:** `svc.ListProviders` probes every configured provider for liveness. Probes are serial within the service call, and the whole query blocks page render.\n- **Missing harnesses:** Subprocess harnesses (claude, codex, gemini) are first-class in the CLI (`ddx agent list`, `ddx agent doctor`, `ddx agent check`) but are not surfaced via `providerStatuses`. They have their own status concepts — binary installed, credentials configured, rate-limit headroom — that don't map cleanly onto the endpoint-provider model.\n- **No quota:** No existing data source captures rolling token usage vs quota. Quota information varies wildly by harness: Claude Code has message / session headroom concepts from response headers; codex has rate-limit headers; endpoint providers have API quota per key. There is no unified representation today.\n\n## Proposed direction\n\nThree deliverables, sequenced from easy to hard.\n\n### Deliverable 1 — Unified \"Agent endpoints\" view (renames/rescopes the page)\n\n- Rename the surface from \"Providers\" to **\"Agent endpoints\"** (or similar — confirm copy in the design note). The domain object is \"a place where agent work can run,\" not \"an API provider.\"\n- Merge the data: `providerStatuses` stays as the source for endpoint providers. A new `harnessStatuses` resolver (read from `svc.ListHarnesses` / existing `ddx agent list` + `ddx agent doctor` machinery) surfaces subprocess harnesses. Page renders both in one table with a `kind` column (`endpoint` | `harness`) — or two tables if design prefers.\n- Status fields common to both: name, kind, reachable (bool), detail (text), last-checked-at, default-for-profile (set of profile names).\n- Asynchronous liveness. The page renders an instant first paint from cached status; liveness probes fire in background and patch rows as they return. No synchronous probe-wall on load.\n\n### Deliverable 2 — Token \u0026 quota fields\n\n- Extend the status payload (per row) with: `usage { tokensUsedLastHour, tokensUsedLast24h, requestsLastHour, requestsLast24h }` and `quota { ceilingTokens?, ceilingWindowSeconds?, remaining?, resetAt? }`. Any field may be null if the provider/harness doesn't expose it.\n- Data sources:\n  - **Endpoint providers:** aggregate from the sessions index (`ddx-2ceb02fa`) filtered to the last 1h / 24h, grouped by provider. Quota ceilings come from any headers we capture during calls, plus optional static config.\n  - **Subprocess harnesses:** same sessions-index aggregation for usage. Quota — parse rate-limit headers from the harness output where available (Claude Code, codex both emit them). For harnesses that don't, leave quota null and render \"not reported.\"\n- Row gains two small inline displays: utilization bar (used / ceiling for the quota window) and a 7-day sparkline of tokens/hour.\n\n### Deliverable 3 — Trend view\n\n- Click into a row → detail page showing: 7-day and 30-day time-series of tokens/hour and requests/hour, with the quota ceiling overlaid. Aggregations come from the sessions index, bucketed server-side to avoid shipping raw rows.\n- Simple \"projected to hit quota in ~X hours/days\" callout computed from the slope of the last 24h — enough to answer \"are we on a trend to run out?\" without building a forecasting system.\n\n## Cross-bead dependencies\n\n- Depends on `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions index) for any usage-rate data. If that bead isn't landed when this work starts, the usage/quota features stub to \"not available\" and ship purely with the unified-view rename + async liveness.\n- Fixture / perf guidance: per the standing directive recorded in `ddx-9ce6842a`, any aggregation here is tested against ≥1k session records. If in-place aggregation can't hit perf targets, bring the DB-substrate question to the user before picking one.","events_attachment":"ddx-23978824/events.jsonl","feature":"FEAT-008","id":"ddx-23978824","issue_type":"task","labels":["feat-008","feat-006","feat-014","providers","observability"],"notes":"Cross-reference: ddx-6904a90b splits session costs into paid, subscription-equivalent, and local billingMode buckets; the unified endpoints usage/quota work should reuse those billingMode semantics when distinguishing paid endpoints, subscription harnesses, and local endpoints.\n\nREVIEW:BLOCK\n\n**Missing entire implementation.** The diff at `7da22459` adds only `.ddx/executions/20260423T044433-ce3a2c9d/result.json` — execution bookkeeping, not deliverable code. data.text: I’ve confirmed the composite interfaces and the current `Store`/`WatcherHub` definitions. The remaining step in this pass is just to pin the concrete backend files and the lint package path so the decomposition notes point at the right roots.

data.output:
1	package bead
     2	
     3	import (
     4		"bufio"
     5		"bytes"
     6		"context"
     7		"encoding/json"
     8		"fmt"
     9		"os"
    10		"path/filepath"
    11		"sort"
    12		"time"
    13	
    14		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead/axon"
    15	)
    16	
    17	// BackendAxon selects the axon-backed RawBackend. Per TD-030 D3 the Axon
    18	// shape splits beads from their events: one row per bead in the
    19	// ddx_beads collection, plus one entity per event in the
    20	// ddx_bead_events collection linked back to its bead via event_of.
    21	//
    22	// When a GraphQL transport is configured (test code wires one in via the
    23	// AxonBackend.GraphQLTransport field), AxonBackend uses the typed GraphQL
    24	// client in cli/internal/bead/axon/ as the primary path. The fallback keeps
    25	// the existing in-process JSONL emulation for callers that have not wired a
    26	// transport yet.
    27	//
    28	// NewStore routes to AxonBackend when bead.backend is set to axon in the
    29	// config or via DDX_BEAD_BACKEND.
    30	const BackendAxon = "axon"
    31	
    32	// axonSchemaVersion is written into every persisted entity. Schema upgrades
    33	// are handled lazy-on-read until axon FEAT-017 ships native schema
    34	// migration (per TD-030 v1 policy on schema versioning).
    35	const axonSchemaVersion = 1
    36	
    37	// AxonDirName is the per-store axon subdirectory under .ddx that holds the
    38	// two-collection snapshot files and the lock dir.
    39	const AxonDirName = "axon"
    40	
    41	// AxonBeadsCollection / AxonEventsCollection are the logical collection names
    42	// from TD-030's schema mapping. They are surfaced as the on-disk filenames so
    43	// inspecting the directory tells the operator which collection an entity
    44	// belongs to.
    45	const (
    46		AxonBeadsCollection  = "ddx_beads"
    47		AxonEventsCollection = "ddx_bead_events"
    48	)
    49	
    50	// AxonBackend stores the bead corpus in axon's two-collection shape.
    51	//
    52	// On WriteAll the backend splits each bead's inline Extra["events"] into one
    53	// entity per event under ddx_bead_events and persists the bead row (sans
    54	// inline events) under ddx_beads. ReadAll re-merges events back into
    55	// Extra["events"] so the higher-level Store and the chaos suite see the
    56	// uniform inline-events shape they expect.
    57	//
    58	// Beads with Extra[events_attachment] set (post-Close externalisation) are
    59	// persisted with their events array empty in axon's events collection. The
    60	// attachment file remains the canonical source for those events so Store's
    61	// existing read path (eventsForBead) keeps working unchanged.
    62	type AxonBackend struct {
    63		Dir              string
    64		BeadsFile        string
    65		EventsFile       string
    66		LockDir          string
    67		LockWait         time.Duration
    68		GraphQLTransport AxonGraphQLTransport
    69		GraphQLClient    any
    70	}
    71	
    72	// AxonGraphQLTransport is the injectable GraphQL execution boundary used by
    73	// the Axon backend. It mirrors the generated client's transport surface, so a
    74	// caller can wire a real transport into the future GraphQL implementation
    75	// without changing the storage code that uses this backend.
    76	type AxonGraphQLTransport interface {
    77		Query(ctx context.Context, query string, variables map[string]any, response any) error
    78	}
    79	
    80	// Compile-time check: AxonBackend satisfies RawBackend.
    81	var _ RawBackend = (*AxonBackend)(nil)
    82	
    83	// NewAxonBackend constructs an axon-backed RawBackend rooted at dir. dir is
    84	// the .ddx directory for the project; collection files and the lock live
    85	// under <dir>/axon/.
    86	func NewAxonBackend(dir string, lockWait time.Duration) *AxonBackend {
    87		root := filepath.Join(dir, AxonDirName)
    88		return &AxonBackend{
    89			Dir:        root,
    90			BeadsFile:  filepath.Join(root, AxonBeadsCollection+".jsonl"),
    91			EventsFile: filepath.Join(root, AxonEventsCollection+".jsonl"),
    92			LockDir:    filepath.Join(root, ".lock"),
    93			LockWait:   lockWait,
    94		}
    95	}
    96	
    97	func (a *AxonBackend) Init() error {
    98		if err := os.MkdirAll(a.Dir, 0o755); err != nil {
    99			return fmt.Errorf("bead: axon init dir: %w", err)
   100		}
   101		for _, path := range []string{a.BeadsFile, a.EventsFile} {
   102			f, err := os.OpenFile(path, os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY, 0o644)
   103			if err != nil {
   104				return fmt.Errorf("bead: axon init %s: %w", path, err)
   105			}
   106			if err := f.Close(); err != nil {
   107				return fmt.Errorf("bead: axon close %s: %w", path, err)
   108			}
   109		}
   110		return nil
   111	}
   112	
   113	const (
   114		axonReadCorpusQuery     = `query AxonReadCorpus { ddxBeads { _collection schema_version data } ddxBeadEvents { _collection schema_version event_of index event } }`
   115		axonWriteCorpusMutation = `mutation AxonWriteCorpus($beads: [AxonBeadRowInput!]!, $events: [AxonEventRowInput!]!) { saveCorpus(beads: $beads, events: $events) { ok } }`
   116	)
   117	
   118	type axonCorpusResponse struct {
   119		Beads  []axonEntityEnvelope `json:"ddxBeads"`
   120		Events []axonEventEnvelope  `json:"ddxBeadEvents"`
   121	}
   122	
   123	type axonWriteCorpusResponse struct {
   124		SaveCorpus axonWriteCorpusResult `json:"saveCorpus"`
   125	}
   126	
   127	type axonWriteCorpusResult struct {
   128		OK bool `json:"ok"`
   129	}
   130	
   131	// ReadAll loads bead rows and event entities from Axon when a GraphQL
   132	// transport is configured, then re-merges events back into each bead's
   133	// Extra["events"]. The legacy on-disk snapshot fallback keeps the old
   134	// compatibility path for callers that have not wired a transport yet.
   135	// Orphan events (event_of pointing at a missing bead, e.g. after a
   136	// partial-write crash) are silently dropped.
   137	func (a *AxonBackend) ReadAll() ([]Bead, error) {

.ddx/beads.jsonl:77:{"created_at":"2026-05-11T18:42:56.885339633Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-74452926","depends_on_id":"ddx-3af1c1a6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-11T20:00:39Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-74452926","depends_on_id":"ddx-71d3a2de","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-11T20:00:39Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-74452926","depends_on_id":"ddx-0ab16765","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-11T20:00:39Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-74452926","depends_on_id":"ddx-9c5bca8f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-11T20:00:40Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-74452926","depends_on_id":"ddx-29f02cf4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-11T20:00:40Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-74452926","depends_on_id":"ddx-900a8d38","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-11T20:00:40Z"}],"description":"# PROBLEM
After Phase 1 (Foundation `ddx-c6317784`) and per-concern beads (L/C/X/Q/D/A/MIG) land, callers no longer use the removed workflow methods on `*Store` — but they still hold `*Store` as a struct field type for foundational reads/writes/queries/etc. The `Backend` interface composite isn't yet asserted on `*Store`. The concrete types (`*Store`, `RawBackend`, `JSONLBackend`, `ExternalBackend`, `AxonStore`, `*WatcherHub`) live at `cli/internal/bead/` — publicly importable from any other package.

The goal of TD-027 §21 is **physical impossibility** of bypass: not lint-prevented, not review-prevented, but a Go compiler error if any code outside `cli/internal/bead/` tries to import the concrete storage implementations. This bead enforces that via Go's `internal/` rule.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` public.
- `cli/internal/bead/backend_jsonl.go` — `JSONLBackend` public.
- `cli/internal/bead/backend_external.go` — `ExternalBackend` public.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go` (or `AxonStore` post `ddx-9c5bca8f`) — public.
- `cli/internal/bead/watcher.go` — `*WatcherHub` public.
- All supporting concrete implementation files (`claim_liveness.go`, `attachments.go`, `archive.go`, `migrate.go`, etc.) — public.

Plus the final assembly step (formerly "K" in earlier breakdowns): `Backend` composite interface declared, `ReadOnlyBackend` convenience composite, compile-time assertion `var _ Backend = (*Store)(nil)`. These were deferred from Foundation because the composite couldn't be asserted until all per-concern beads removed their methods.

# PROPOSED FIX

This bead does three load-bearing things in one atomic change:

## 1. Narrow caller field types from `*bead.Store` to `bead.Backend` (or narrower)

Every caller file in `cli/cmd/`, `cli/internal/agent/`, `cli/internal/server/`, `cli/internal/escalation/`, `cli/internal/agentmetrics/`, `cli/internal/attemptmetrics/`, `cli/internal/processmetrics/`, `cli/internal/exec/`, `cli/internal/agent/try/` that holds `*bead.Store` as a struct field or local variable is updated:

- Most callers: change `*bead.Store` to `bead.Backend` (full interface). Mechanical rename.
- Narrow candidates (identified during per-concern beads): use narrower sub-interface where appropriate (e.g., `cli/cmd/work_focus.go` → `bead.BeadQueries`; `cli/internal/agentmetrics/loader.go` → `bead.BeadReader`).
- Documented exceptions remain on concrete `*Store` access OR on direct file I/O (allowlisted):
  - `cli/cmd/bead_doctor.go` — file-level repair; reads `.ddx/beads.jsonl` directly.
  - `cli/cmd/bead.go` (git auto-commit + conflict-resolution code paths only) — operates on the file as a file.
  - `cli/cmd/sync.go` — `ddx sync` operates on the file.

## 2. Add `Backend` composite + compile-time assertion

- Declare `Backend` as the union of all 11 sub-interfaces:
  `BeadInitializer + BeadReader + BeadLifecycle + BeadEventReader + BeadEventWriter + BeadQueries + BeadDependencyReader + BeadDependencyWriter + BeadArchive + BeadInterchangeReader + BeadInterchangeWriter`.
- Declare `ReadOnlyBackend` convenience composite (5 reader sub-interfaces) per TD-027 §6.1.
- Add compile-time assertion: `var _ Backend = (*Store)(nil)`.

## 3. Move concretes to internal/

- Create `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/` and `cli/internal/bead/internal/lifecycle/`.
- Move (with package rename):
  - `store.go` → `internal/storage/store.go` (package `storage`)
  - `backend_jsonl.go` → `internal/storage/backend_jsonl.go`
  - `backend_external.go` → `internal/storage/backend_external.go`
  - `axon_backend.go` / `axon_store.go` → `internal/storage/`
  - `claim_liveness.go` → `internal/storage/`
  - `attachments.go`, `archive.go`, `migrate.go`, etc. → `internal/storage/`
  - `watcher.go` → `internal/lifecycle/watcher_hub.go` (package `lifecycle`)
  - `RawBackend` and `OperationApplier` interfaces also move into `internal/storage/`
- Create `cli/internal/bead/factory.go` as the SOLE public construction path:
  ```go
  package bead
  import "cli/internal/bead/internal/storage"
  func NewStore(opts StoreOptions) (Backend, error) { return storage.New(opts) }
  ```
- The public `cli/internal/bead/` package surface is now: `Bead` struct, `Backend` + sub-interfaces, `Operation` + named ops, errors, `IDGenerator` + `ValidateID`, ctx accessors, `LifecycleSubscriber` interface, `Migrator` factory (from MIG bead), and the `ops/` helper sub-packages.

## 4. Lint cleanup

After lockdown, two analyzers in the lint suite (`ddx-e91a45c0`) are redundant because Go's `internal/` rule enforces the same property at the compiler level:
- `concrete-store-methods` — removed.
- `no-internal-store-construction` — removed.

Retained analyzers (defense-in-depth inside `cli/internal/bead/`):
- `direct-bead-jsonl-io` (callers still shouldn't bypass the abstraction)
- `no-new-rawbackend-impls` (new backends use Backend directly)
- `typed-context-accessors-only` (ctx.Value discipline)

# NON-SCOPE
- Per-concern bead work — must complete first.
- AxonStore implementation — `ddx-9c5bca8f`.
- IMP (JSONL→Postgres importer) — separate downstream bead.
- WIRE (real-wire integration tests) — separate downstream bead.

# Acceptance criteria

1. Every caller file in scope (per PROPOSED FIX §1) has its `*bead.Store` field/variable types narrowed to `bead.Backend` or a sub-interface, with the allowlist exceptions (`bead_doctor.go`, file-level paths in `bead.go`, `sync.go`).
2. `cli/internal/bead/backend.go` declares `Backend` as the union of 11 sub-interfaces. `ReadOnlyBackend` declared per TD-027 §6.1.
3. `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/` and `cli/internal/bead/internal/lifecycle/` exist; all concrete implementation files relocated with appropriate package renames.
4. `cli/internal/bead/factory.go` is the sole public construction path. Exports `NewStore`, `NewLifecycleSubscriber`, `NewMigrator` (from MIG). No other public function constructs a concrete storage type.
5. **Build must succeed**: `cd cli && go build ./...`. **Failure indicates incomplete per-concern bead migration** — diagnose the failing caller and complete its migration before retrying. Do NOT bypass via build tags or temporary public re-exports.
6. Boundary verification test: `TestModuleBoundary_NoInternalImportsOutsideBead` — static analysis scans every Go file under `cli/` outside `cli/internal/bead/` and asserts none of them import `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage` or `cli/internal/bead/internal/lifecycle`. AST-based.
7. Compile-time `var _ Backend = (*storage.Store)(nil)` exists inside `internal/storage/` (verifying Backend satisfaction now that the concrete type is private to that package).
8. Lint cleanup: `concrete-store-methods` and `no-internal-store-construction` analyzers removed from the bead-lints binary. Documented in `cli/tools/lints/README.md`. The retained 3 analyzers stay.
9. Existing tests pass without modification. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
10. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

The new interfaces are decorative until callers migrate to them.

Without caller migration, the Stage 3 boundary lockdown (moving `*Store` into `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/`) is impossible — any caller still holding `*Store` would fail to compile, and the gating bead's "physically impossible bypass" goal (TD-027 §21) is unrealized.

# ROOT CAUSE (file:line)
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` is currently public and exported. Every caller importing `cli/internal/bead/` can hold it concretely.
- ~60 files identified via `grep -rln "internal/bead\"" cli/ | grep -v _test`. Each caller file uses some subset of `*Store`'s 69 public methods directly rather than going through the `Backend` interface, sub-interfaces, or helper packages.

1. **Manual review per caller file** (~60 files). For each:
   1. Enumerate every method called on the `*Store` value (grep `<varname>\.` for the local variable name).

      - Method exists on a `Backend` sub-interface → replace `*bead.Store` field type with the narrowest sub-interface (e.g. `bead.BeadReader`, `bead.BeadQueries`).
      - Method has a typed `Operation` covering it → call `store.Apply(ctx, id, op)` instead of the convenience method.
      - Method has a helper in `cli/internal/bead/ops/<concern>/` → use the helper.
      - Method has none of the above → either (a) add to the interface in this bead, (b) add as a new `Operation`, (c) add as a new helper, or (d) document as a legitimate exception (file-level repair, git ops, conflict resolution).
   3. Refactor the caller to use the chosen substitute. Update tests.

2. **Documented exceptions** (callers that stay concrete on `*Store` or do direct file I/O):
   - `cli/cmd/bead_doctor.go` — file-level repair of corrupted `beads.jsonl`. Operates on the file as data, not as bead-as-entity. Allowlisted.
   - `cli/cmd/bead.go:1550-1586` — git conflict resolution. File-level merge resolution. Allowlisted.
   - `cli/cmd/bead.go:80-127` — git auto-commit knows the file path. Allowlisted.
   - `cli/cmd/sync.go` — `ddx sync` synchronizes the file across machines. Allowlisted.

3. **Lint enforcement**: per TD-027 §21, `cli/tools/lints/concrete-store-methods` flags new files holding `*bead.Store` as a struct field. After this bead lands, the allowlist shrinks dramatically — only the documented exceptions remain. Implementation of the lint suite itself is `ddx-e91a45c0` (parallel work).

4. **Test parameterization**: tests that assert backend-portable behavior move into the parameterized conformance suite in `cli/internal/bead/`. Tests asserting JSONL-internal implementation details stay using `*Store` concretely (acceptable; backend-internal tests).

- Changes to `*Store`'s public surface — backwards compat is preserved.

Reviewed-file list documented in a checklist (or via git-diff coverage) in the bead's evidence directory.

.ddx/beads.jsonl:8:{"closing_commit_sha":"81958ef5baab9362ad0e85b1c481c0c5a20a4c21","created_at":"2026-05-11T19:59:02.143711599Z","description":"# PROBLEM
`*Store` exposes 7 migration-tooling methods used by `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` and related admin commands: `MigrateLifecycle`, `MigrateFromHelix`, `MigrateToAxon`, `MigrateDryRun`, `MigrateLifecycleDryRun`, `DetectLifecycleMigrationRequired`, `ReconcileLifecycleMetadata`. These are admin/one-shot tooling, not steady-state Backend operations. After the BL bead moves concrete `*Store` to `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/`, `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` cannot reach these methods. They need an exposure path that doesn't violate the boundary lockdown.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/store.go` exposes the 7 migration methods. `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` imports `cli/internal/bead` and calls them on `*Store` concretely. Per TD-027 §11.5 + Migration row in the mutation rules table, these are admin tools not on the `Backend` interface. After BL, the only public surface in `cli/internal/bead/` is what's exported there — concrete `*Store` won't be reachable.

# PROPOSED FIX

Per the converged design recommendation (separate factory rather than `BeadMigration` sub-interface on Backend, to avoid forcing every backend to stub source-specific migrations):

1. Create `cli/internal/bead/migrator.go` with a `Migrator` interface and `NewMigrator` factory:
   ```go
   type Migrator interface {
       MigrateLifecycle(ctx context.Context, opts MigrationOptions) (MigrationResult, error)
       MigrateLifecycleDryRun(ctx context.Context, opts MigrationOptions) (MigrationResult, error)
       MigrateFromHelix(ctx context.Context, src string) error
       MigrateToAxon(ctx context.Context, opts MigrateToAxonOptions) error  // deprecated; replaced by ddx-IMP
       MigrateDryRun(ctx context.Context, opts MigrationOptions) (MigrationResult, error)
       DetectLifecycleMigrationRequired(ctx context.Context) (bool, MigrationReport, error)
       ReconcileLifecycleMetadata(ctx context.Context) (ReconcileReport, error)
   }

   func NewMigrator(opts MigratorOptions) (Migrator, error)
   ```
   The concrete implementation lives in `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/` after BL; the public factory returns a `Migrator` interface.

2. Move the 7 methods' bodies from `*Store` (in `cli/internal/bead/store.go` and `migrate.go`) onto an unexported `*storeMigrator` type that holds a `*Store` reference and implements `Migrator`. The `NewMigrator` factory returns this.

3. Update `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` and any related admin commands:
   ```go
   mig, err := bead.NewMigrator(bead.MigratorOptions{Dir: workspace})
   if err != nil { ... }
   mig.MigrateLifecycle(ctx, opts)
   ```

4. Remove `*Store.MigrateLifecycle`, `MigrateLifecycleDryRun`, `MigrateFromHelix`, `MigrateToAxon`, `MigrateDryRun`, `DetectLifecycleMigrationRequired`, `ReconcileLifecycleMetadata` from the `*Store` public method set. Logic moves to the `*storeMigrator` type.

5. `MigrateToAxon` retained as a deprecated method on `Migrator` that returns `ErrDeprecated` with a pointer to the IMP bead's new importer. Final removal happens after IMP lands.

# NON-SCOPE
- The new JSONL→Postgres importer (replaces `MigrateToAxon`'s JSONL-writing behavior) — separate IMP bead.
- Schema-versioning migration ladder framework — deferred until first breaking change is proposed.
- Other concerns — separate beads.

# Acceptance criteria

1. `cli/internal/bead/migrator.go` declares the `Migrator` interface (7 methods, ctx-aware) and `NewMigrator(opts) (Migrator, error)` factory. Compile-time assertion: `var _ Migrator = (*storeMigrator)(nil)`.
2. `*Store` no longer exposes the 7 migration methods directly. Verified by `grep -E 'func \(s \*Store\) (MigrateLifecycle|MigrateLifecycleDryRun|MigrateFromHelix|MigrateToAxon|MigrateDryRun|DetectLifecycleMigrationRequired|ReconcileLifecycleMetadata)\b' cli/internal/bead/*.go` returning nothing.
3. `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` constructs `Migrator` via `bead.NewMigrator(...)` and invokes operations. Holds `Migrator` interface, not `*storeMigrator` concrete.
4. End-to-end CLI invocation works: `ddx bead migrate --lifecycle --dry-run`, `ddx bead migrate --lifecycle --apply` produce identical output to today. Test: `TestMigrator_LifecycleMigration_RoundTrip`, `TestMigrator_FromHelix_PreservesAllFields`.
5. `MigrateToAxon` on the Migrator returns `ErrDeprecated` with message pointing at the new importer (will be implemented in IMP). Test: `TestMigrator_MigrateToAxon_ReturnsDeprecated`.
6. Existing migration tests in `cli/internal/bead/migrate_test.go` continue to pass against the new Migrator-mediated path.
7. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

`*Store` exposes 3 dependency-graph methods (`DepAdd`, `DepRemove`, `DepTree`) and 3 queue-ordering methods (`QueueTop`, `QueueMove`, `QueueClear`). Per TD-027 §6 the dep methods belong on `BeadDependencyReader`/`BeadDependencyWriter` sub-interfaces (signature-only change — ctx added). Queue ordering is operator-driven workflow; per TD-027 §14 it belongs in `ops/queue/` helpers with typed `Operation` values.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/store.go` exposes these 6 methods. Foundation declared the `BeadDependencyReader`/`BeadDependencyWriter` interfaces but ctx isn't yet on `*Store`'s dep methods. `BeadQueue` isn't an interface in TD-027 — queue ops are workflow concerns that live as helpers.

# PROPOSED FIX

## Dependencies (signature-only)

1. Update `*Store.DepAdd(ctx, id, depID)`, `DepRemove(ctx, id, depID)`, `DepTree(ctx, rootID)` to take ctx.
2. Compile-time assertions: `var _ BeadDependencyReader = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadDependencyWriter = (*Store)(nil)`.
3. Thread ctx through callers. Narrow field types opportunistically (e.g., `ddx bead dep tree` CLI handler can narrow to `BeadDependencyReader`).

## Queue (helper-migration; methods removed)

4. Add named `Operation` types to `cli/internal/bead/operation.go`:
   - `QueueSetTop{}` — move bead to top of queue (sparse rank ordering).
   - `QueueSetPosition{Position int}` — operator move-to-position.
   - `QueueClearOp{}` — remove queue rank.
5. Create `cli/internal/bead/ops/queue/queue.go`:
   - `Top(ctx, l, id) error` — wraps `QueueSetTop{}`.
   - `Move(ctx, l, id, position int) error` — wraps `QueueSetPosition`.
   - `Clear(ctx, l, id) error` — wraps `QueueClearOp{}`.
6. Migrate callers (operator queue manipulation in `cli/cmd/bead.go` queue subcommands) from `*Store.QueueTop`/`QueueMove`/`QueueClear` → `ops/queue/` helpers.
7. Remove `*Store.QueueTop`, `QueueMove`, `QueueClear`.

# NON-SCOPE
- Dependency cycle detection — handled at higher layer.
- Queue rank renormalization algorithm — internal to ops/queue; sparse-rank logic remains as-is.
- Other concerns — separate beads.
- Module boundary lockdown — `ddx-74452926`.

# Acceptance criteria

1. `*Store.DepAdd`, `DepRemove`, `DepTree` take ctx. Compile-time assertions for `BeadDependencyReader` and `BeadDependencyWriter` compile.
2. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` declares `QueueSetTop`, `QueueSetPosition{Position}`, `QueueClearOp` types with `Apply(b *Bead) error` impls. Tests: `TestOperation_QueueSetTop_AssignsTopRank`, `TestOperation_QueueSetPosition_RespectsBucket`, `TestOperation_QueueClearOp_RemovesRank`.
3. `cli/internal/bead/ops/queue/` package exists with helpers + tests: `TestOpsQueue_Top_MovesBeadToFront`, `TestOpsQueue_Move_PreservesPriorityBucket`, `TestOpsQueue_Clear_RemovesOperatorOverride`.
4. `*Store` no longer exposes `QueueTop`, `QueueMove`, `QueueClear`. Verified by grep.
5. Caller files no longer reference these methods on `*Store` outside the bead package.
6. Queue-ordering invariants (SD-004 §"Ready queue" / queue derivation) preserved — operator overrides apply within a priority bucket; explicit rank wins over missing rank within bucket. Test: `TestQueueOrdering_OperatorRankWithinBucket`.
7. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

The legacy `*Store.MigrateToAxon` method (now exposed via `Migrator.MigrateToAxon` in MIG bead, returning `ErrDeprecated`) writes JSONL files under `.ddx/axon/` rather than performing an actual JSONL→Postgres migration. Per the audit in `docs/plans/plan-2026-05-10-axon-only-architecture.md`, production AxonStore deployment requires migrating existing `.ddx/beads.jsonl` corpora into Lakebase Postgres. The real importer is unimplemented.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/migrate.go` (function previously at `*Store.MigrateToAxon`, now folded into `*storeMigrator` per MIG bead) writes JSONL into a sidecar directory. The function does not connect to Postgres or invoke `AxonStore.Apply`/`AxonStore.Create`. Production deployment cannot use it to populate Lakebase.

# PROPOSED FIX

1. Implement a real importer in `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/import_postgres.go` (or equivalent post-BL location). The importer:
   - Reads `.ddx/beads.jsonl` (and `.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl` if present) — uses existing JSONL parser.
   - For each bead, calls `axonStore.Create(ctx, &b)` to insert into Postgres. Idempotent on `ErrConflict` (skip + log).
   - For each bead, appends events via `axonStore.Apply(ctx, id, AppendEventOp{...})` per TD-027 §13 vocabulary (events come from `Extra["events"]` on the JSONL bead or from sidecar `.ddx/attachments/<bead>/events.jsonl`).
   - Tracks progress, supports `--dry-run` (counts without writes), `--limit N` (batched migration).
   - Verifies post-migration: read back beads from Postgres, assert field-for-field equality with source JSONL (sample-based for large corpora).

2. Expose via `Migrator` interface (added in MIG): rename/replace `MigrateToAxon` with the real implementation; same method name, different (correct) behavior.

3. Update `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` to support the importer with appropriate flags:
   - `ddx bead migrate --to-axon --dry-run`
   - `ddx bead migrate --to-axon --apply [--limit N]`
   - `ddx bead migrate --to-axon --verify` (read-back + checksum)

4. Remove the deprecated JSONL-writing behavior. `ErrDeprecated` return from MIG bead is replaced by the real implementation.

# NON-SCOPE
- Schema-versioning migration ladder (separate concern; deferred until first v1→v2 breaking change).
- Real-wire integration test against actual Postgres — separate WIRE bead.
- Continuous sync between JSONL and Postgres — not in scope; this is one-shot migration.
- AxonStore itself — implemented in AX (`ddx-9c5bca8f`).

# Acceptance criteria

1. New file `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/import_postgres.go` (or equivalent) implements the JSONL→Postgres importer. Uses `axonStore.Create` + `axonStore.Apply` for inserts + event appends.
2. Existing `Migrator.MigrateToAxon` method returns the real importer's result (not `ErrDeprecated`).
3. CLI flags wired: `--to-axon --dry-run`, `--to-axon --apply`, `--to-axon --verify`. Test: `TestImporter_DryRun_CountsOnly`, `TestImporter_Apply_WritesToPostgres`, `TestImporter_Verify_DetectsDrift`.
4. Idempotency: re-running `--apply` on an already-imported corpus is a no-op (skips conflicts). Test: `TestImporter_Apply_Idempotent_OnReRun`.
5. Field-fidelity: every JSONL bead field is preserved in Postgres, including `extra` preserved-keys. Test: `TestImporter_PreservesExtras`.
6. Event ordering: events imported preserve monotonic `created_at` (TD-027 §11.2 invariant 8). Test: `TestImporter_PreservesEventOrdering`.
7. Attachment migration: sidecar files under `.ddx/attachments/<bead>/` are uploaded to Unity Catalog Volumes (or local-equivalent for testing). Test: `TestImporter_MigratesAttachments`.
8. Verification mode: post-import, sample N beads, read back from AxonStore, assert equality with source. Test: `TestImporter_Verify_RoundTripPassesOnCleanImport`.
9. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green (with httptest-served Axon-shaped GraphQL endpoint or in-memory backend).
10. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

# Labels

`cli/internal/bead/store.go`, `cli/internal/bead/archive.go`, `cli/internal/bead/import.go` define these 6 methods without ctx. Callers: `cli/cmd/bead_archive.go`, `cli/cmd/bead.go` (export/import subcommands).

# PROPOSED FIX
1. Update `*Store.Archive`, `ArchiveWithEvents`, `Migrate`, `Import`, `ExportTo`, `ExportToFile` to take `ctx context.Context` first.
2. Compile-time assertions: `var _ BeadArchive = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadInterchangeReader = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadInterchangeWriter = (*Store)(nil)`.
3. Thread ctx through callers (`cli/cmd/bead_archive.go`, `cli/cmd/bead.go`).

# NON-SCOPE
- Archive policy (TD-027 §16) — not modified.
- Attachment storage layout (TD-027 §17) — not modified.
- bd/br interchange shape (TD-027 §20) — wire format unchanged.
- Migration tooling (`MigrateLifecycle`/`MigrateFromHelix`/etc.) — separate concern, handled in MIG bead.
- Other concerns — separate beads.
- Module boundary lockdown — `ddx-74452926`.

# Acceptance criteria

1. The 6 methods take ctx as first parameter. Compile-time assertions for `BeadArchive`, `BeadInterchangeReader`, `BeadInterchangeWriter` compile.
2. Callers thread ctx. Existing archive/import/export tests pass.
3. `ddx bead archive`, `ddx bead export`, `ddx bead import` CLI commands continue to work end-to-end. Test: `TestBeadArchiveCommand_RoundTrip`, `TestBeadExportImport_RoundTripsAllFields`.
4. Schema round-trip preserved (per TD-027 §20 — bd/br interchange compatibility). Test: `TestBead_ExportImportRoundTrip_PreservesExtras`.
5. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

.ddx/beads.jsonl:77:{"created_at":"2026-05-11T18:42:56.885339633Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-74452926","depends_on_id":"ddx-3af1c1a6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-11T20:00:39Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-74452926","depends_on_id":"ddx-71d3a2de","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-11T20:00:39Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-74452926","depends_on_id":"ddx-0ab16765","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-11T20:00:39Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-74452926","depends_on_id":"ddx-9c5bca8f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-11T20:00:40Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-74452926","depends_on_id":"ddx-29f02cf4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-11T20:00:40Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-74452926","depends_on_id":"ddx-900a8d38","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-11T20:00:40Z"}],"description":"# PROBLEM
After Phase 1 (Foundation `ddx-c6317784`) and per-concern beads (L/C/X/Q/D/A/MIG) land, callers no longer use the removed workflow methods on `*Store` — but they still hold `*Store` as a struct field type for foundational reads/writes/queries/etc. The `Backend` interface composite isn't yet asserted on `*Store`. The concrete types (`*Store`, `RawBackend`, `JSONLBackend`, `ExternalBackend`, `AxonStore`, `*WatcherHub`) live at `cli/internal/bead/` — publicly importable from any other package.

The goal of TD-027 §21 is **physical impossibility** of bypass: not lint-prevented, not review-prevented, but a Go compiler error if any code outside `cli/internal/bead/` tries to import the concrete storage implementations. This bead enforces that via Go's `internal/` rule.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` public.
- `cli/internal/bead/backend_jsonl.go` — `JSONLBackend` public.
- `cli/internal/bead/backend_external.go` — `ExternalBackend` public.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go` (or `AxonStore` post `ddx-9c5bca8f`) — public.
- `cli/internal/bead/watcher.go` — `*WatcherHub` public.
- All supporting concrete implementation files (`claim_liveness.go`, `attachments.go`, `archive.go`, `migrate.go`, etc.) — public.

Plus the final assembly step (formerly "K" in earlier breakdowns): `Backend` composite interface declared, `ReadOnlyBackend` convenience composite, compile-time assertion `var _ Backend = (*Store)(nil)`. These were deferred from Foundation because the composite couldn't be asserted until all per-concern beads removed their methods.

# PROPOSED FIX

This bead does three load-bearing things in one atomic change:

## 1. Narrow caller field types from `*bead.Store` to `bead.Backend` (or narrower)

Every caller file in `cli/cmd/`, `cli/internal/agent/`, `cli/internal/server/`, `cli/internal/escalation/`, `cli/internal/agentmetrics/`, `cli/internal/attemptmetrics/`, `cli/internal/processmetrics/`, `cli/internal/exec/`, `cli/internal/agent/try/` that holds `*bead.Store` as a struct field or local variable is updated:

- Most callers: change `*bead.Store` to `bead.Backend` (full interface). Mechanical rename.
- Narrow candidates (identified during per-concern beads): use narrower sub-interface where appropriate (e.g., `cli/cmd/work_focus.go` → `bead.BeadQueries`; `cli/internal/agentmetrics/loader.go` → `bead.BeadReader`).
- Documented exceptions remain on concrete `*Store` access OR on direct file I/O (allowlisted):
  - `cli/cmd/bead_doctor.go` — file-level repair; reads `.ddx/beads.jsonl` directly.
  - `cli/cmd/bead.go` (git auto-commit + conflict-resolution code paths only) — operates on the file as a file.
  - `cli/cmd/sync.go` — `ddx sync` operates on the file.

## 2. Add `Backend` composite + compile-time assertion

- Declare `Backend` as the union of all 11 sub-interfaces:
  `BeadInitializer + BeadReader + BeadLifecycle + BeadEventReader + BeadEventWriter + BeadQueries + BeadDependencyReader + BeadDependencyWriter + BeadArchive + BeadInterchangeReader + BeadInterchangeWriter`.
- Declare `ReadOnlyBackend` convenience composite (5 reader sub-interfaces) per TD-027 §6.1.
- Add compile-time assertion: `var _ Backend = (*Store)(nil)`.

## 3. Move concretes to internal/

- Create `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/` and `cli/internal/bead/internal/lifecycle/`.
- Move (with package rename):
  - `store.go` → `internal/storage/store.go` (package `storage`)
  - `backend_jsonl.go` → `internal/storage/backend_jsonl.go`
  - `backend_external.go` → `internal/storage/backend_external.go`
  - `axon_backend.go` / `axon_store.go` → `internal/storage/`
  - `claim_liveness.go` → `internal/storage/`
  - `attachments.go`, `archive.go`, `migrate.go`, etc. → `internal/storage/`
  - `watcher.go` → `internal/lifecycle/watcher_hub.go` (package `lifecycle`)
  - `RawBackend` and `OperationApplier` interfaces also move into `internal/storage/`
- Create `cli/internal/bead/factory.go` as the SOLE public construction path:
  ```go
  package bead
  import "cli/internal/bead/internal/storage"
  func NewStore(opts StoreOptions) (Backend, error) { return storage.New(opts) }
  ```
- The public `cli/internal/bead/` package surface is now: `Bead` struct, `Backend` + sub-interfaces, `Operation` + named ops, errors, `IDGenerator` + `ValidateID`, ctx accessors, `LifecycleSubscriber` interface, `Migrator` factory (from MIG bead), and the `ops/` helper sub-packages.

## 4. Lint cleanup

After lockdown, two analyzers in the lint suite (`ddx-e91a45c0`) are redundant because Go's `internal/` rule enforces the same property at the compiler level:
- `concrete-store-methods` — removed.
- `no-internal-store-construction` — removed.

Retained analyzers (defense-in-depth inside `cli/internal/bead/`):
- `direct-bead-jsonl-io` (callers still shouldn't bypass the abstraction)
- `no-new-rawbackend-impls` (new backends use Backend directly)
- `typed-context-accessors-only` (ctx.Value discipline)

# NON-SCOPE
- Per-concern bead work — must complete first.
- AxonStore implementation — `ddx-9c5bca8f`.
- IMP (JSONL→Postgres importer) — separate downstream bead.
- WIRE (real-wire integration tests) — separate downstream bead.

# Acceptance criteria

1. Every caller file in scope (per PROPOSED FIX §1) has its `*bead.Store` field/variable types narrowed to `bead.Backend` or a sub-interface, with the allowlist exceptions (`bead_doctor.go`, file-level paths in `bead.go`, `sync.go`).
2. `cli/internal/bead/backend.go` declares `Backend` as the union of 11 sub-interfaces. `ReadOnlyBackend` declared per TD-027 §6.1.
3. `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/` and `cli/internal/bead/internal/lifecycle/` exist; all concrete implementation files relocated with appropriate package renames.
4. `cli/internal/bead/factory.go` is the sole public construction path. Exports `NewStore`, `NewLifecycleSubscriber`, `NewMigrator` (from MIG). No other public function constructs a concrete storage type.
5. **Build must succeed**: `cd cli && go build ./...`. **Failure indicates incomplete per-concern bead migration** — diagnose the failing caller and complete its migration before retrying. Do NOT bypass via build tags or temporary public re-exports.
6. Boundary verification test: `TestModuleBoundary_NoInternalImportsOutsideBead` — static analysis scans every Go file under `cli/` outside `cli/internal/bead/` and asserts none of them import `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage` or `cli/internal/bead/internal/lifecycle`. AST-based.
7. Compile-time `var _ Backend = (*storage.Store)(nil)` exists inside `internal/storage/` (verifying Backend satisfaction now that the concrete type is private to that package).
8. Lint cleanup: `concrete-store-methods` and `no-internal-store-construction` analyzers removed from the bead-lints binary. Documented in `cli/tools/lints/README.md`. The retained 3 analyzers stay.
9. Existing tests pass without modification. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
10. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

.ddx/beads.jsonl:95:{"closing_commit_sha":"87b65ce87c3b4e9054abe292bfa8d5959bc5584f","created_at":"2026-05-11T17:54:48.993345328Z","description":"# PROBLEM
`*bead.WatcherHub` (`cli/internal/bead/watcher.go:36-66`) constructs its own `*Store` internally via `NewStore(projectID + "/.ddx")`. This bypasses any factory-level capability validation or backend selection. Today everything is JSONL so this is invisible; once AxonStore ships and `bead.backend: axon` is configured for production, the WatcherHub still polls a local JSONL file while the authoritative bead state lives in Lakebase Postgres. Result: silent stale-data bug — `beadLifecycle` GraphQL subscriptions return empty or stale events.

# ROOT CAUSE (file:line)
- `cli/internal/bead/watcher.go:54-66` — `SubscribeLifecycle` instantiates a `*Store` directly: `store := NewStore(projectID + "/.ddx")`. No injection point for the configured backend.
- `cli/internal/bead/watcher.go:60-65` — `newProjectWatcher(store, h.interval)` ties the watcher to that locally-constructed Store.
- `cli/internal/server/server.go` — constructs `*WatcherHub` and passes it to subscription resolvers. The hub is constructed once at server startup; the project Stores it creates internally are detached from any per-project factory.

# PROPOSED FIX
Change `WatcherHub` to take a `bead.BeadReader` (or `Backend`) factory function at construction:

```go
type StoreFactory func(projectID string) (bead.BeadReader, error)

type WatcherHub struct {
    factory  StoreFactory
    watchers map[string]*projectWatcher
    interval time.Duration
    ...
}

func NewWatcherHub(factory StoreFactory, interval time.Duration) *WatcherHub { ... }

func (h *WatcherHub) SubscribeLifecycle(ctx context.Context, projectID string) (<-chan LifecycleEvent, func(), error) {
    // ...
    store, err := h.factory(projectID)
    if err != nil { return nil, nil, err }
    pw = newProjectWatcher(store, h.interval)
    // ...
}
```

`cli/internal/server/server.go` is updated to pass a factory that uses the project's configured backend (selected via the same config pathway that the rest of ddx-server uses).

# NON-SCOPE
- Native subscription transport on AxonStore (separate work — would replace the polling-based `projectWatcher` with native push when available).
- Changing `LifecycleEvent` shape or semantics.
- Multi-tenant identity propagation (handled separately via the `bead.WithIdentity` ctx accessor pattern).
- Modifying callers other than `cli/internal/server/server.go` and the subscription resolver.

# Acceptance criteria

1. `WatcherHub` constructor signature changes to accept a `StoreFactory` parameter. Compile-time assertion `var _ LifecycleSubscriber = (*WatcherHub)(nil)` still holds.
2. `cli/internal/bead/watcher.go` no longer contains a direct `NewStore(...)` call.
3. `cli/internal/server/server.go` constructs `WatcherHub` with a factory that delegates to the same backend-selection path the rest of the server uses.
4. Test `TestWatcherHub_UsesProvidedFactory` injects a fake factory, asserts the factory is invoked when SubscribeLifecycle is called, and asserts the watcher polls the injected `bead.BeadReader` rather than the filesystem.
5. Test `TestWatcherHub_PropagatesFactoryErrors` injects a factory that returns an error and asserts SubscribeLifecycle returns the error.
6. Existing `TestWatcherHub_*` tests in `cli/internal/bead/watcher_test.go` are updated to use the factory pattern; they continue to pass.
7. `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./internal/server/...` is green.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

# Labels

The current Axon backend does not talk to Axon at all; it rewrites local JSONL snapshots under <dir>/axon/ instead of using the GraphQL entity store described by TD-030. That is visible in cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:59-215, where AxonBackend stores beads/events as local files and serializes them with writeAtomicFile instead of remote operations.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:59-215 implements the backend as file-backed emulation, not a GraphQL-backed store. This bypasses the typed client, bypasses optimistic concurrency against Axon, and makes the backend selectable only in a fake local mode.

PROPOSED FIX
- Replace local snapshot writes with real GraphQL CRUD, dependency, and event mutations using cli/internal/bead/axon/.
- Preserve the two-collection model from TD-030: ddx_beads and ddx_bead_events, with event_of links.
- Keep the existing backend interface surface working for Create, Get, Update, Claim, Close, DepAdd, DepRemove, AppendEvent, ExportTo, and Import.
- Preserve jsonl as the default backend path so day-1 behavior does not change.

NON-SCOPE
- Backend selection config plumbing.
- Package test matrix broadening.
- WebSocket subscription smoke tests.","events_attachment":"ddx-9c5bca8f/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-9c5bca8f","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:beads","area:storage","axon","decomposed","from-upstream:axon-05c1019d","from-upstream:axon-82b6f7b2","kind:feature","phase:2","reconciled:no-changes-state"],"notes":"REFERENCE UPDATE 2026-05-11: design + integration plan consolidated into TD-027 (docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-027-bead-collection-abstraction.md). After the gating bead `ddx-c6317784` lands the new `Backend` sub-interfaces, `Operation` pattern, and helper packages, the codebase still depends on `*bead.Store` concretely in ~60+ files across `cli/cmd/`, `cli/internal/agent/`, `cli/internal/server/`, `cli/internal/escalation/`, `cli/internal/agentmetrics/`, `cli/internal/attemptmetrics/`, `cli/internal/processmetrics/`, `cli/internal/exec/`, `cli/internal/agent/try/`. The new interfaces are decorative until callers migrate to them.

Without caller migration, the Stage 3 boundary lockdown (moving `*Store` into `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/`) is impossible — any caller still holding `*Store` would fail to compile, and the gating bead's "physically impossible bypass" goal (TD-027 §21) is unrealized.

# ROOT CAUSE (file:line)
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` is currently public and exported. Every caller importing `cli/internal/bead/` can hold it concretely.
- ~60 files identified via `grep -rln "internal/bead\"" cli/ | grep -v _test`. Each caller file uses some subset of `*Store`'s 69 public methods directly rather than going through the `Backend` interface, sub-interfaces, or helper packages.
- The `concrete-store-methods` lint rule documented in TD-027 §21 has an initial allowlist of every existing caller file as the grandfathered set.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement Stage 2 of the three-stage path defined in TD-027 §21:

1. **Manual review per caller file** (~60 files). For each:
   1. Enumerate every method called on the `*Store` value (grep `<varname>\.` for the local variable name).
   2. For each method, identify the substitute:
      - Method exists on a `Backend` sub-interface → replace `*bead.Store` field type with the narrowest sub-interface (e.g. `bead.BeadReader`, `bead.BeadQueries`).
      - Method has a typed `Operation` covering it → call `store.Apply(ctx, id, op)` instead of the convenience method.
      - Method has a helper in `cli/internal/bead/ops/<concern>/` → use the helper.
      - Method has none of the above → either (a) add to the interface in this bead, (b) add as a new `Operation`, (c) add as a new helper, or (d) document as a legitimate exception (file-level repair, git ops, conflict resolution).
   3. Refactor the caller to use the chosen substitute. Update tests.

2. **Documented exceptions** (callers that stay concrete on `*Store` or do direct file I/O):
   - `cli/cmd/bead_doctor.go` — file-level repair of corrupted `beads.jsonl`. Operates on the file as data, not as bead-as-entity. Allowlisted.
   - `cli/cmd/bead.go:1550-1586` — git conflict resolution. File-level merge resolution. Allowlisted.
   - `cli/cmd/bead.go:80-127` — git auto-commit knows the file path. Allowlisted.
   - `cli/cmd/sync.go` — `ddx sync` synchronizes the file across machines. Allowlisted.

3. **Lint enforcement**: per TD-027 §21, `cli/tools/lints/concrete-store-methods` flags new files holding `*bead.Store` as a struct field. After this bead lands, the allowlist shrinks dramatically — only the documented exceptions remain. Implementation of the lint suite itself is `ddx-e91a45c0` (parallel work).

4. **Test parameterization**: tests that assert backend-portable behavior move into the parameterized conformance suite in `cli/internal/bead/`. Tests asserting JSONL-internal implementation details stay using `*Store` concretely (acceptable; backend-internal tests).

# NON-SCOPE
- Stage 3 boundary lockdown (moving `*Store` to `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/`) — separate bead.
- AxonStore implementation — separate bead `ddx-9c5bca8f`.
- Changes to `*Store`'s public surface — backwards compat is preserved.
- Changes to test fixtures that use `t.TempDir() + NewStore` for backend-internal tests.

# Acceptance criteria

1. Every file under `cli/cmd/`, `cli/internal/agent/`, `cli/internal/server/`, `cli/internal/escalation/`, `cli/internal/agentmetrics/`, `cli/internal/attemptmetrics/`, `cli/internal/processmetrics/`, `cli/internal/exec/`, `cli/internal/agent/try/` that imports `cli/internal/bead/` is reviewed. Reviewed-file list documented in a checklist (or via git-diff coverage) in the bead's evidence directory.
2. No file in those packages holds `*bead.Store` as a struct field OR local variable type, except for the documented allowlist entries (above).
3. Caller files instead hold `bead.Backend` or a narrower sub-interface (`bead.BeadReader`, `bead.BeadQueries`, `bead.BeadLifecycle + BeadEventWriter`, etc.). Test `TestCallerMigration_NoConcreteStore` greps the migrated packages for `\*bead\.Store` and asserts zero matches outside the allowlist.
4. `cli/tools/lints/concrete-store-methods` (per `ddx-e91a45c0`) allowlist is reduced to ONLY the documented exceptions; every other file previously grandfathered is removed.
5. New `Operation` types or helpers added to `cli/internal/bead/` to cover migration gaps are listed in the bead's notes with rationale.
6. Existing tests across the migrated packages continue to pass.
7. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

Test functions: `TestCallerMigration_NoConcreteStore`, `TestCallerMigration_AllowlistMatchesDocumentedExceptions`.

ID:       ddx-74452926
Title:    BL (Boundary Lockdown): caller narrowing + relocate concretes to internal/storage + Backend composite + compile-time enforcement (TD-027 §21)
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 1
Labels:   area:bead, area:storage, kind:refactor, phase:iterate, reliability, spec:TD-027
Parent:   ddx-8d747049

Desc:     # PROBLEM
After Phase 1 (Foundation `ddx-c6317784`) and per-concern beads (L/C/X/Q/D/A/MIG) land, callers no longer use the removed workflow methods on `*Store` — but they still hold `*Store` as a struct field type for foundational reads/writes/queries/etc. The `Backend` interface composite isn't yet asserted on `*Store`. The concrete types (`*Store`, `RawBackend`, `JSONLBackend`, `ExternalBackend`, `AxonStore`, `*WatcherHub`) live at `cli/internal/bead/` — publicly importable from any other package.

The goal of TD-027 §21 is **physical impossibility** of bypass: not lint-prevented, not review-prevented, but a Go compiler error if any code outside `cli/internal/bead/` tries to import the concrete storage implementations. This bead enforces that via Go's `internal/` rule.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` public.
- `cli/internal/bead/backend_jsonl.go` — `JSONLBackend` public.
- `cli/internal/bead/backend_external.go` — `ExternalBackend` public.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go` (or `AxonStore` post `ddx-9c5bca8f`) — public.
- `cli/internal/bead/watcher.go` — `*WatcherHub` public.
- All supporting concrete implementation files (`claim_liveness.go`, `attachments.go`, `archive.go`, `migrate.go`, etc.) — public.

Plus the final assembly step (formerly "K" in earlier breakdowns): `Backend` composite interface declared, `ReadOnlyBackend` convenience composite, compile-time assertion `var _ Backend = (*Store)(nil)`. These were deferred from Foundation because the composite couldn't be asserted until all per-concern beads removed their methods.

# PROPOSED FIX

This bead does three load-bearing things in one atomic change:

## 1. Narrow caller field types from `*bead.Store` to `bead.Backend` (or narrower)

Every caller file in `cli/cmd/`, `cli/internal/agent/`, `cli/internal/server/`, `cli/internal/escalation/`, `cli/internal/agentmetrics/`, `cli/internal/attemptmetrics/`, `cli/internal/processmetrics/`, `cli/internal/exec/`, `cli/internal/agent/try/` that holds `*bead.Store` as a struct field or local variable is updated:

- Most callers: change `*bead.Store` to `bead.Backend` (full interface). Mechanical rename.
- Narrow candidates (identified during per-concern beads): use narrower sub-interface where appropriate (e.g., `cli/cmd/work_focus.go` → `bead.BeadQueries`; `cli/internal/agentmetrics/loader.go` → `bead.BeadReader`).
- Documented exceptions remain on concrete `*Store` access OR on direct file I/O (allowlisted):
  - `cli/cmd/bead_doctor.go` — file-level repair; reads `.ddx/beads.jsonl` directly.
  - `cli/cmd/bead.go` (git auto-commit + conflict-resolution code paths only) — operates on the file as a file.
  - `cli/cmd/sync.go` — `ddx sync` operates on the file.

## 2. Add `Backend` composite + compile-time assertion

- Declare `Backend` as the union of all 11 sub-interfaces:
  `BeadInitializer + BeadReader + BeadLifecycle + BeadEventReader + BeadEventWriter + BeadQueries + BeadDependencyReader + BeadDependencyWriter + BeadArchive + BeadInterchangeReader + BeadInterchangeWriter`.
- Declare `ReadOnlyBackend` convenience composite (5 reader sub-interfaces) per TD-027 §6.1.
- Add compile-time assertion: `var _ Backend = (*Store)(nil)`.

## 3. Move concretes to internal/

- Create `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/` and `cli/internal/bead/internal/lifecycle/`.
- Move (with package rename):
  - `store.go` → `internal/storage/store.go` (package `storage`)
  - `backend_jsonl.go` → `internal/storage/backend_jsonl.go`
  - `backend_external.go` → `internal/storage/backend_external.go`
  - `axon_backend.go` / `axon_store.go` → `internal/storage/`
  - `claim_liveness.go` → `internal/storage/`
  - `attachments.go`, `archive.go`, `migrate.go`, etc. → `internal/storage/`
  - `watcher.go` → `internal/lifecycle/watcher_hub.go` (package `lifecycle`)
  - `RawBackend` and `OperationApplier` interfaces also move into `internal/storage/`
- Create `cli/internal/bead/factory.go` as the SOLE public construction path:
  ```go
  package bead
  import "cli/internal/bead/internal/storage"
  func NewStore(opts StoreOptions) (Backend, error) { return storage.New(opts) }
  ```
- The public `cli/internal/bead/` package surface is now: `Bead` struct, `Backend` + sub-interfaces, `Operation` + named ops, errors, `IDGenerator` + `ValidateID`, ctx accessors, `LifecycleSubscriber` interface, `Migrator` factory (from MIG bead), and the `ops/` helper sub-packages.

## 4. Lint cleanup

After lockdown, two analyzers in the lint suite (`ddx-e91a45c0`) are redundant because Go's `internal/` rule enforces the same property at the compiler level:
- `concrete-store-methods` — removed.
- `no-internal-store-construction` — removed.

Retained analyzers (defense-in-depth inside `cli/internal/bead/`):
- `direct-bead-jsonl-io` (callers still shouldn't bypass the abstraction)
- `no-new-rawbackend-impls` (new backends use Backend directly)
- `typed-context-accessors-only` (ctx.Value discipline)

# NON-SCOPE
- Per-concern bead work — must complete first.
- AxonStore implementation — `ddx-9c5bca8f`.
- IMP (JSONL→Postgres importer) — separate downstream bead.
- WIRE (real-wire integration tests) — separate downstream bead.

# Acceptance criteria

1. Every caller file in scope (per PROPOSED FIX §1) has its `*bead.Store` field/variable types narrowed to `bead.Backend` or a sub-interface, with the allowlist exceptions (`bead_doctor.go`, file-level paths in `bead.go`, `sync.go`).
2. `cli/internal/bead/backend.go` declares `Backend` as the union of 11 sub-interfaces. `ReadOnlyBackend` declared per TD-027 §6.1.
3. `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/` and `cli/internal/bead/internal/lifecycle/` exist; all concrete implementation files relocated with appropriate package renames.
4. `cli/internal/bead/factory.go` is the sole public construction path. Exports `NewStore`, `NewLifecycleSubscriber`, `NewMigrator` (from MIG). No other public function constructs a concrete storage type.
5. **Build must succeed**: `cd cli && go build ./...`. **Failure indicates incomplete per-concern bead migration** — diagnose the failing caller and complete its migration before retrying. Do NOT bypass via build tags or temporary public re-exports.
6. Boundary verification test: `TestModuleBoundary_NoInternalImportsOutsideBead` — static analysis scans every Go file under `cli/` outside `cli/internal/bead/` and asserts none of them import `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage` or `cli/internal/bead/internal/lifecycle`. AST-based.
7. Compile-time `var _ Backend = (*storage.Store)(nil)` exists inside `internal/storage/` (verifying Backend satisfaction now that the concrete type is private to that package).
8. Lint cleanup: `concrete-store-methods` and `no-internal-store-construction` analyzers removed from the bead-lints binary. Documented in `cli/tools/lints/README.md`. The retained 3 analyzers stay.
9. Existing tests pass without modification. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
10. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

The contract becomes: callers see only the public `Backend` interface (and sub-interfaces, Operations, sentinels, IDGenerator, factory); they cannot reach the concrete type.

.ddx/beads.jsonl:77:{"created_at":"2026-05-11T18:42:56.885339633Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-74452926","depends_on_id":"ddx-3af1c1a6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-11T20:00:39Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-74452926","depends_on_id":"ddx-71d3a2de","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-11T20:00:39Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-74452926","depends_on_id":"ddx-0ab16765","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-11T20:00:39Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-74452926","depends_on_id":"ddx-9c5bca8f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-11T20:00:40Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-74452926","depends_on_id":"ddx-29f02cf4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-11T20:00:40Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-74452926","depends_on_id":"ddx-900a8d38","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-11T20:00:40Z"}],"description":"# PROBLEM
After Phase 1 (Foundation `ddx-c6317784`) and per-concern beads (L/C/X/Q/D/A/MIG) land, callers no longer use the removed workflow methods on `*Store` — but they still hold `*Store` as a struct field type for foundational reads/writes/queries/etc. The `Backend` interface composite isn't yet asserted on `*Store`. The concrete types (`*Store`, `RawBackend`, `JSONLBackend`, `ExternalBackend`, `AxonStore`, `*WatcherHub`) live at `cli/internal/bead/` — publicly importable from any other package.

The goal of TD-027 §21 is **physical impossibility** of bypass: not lint-prevented, not review-prevented, but a Go compiler error if any code outside `cli/internal/bead/` tries to import the concrete storage implementations. This bead enforces that via Go's `internal/` rule.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` public.
- `cli/internal/bead/backend_jsonl.go` — `JSONLBackend` public.
- `cli/internal/bead/backend_external.go` — `ExternalBackend` public.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go` (or `AxonStore` post `ddx-9c5bca8f`) — public.
- `cli/internal/bead/watcher.go` — `*WatcherHub` public.
- All supporting concrete implementation files (`claim_liveness.go`, `attachments.go`, `archive.go`, `migrate.go`, etc.) — public.

Plus the final assembly step (formerly "K" in earlier breakdowns): `Backend` composite interface declared, `ReadOnlyBackend` convenience composite, compile-time assertion `var _ Backend = (*Store)(nil)`. These were deferred from Foundation because the composite couldn't be asserted until all per-concern beads removed their methods.

# PROPOSED FIX

This bead does three load-bearing things in one atomic change:

## 1. Narrow caller field types from `*bead.Store` to `bead.Backend` (or narrower)

Every caller file in `cli/cmd/`, `cli/internal/agent/`, `cli/internal/server/`, `cli/internal/escalation/`, `cli/internal/agentmetrics/`, `cli/internal/attemptmetrics/`, `cli/internal/processmetrics/`, `cli/internal/exec/`, `cli/internal/agent/try/` that holds `*bead.Store` as a struct field or local variable is updated:

- Most callers: change `*bead.Store` to `bead.Backend` (full interface). Mechanical rename.
- Narrow candidates (identified during per-concern beads): use narrower sub-interface where appropriate (e.g., `cli/cmd/work_focus.go` → `bead.BeadQueries`; `cli/internal/agentmetrics/loader.go` → `bead.BeadReader`).
- Documented exceptions remain on concrete `*Store` access OR on direct file I/O (allowlisted):
  - `cli/cmd/bead_doctor.go` — file-level repair; reads `.ddx/beads.jsonl` directly.
  - `cli/cmd/bead.go` (git auto-commit + conflict-resolution code paths only) — operates on the file as a file.
  - `cli/cmd/sync.go` — `ddx sync` operates on the file.

## 2. Add `Backend` composite + compile-time assertion

- Declare `Backend` as the union of all 11 sub-interfaces:
  `BeadInitializer + BeadReader + BeadLifecycle + BeadEventReader + BeadEventWriter + BeadQueries + BeadDependencyReader + BeadDependencyWriter + BeadArchive + BeadInterchangeReader + BeadInterchangeWriter`.
- Declare `ReadOnlyBackend` convenience composite (5 reader sub-interfaces) per TD-027 §6.1.
- Add compile-time assertion: `var _ Backend = (*Store)(nil)`.

## 3. Move concretes to internal/

- Create `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/` and `cli/internal/bead/internal/lifecycle/`.
- Move (with package rename):
  - `store.go` → `internal/storage/store.go` (package `storage`)
  - `backend_jsonl.go` → `internal/storage/backend_jsonl.go`
  - `backend_external.go` → `internal/storage/backend_external.go`
  - `axon_backend.go` / `axon_store.go` → `internal/storage/`
  - `claim_liveness.go` → `internal/storage/`
  - `attachments.go`, `archive.go`, `migrate.go`, etc. → `internal/storage/`
  - `watcher.go` → `internal/lifecycle/watcher_hub.go` (package `lifecycle`)
  - `RawBackend` and `OperationApplier` interfaces also move into `internal/storage/`
- Create `cli/internal/bead/factory.go` as the SOLE public construction path:
  ```go
  package bead
  import "cli/internal/bead/internal/storage"
  func NewStore(opts StoreOptions) (Backend, error) { return storage.New(opts) }
  ```
- The public `cli/internal/bead/` package surface is now: `Bead` struct, `Backend` + sub-interfaces, `Operation` + named ops, errors, `IDGenerator` + `ValidateID`, ctx accessors, `LifecycleSubscriber` interface, `Migrator` factory (from MIG bead), and the `ops/` helper sub-packages.

## 4. Lint cleanup

After lockdown, two analyzers in the lint suite (`ddx-e91a45c0`) are redundant because Go's `internal/` rule enforces the same property at the compiler level:
- `concrete-store-methods` — removed.
- `no-internal-store-construction` — removed.

Retained analyzers (defense-in-depth inside `cli/internal/bead/`):
- `direct-bead-jsonl-io` (callers still shouldn't bypass the abstraction)
- `no-new-rawbackend-impls` (new backends use Backend directly)
- `typed-context-accessors-only` (ctx.Value discipline)

# NON-SCOPE
- Per-concern bead work — must complete first.
- AxonStore implementation — `ddx-9c5bca8f`.
- IMP (JSONL→Postgres importer) — separate downstream bead.
- WIRE (real-wire integration tests) — separate downstream bead.

# Acceptance criteria

1. Every caller file in scope (per PROPOSED FIX §1) has its `*bead.Store` field/variable types narrowed to `bead.Backend` or a sub-interface, with the allowlist exceptions (`bead_doctor.go`, file-level paths in `bead.go`, `sync.go`).
2. `cli/internal/bead/backend.go` declares `Backend` as the union of 11 sub-interfaces. `ReadOnlyBackend` declared per TD-027 §6.1.
3. `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/` and `cli/internal/bead/internal/lifecycle/` exist; all concrete implementation files relocated with appropriate package renames.
4. `cli/internal/bead/factory.go` is the sole public construction path. Exports `NewStore`, `NewLifecycleSubscriber`, `NewMigrator` (from MIG). No other public function constructs a concrete storage type.
5. **Build must succeed**: `cd cli && go build ./...`. **Failure indicates incomplete per-concern bead migration** — diagnose the failing caller and complete its migration before retrying. Do NOT bypass via build tags or temporary public re-exports.
6. Boundary verification test: `TestModuleBoundary_NoInternalImportsOutsideBead` — static analysis scans every Go file under `cli/` outside `cli/internal/bead/` and asserts none of them import `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage` or `cli/internal/bead/internal/lifecycle`. AST-based.
7. Compile-time `var _ Backend = (*storage.Store)(nil)` exists inside `internal/storage/` (verifying Backend satisfaction now that the concrete type is private to that package).
8. Lint cleanup: `concrete-store-methods` and `no-internal-store-construction` analyzers removed from the bead-lints binary. Documented in `cli/tools/lints/README.md`. The retained 3 analyzers stay.
9. Existing tests pass without modification. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
10. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
